project_goal.value: React Native mobile app replacing a legacy Flash-era VOD platform for a regional cable operator, enabling subscribers to browse, purchase, and stream their content library on iOS and Android
project_goal.rationale: Core modernization mandate from board to retire Flash dependency before EOL deadline

target_users.value: Cable TV subscribers (iOS and Android), ranging from casual viewers to power users managing multiple household devices
target_users.rationale: Existing subscriber base migrating from web Flash player

project_class.rationale: Primary delivery channel is iOS and Android native via React Native

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, payments, analytics, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Streaming entitlement, in-app purchases, real-time playback telemetry, and analytics all required

complexity_level.rationale: DRM integration, cross-device entitlement sync, and real-time stream session management add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Handles personal subscriber data, payment flows, and DRM-protected licensed content

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores subscriber PII, watch history, and purchase records subject to GDPR and CCPA

external_exposure.rationale: Mobile app accessible to any subscriber over public internet

access_model.value: Subscriber accounts with Cognito, device registration limit of 5 per account, entitlement checked per-stream
access_model.rationale: Cable operator licensing terms restrict concurrent device access

feature_scope.value: Content browsing, channel bundle management, VOD purchase and rental, live stream playback, device management, watch history, wishlists, push notifications for content expiry
feature_scope.rationale: Full replacement of Flash player feature set plus mobile-native enhancements

mvp_scope.value: Subscriber auth with device registration, content catalog browse, VOD playback with Widevine/FairPlay DRM, in-app purchase for single titles, watch history sync
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for Q4 Flash retirement deadline

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API traffic, Widevine L1/L3 and FairPlay DRM, JWT tokens with 15-min expiry, certificate pinning in mobile app
security_baseline.rationale: DRM compliance and subscriber data protection requirements

frontend_stack.value: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript, Expo bare workflow
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform iOS and Android with native DRM module access

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Fastify, JWT auth middleware, Bull for background jobs
backend_stack.rationale: High-throughput entitlement checks require low-latency API responses

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 (subscribers, entitlements, purchases), Redis 7 (session cache, stream tokens)
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for entitlements, Redis for sub-100ms token validation

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with ALB, CloudFront for CDN-delivered content, S3 for packaged video segments
hosting_target.rationale: Operator already on AWS; ECS Fargate removes cluster management overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Watch history retained 12 months then purged; purchase records retained 7 years for financial compliance; EU subscriber data stored in eu-west-1
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimization and CCPA deletion rights obligations

future_scope.value: Live sports multi-view, offline download, family profile management, third-party channel add-ons
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap post-Flash migration completion

constraints.value: Must pass App Store and Google Play DRM certification; video segments must be served via CloudFront with signed URLs expiring in 60 seconds; no third-party player SDKs that introduce GPL obligations
constraints.rationale: App store requirements and content licensor obligations

observability_baseline.value: Datadog RUM for mobile crash/ANR tracking, custom playback telemetry (buffer ratio, start time, bitrate switches) shipped to Datadog Metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: QoE metrics are contractual with content licensors

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints, platform-split QA (iOS and Android), staged App Store rollout via TestFlight then production
execution_preference.rationale: Mobile release cadence with staged rollout to catch platform-specific regressions

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU subscribers (Article 17 deletion, consent for analytics), CCPA opt-out, Widevine and FairPlay DRM compliance, content licensor audit rights
compliance_context.rationale: Personal data handling and DRM obligations trigger compliance requirements

project.goal: Replace Flash VOD platform with React Native mobile app preserving all subscriber entitlements and content library
project.target_users: Regional cable TV subscribers on iOS and Android
project.timeline: Q4 Flash retirement deadline, MVP in 14 weeks

frontend.framework: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript, Expo bare workflow
frontend.state: Zustand for UI state, React Query for catalog/entitlement data

backend.framework: Fastify with JWT middleware and OpenAPI 3.0
backend.jobs: Bull queue on Redis for content expiry notifications and entitlement sync

security.auth: AWS Cognito with device registration tokens
security.drm: Widevine L1/L3 (Android), FairPlay (iOS) via license proxy service
security.transport: TLS 1.3, CloudFront signed URLs with 60s TTL

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for subscribers, entitlements, purchases, device_registrations
data.cache: Redis 7 for stream tokens and session data

devops.ci: GitHub Actions building iOS/Android binaries, ECS service deploy
devops.environments: dev, staging, production with feature flags via LaunchDarkly

constraints.drm_cert: Must pass Widevine and FairPlay certification

open_questions.download_offline: Offline download DRM feasibility deferred to Phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The Flash replacement deadline creates firm delivery pressure. The MVP scope is appropriately scoped to browse, purchase, and DRM playback. Watch history sync across devices is essential for subscriber retention and must not be cut from MVP.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The DRM license proxy is the highest-complexity component and should be architecturally isolated within the Fastify service. Widevine L1 requires hardware-backed key storage on Android; the app must detect and fall back to L3 gracefully. Signed URL generation at stream start is correct; ensure the CloudFront key pair rotation does not invalidate active sessions.
reasoner_reviews.security: Device registration must enforce the 5-device limit atomically to prevent race conditions allowing 6+ concurrent registrations. Stream tokens in Redis must include device fingerprint so a stolen token cannot be replayed from a different device. GDPR Article 17 deletion must cascade through purchase_records to anonymize rather than delete to resolve the financial retention conflict via pseudonymization.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Dual-region deployment (us-east-1, eu-west-1) is necessary for GDPR data residency but doubles infrastructure cost. Routing EU subscriber traffic via CloudFront geo-restriction to eu-west-1 ALB keeps EU PII in region.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan lacked DRM license proxy architecture detail and did not address the Cognito device registration race condition. These are the two highest-risk gaps for MVP delivery.

frontend.app_structure: React Native screens: ContentCatalog, TitleDetail, PlayerScreen, PurchaseFlow, MyLibrary, DeviceManager, ProfileSettings, WatchHistory
frontend.pages_and_flows: Subscriber logs in via Cognito, selects title, entitlement check shows purchase/rent/play CTA, DRM license acquired in PlayerScreen before first frame, WatchHistory position synced on pause/exit
frontend.state_management: Zustand stores: authStore (tokens, device_id), playerStore (playback position, quality level). React Query handles catalog pagination and entitlement status with 30s stale-while-revalidate
frontend.performance_notes: React Native Reanimated for smooth scroll in catalog grid; FastImage for lazy loading; player pre-rolls DRM license 3s before user hits play to hide license latency
frontend.frontend_risks: Widevine L1 hardware attestation failures on older Android devices require graceful L3 fallback with content licensor pre-approval

backend.service_design: Fastify monolith with domain modules: catalog, entitlements, purchases, stream-tokens, drm-proxy, notifications. DRM proxy module issues Widevine and FairPlay license challenges to external KMS
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0: POST /auth/device-register, GET /catalog/titles, GET /entitlements/:subscriber_id, POST /stream-tokens, POST /drm/widevine-license, POST /drm/fairplay-license, POST /purchases
backend.failure_handling: Bull job retry with exponential backoff for content expiry notifications; circuit breaker on DRM KMS calls with 5s timeout before returning cached license; idempotency keys on purchase endpoints
backend.background_jobs: Nightly content license expiry job: query content_licenses expiring in 7 days, remove from wishlists, queue push notifications to affected subscribers via SNS

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with SRP auth; device registration issues a device_token JWT (signed, device-bound); stream tokens are short-lived (60s) Redis keys containing subscriber_id + device_id + title_id hash
security.authorization_model: Subscriber role: browse + stream entitled content only. Admin role: catalog management. Device limit enforced via PostgreSQL advisory lock during registration to prevent race conditions
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limit on POST /stream-tokens (10/min per device); CloudFront WAF blocking IP ranges with >500 stream token requests/min; signed URL non-reuse enforced via Redis SET NX

data.entities: subscribers (id, cognito_sub, email_hash, region ENUM us/eu, gdpr_consent_at, ccpa_opt_out, created_at), content_licenses (id, title_id, license_type ENUM svod/tvod/live, expiry_date, drm_key_id), device_registrations (id, subscriber_id, device_fingerprint, platform ENUM ios/android, registered_at, last_seen_at), purchase_records (id, subscriber_id, title_id, purchase_type ENUM buy/rent, store ENUM apple/google/direct, amount_cents, purchased_at), stream_sessions (id, subscriber_id, device_id, title_id, started_at, ended_at, bytes_delivered, avg_bitrate_kbps), watch_history (id, subscriber_id, title_id, position_seconds, updated_at)
data.retention_and_deletion: GDPR Article 17: subscriber PII anonymized (email_hash nulled, name cleared) on deletion request; purchase_records pseudonymized (subscriber_id replaced with deleted_subscriber_uuid); watch_history purged after 12 months; EU data in eu-west-1 PostgreSQL instance

devops.deployment_topology: Two ECS Fargate clusters (us-east-1, eu-west-1) behind regional ALBs; CloudFront with geo-routing origin groups directing EU requests to eu-west-1 ALB; RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ per region; ElastiCache Redis cluster per region
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint + unit tests, jest integration tests, Docker build + push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy with 50% minimum healthy. Mobile: Expo EAS Build for iOS (TestFlight) and Android (internal track) on each main merge
devops.environments: dev (single-AZ, synthetic data), staging (multi-AZ, anonymized subscriber data), production (multi-AZ dual-region, staged rollout 5/25/100%)

ISS-001.title: DRM license proxy architecture not specified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan referenced DRM but did not define the license proxy service design, KMS integration, or how Widevine and FairPlay challenges are routed. Content licensor audit requires documented DRM architecture.

history.note: Identified in architect_reasoner review as highest-risk gap

ISS-002.title: Device registration race condition unaddressed
ISS-002.detail: POST /auth/device-register did not specify atomicity mechanism. Concurrent registrations from multiple devices can exceed the 5-device limit.

history.note: Identified in security review

ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan specified hard delete for subscriber records without addressing the conflict between GDPR right-to-erasure and 7-year financial record retention for purchase_records.

history.note: Raised in compliance review

focus_issues.title: Device registration race condition unaddressed
focus_issues.detail: POST /auth/device-register did not specify atomicity mechanism for the 5-device limit.
