previous_plan.title: Short-Form Video Microservices - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Flutter 3.22 app with Go microservices on GKE Autopilot replacing a monolith for a Gen Z short-form video platform. Services: feed-ranking, upload-processor, social-graph, engagement-collector, content-moderation, admin-api.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Go gRPC microservices on GKE Autopilot. feed-ranking calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 for embedding similarity during GetFeed. upload-processor publishes to Pub/Sub on completion. Cassandra for engagement events, PostgreSQL with pgvector for creator profiles and content embeddings.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth with coppa_minor custom claim. GDPR deletion coordinator. DSA takedown via admin-api. CDN purge path absent. COPPA engagement-collector check absent.
previous_plan.data_model: creator_profiles, video_posts (pgvector content_embedding), feed_rankings (Cassandra), engagement_signals (Cassandra), social_edges, moderation_queues defined.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (LLM in request path) and ISS-002 (CDN purge absent). For ISS-001: embeddings are generated asynchronously by a Pub/Sub-triggered job at upload-complete time and stored in PostgreSQL pgvector; feed-ranking re-scores candidates every 30 seconds using pre-computed embeddings cached in Redis, never during a live GetFeed request. For ISS-002: AdminService.TakedownVideo handler calls Cloud CDN API synchronously within the gRPC handler before returning, logs cdn_purge_job_id to the moderation audit trail for DSA evidence. ISS-003 COPPA: engagement-collector gRPC interceptor reads coppa_minor claim and drops without persisting.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved LLM embedding computation from GetFeed request path to a Pub/Sub-triggered background job: on video_post.status=transcoded event, embedding-generator job calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding (pgvector). Feed-ranking service re-scores top-K candidates every 30 seconds via a scheduled worker reading pgvector cosine similarity from PostgreSQL and writing ranked feed to Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id}. GetFeed handler reads only from Redis cache (<5ms), never from Vertex AI
fix_report.expected_outcome: GetFeed p99 latency drops from 200-400ms to <10ms for cached feed reads; LLM embedding freshness lag of up to 30 seconds is acceptable for feed ranking quality

fix_report.action_taken: Added synchronous Cloud CDN cache invalidation to AdminService.TakedownVideo gRPC handler: after updating video_post.status=taken_down in PostgreSQL, handler calls GCP Cloud CDN API cdn.CacheInvalidation.Create with pattern /videos/{video_id}/* before returning gRPC response; cdn_purge_job_id logged to moderation_audit_events table with timestamp; Pub/Sub event emitted post-purge for notification service to alert affected creator
fix_report.expected_outcome: CDN cache invalidation initiated within 1-2 seconds of moderator action; propagation completes in 10-30 seconds on Cloud CDN; DSA 60-second SLA met with buffer; cdn_purge_job_id provides audit evidence for DSA transparency reports

target_output.title: Gen Z Short-Form Video Platform - Microservices Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A COPPA/GDPR/DSA-compliant Flutter 3.22 mobile app backed by Go 1.22 gRPC microservices on GKE Autopilot, decomposed from a scaling-bottlenecked monolith. The feed-ranking service delivers personalized vertical video feeds at p99 <100ms by reading pre-scored Redis caches refreshed every 30 seconds using Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 content embeddings computed asynchronously at upload time. Content moderation combines hash fingerprinting and LLM scoring; moderator takedowns synchronously invoke Cloud CDN cache invalidation within the gRPC handler for DSA 60-second propagation compliance. COPPA minor users have engagement signal collection blocked at the gRPC interceptor layer before any Cassandra write.
target_output.architecture_overview: Flutter client connects via gRPC-Dart to a GKE Ingress routing to seven Go microservices: feed-ranking, upload-processor, social-graph, engagement-collector, content-moderation, admin-api, and notification. Feed delivery critical path: GetFeed reads Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} (pre-scored every 30 seconds by a background feed-scorer worker) for sub-10ms response, bypassing Vertex AI entirely at request time. LLM embedding pipeline: upload-processor publishes upload_complete to Pub/Sub; embedding-generator service calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding (PostgreSQL pgvector); feed-scorer worker runs cosine similarity queries against cached viewer interest embeddings every 30 seconds and writes results to Redis. CDN takedown path: TakedownVideo gRPC handler updates PostgreSQL video_post status, calls Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation API synchronously, logs cdn_purge_job_id to audit trail. Dual-region GKE (us-central1, europe-west1) with Cloud CDN fronting Cloud Storage video segments globally.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22, Dart, Riverpod, gRPC-Dart, video_player package. Backend: Go 1.22, gRPC with protobuf, GCP Pub/Sub for async events, Firebase Admin SDK for JWT validation. Data: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension (creator_profiles, video_posts, social_edges), Cassandra 4.1 (engagement_signals, feed_rankings), Redis 7 (feed_cache, trending_topics). LLM: Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API (768-dim). Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot (us-central1, europe-west1), Cloud Build, Cloud CDN, Cloud Storage, Cloud Run for transcoding jobs. Observability: Datadog APM + RUM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Vertical Feed: Flutter PageView rendering HLS video posts pre-loaded from Redis feed cache, refreshed every 30s by LLM-assisted relevance scoring. Upload Pipeline: creator submits clip via gRPC UploadService, upload-processor stores raw video in Cloud Storage, triggers Cloud Run FFmpeg transcode job, publishes upload_complete event to Pub/Sub which triggers embedding generation. Social Graph: SocialService.Follow/Unfollow writes PostgreSQL social_edges; follower counts maintained with PostgreSQL counter tables. Engagement Collection: streaming gRPC EngagementService.RecordSignals batches like/share/watch_time events to Cassandra; COPPA interceptor drops events for coppa_minor users. Content Moderation: content-moderation service checks PhotoDNA hash + LLM moderation score at upload_complete, routes flagged content to moderation_queues. Moderator Admin Panel: Flutter admin tab (moderator role only) calls AdminService.TakedownVideo which purges CDN within 60 seconds. Trending Topics: Redis sorted set updated hourly by trending-scorer worker based on engagement velocity.
target_output.system_components: Flutter app (iOS/Android): Riverpod providers, gRPC-Dart stubs, video_player with HLS. GKE Ingress (gRPC HTTP/2 passthrough). Go microservices: feed-ranking (Redis read + 30s scorer worker), upload-processor (gRPC + Pub/Sub publisher + Cloud Run job launcher), social-graph (PostgreSQL), engagement-collector (Cassandra + coppa_minor interceptor), content-moderation (Pub/Sub consumer, hash + LLM scoring), admin-api (PostgreSQL + Cloud CDN API caller), notification (Pub/Sub consumer + FCM). embedding-generator service (Pub/Sub consumer, Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 API, pgvector write). feed-scorer worker (30s cron, PostgreSQL pgvector cosine similarity, Redis write). PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector. Cassandra 4.1. Redis 7. Cloud Storage (raw video + HLS segments). Cloud CDN. Datadog APM agents on all services.
target_output.workflows: Feed Delivery (Critical Path): Flutter calls FeedService.GetFeed via gRPC. feed-ranking handler reads Redis LIST feed_cache:{viewer_id} (pre-populated by feed-scorer worker). Returns top 20 video_post_ids with HLS manifest URLs signed for Cloud CDN. p99 < 10ms from Redis. Background Feed Scoring (every 30s): feed-scorer worker queries Cassandra engagement_signals for each active viewer (last 7 days), fetches top-500 candidate videos, runs pgvector cosine similarity query: SELECT video_id FROM video_posts ORDER BY content_embedding <=> $viewer_interest_embedding LIMIT 500, applies collaborative filter boost from Cassandra follow-graph signal, writes re-ranked list to Redis feed_cache:{viewer_id} with 60s TTL. Upload and Embedding Pipeline: Creator uploads clip via UploadService.StartUpload. upload-processor stores raw file in Cloud Storage, launches Cloud Run FFmpeg transcode job (HLS at 720p/1080p). On transcode complete, publishes {video_id, hls_manifest_url} to upload_complete Pub/Sub topic. embedding-generator consumes event, calls Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API with video thumbnail + caption text, stores 768-dim vector in video_posts.content_embedding via pgvector. content-moderation consumes same event, checks hash, runs LLM moderation score, routes to moderation_queues if score > 0.7. Moderator Takedown (DSA SLA): Moderator clicks takedown in Flutter admin panel. Flutter calls AdminService.TakedownVideo(video_id, reason). admin-api gRPC handler: UPDATE video_posts SET status='taken_down'; calls GCP Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation.Create API with url_map pattern /videos/{video_id}/*; receives cdn_purge_job_id; INSERTs moderation_audit_event row with cdn_purge_job_id and initiated_at timestamp; returns TakedownAck with cdn_purge_job_id to Flutter. Flutter shows confirmation with purge job ID. CDN propagation completes in 10-30s; total takedown propagation under 60s. Pub/Sub notification event emitted to notify creator.
target_output.data_model: creator_profiles (id UUID PK, firebase_uid VARCHAR, username VARCHAR UNIQUE, region VARCHAR, age_verified BOOL DEFAULT false, coppa_minor BOOL DEFAULT false, follower_count INT DEFAULT 0, following_count INT DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. video_posts (id UUID PK, creator_id UUID FK, caption TEXT, hls_manifest_url TEXT, thumbnail_url TEXT, duration_seconds FLOAT, status TEXT CHECK IN ('uploading','transcoding','active','taken_down'), content_embedding vector(768), moderation_score FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL with pgvector. social_edges (follower_id UUID, followee_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (follower_id, followee_id)) - PostgreSQL. engagement_signals (viewer_id UUID, video_id UUID, signal_type TEXT, watch_time_ms INT, occurred_at TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (viewer_id, occurred_at)) - Cassandra MV partition. feed_rankings (viewer_id UUID, bucket_key TEXT, ranked_video_ids LIST<UUID>, scored_at TIMESTAMP) - Cassandra. moderation_queues (id UUID PK, video_id UUID FK, queue_type TEXT, assigned_moderator_id UUID, status TEXT, cdn_purge_job_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP) - PostgreSQL. trending_topics stored in Redis ZSET trending:topics with score = engagement_velocity_score.
target_output.api_design: gRPC services (proto): FeedService.GetFeed(ViewerFeedRequest{viewer_id, page_token}) returns FeedResponse{video_posts[], next_page_token}. UploadService.StartUpload(UploadRequest{creator_id, duration_seconds, caption}) returns UploadSession{upload_url, session_id}. EngagementService.RecordSignals(stream EngagementEvent{viewer_id, video_id, signal_type, watch_time_ms}) returns Ack. SocialService.Follow(FollowRequest{follower_id, followee_id}) returns FollowAck. AdminService.TakedownVideo(TakedownRequest{video_id, moderator_id, reason}) returns TakedownAck{cdn_purge_job_id, initiated_at}. AdminService.GetModerationQueue(QueueRequest) returns ModerationQueueResponse. REST (admin panel DSA reporting): GET /admin/dsa-report?period=monthly (returns takedown counts and avg propagation times for DSA transparency report).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth custom claims: coppa_minor (set on DOB < 13 at registration), age_verified (parental consent received), moderator (granted by admin). gRPC interceptors on all services: JWT validation, and on engagement-collector: coppa_minor=true drops event without Cassandra write. COPPA: no behavioral signal collection for minors; watch-time not persisted; no ad targeting. GDPR: deletion-coordinator receives DELETE /users/:id, publishes user_delete_request to Pub/Sub; each service purges user rows within 72 hours; PostgreSQL social_edges and video_posts cascade on creator_profiles delete. DSA: TakedownVideo handler calls Cloud CDN CacheInvalidation synchronously; cdn_purge_job_id and timing logged to moderation_audit_events for quarterly DSA transparency report; GET /admin/dsa-report endpoint for regulatory submissions. Content hash fingerprinting: PhotoDNA hash checked at upload_complete before video becomes active.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Dual-region GKE Autopilot: us-central1 (global traffic default), europe-west1 (EU user affinity via Cloud Load Balancing geo-routing for GDPR residency). Each region deploys all Go microservices via Cloud Build. HPA on CPU + custom Datadog metrics: feed-ranking scales on feed_request_queue_depth, upload-processor scales on upload_queue_depth. Cloud Build pipeline per service: go test ./..., golangci-lint, ko build, kubectl apply with Workload Identity. Flutter CI: flutter test, flutter build appbundle --release, Firebase App Distribution for beta. Canary: Istio VirtualService 5% traffic split before full rollout. Rollback: kubectl rollout undo per service.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM gRPC interceptor on all Go services with distributed trace propagation via gRPC metadata. Key traces: GetFeed (Redis read latency), TakedownVideo (CDN purge latency), embedding-generator (Vertex AI call duration). Custom Datadog metrics: feed_cache_hit_rate (target >99%), embedding_generation_lag_seconds (p95 < 120s post-upload), cdn_purge_latency_seconds (p99 < 55s for DSA SLA), engagement_signal_drop_rate_coppa (count of COPPA-dropped signals for audit). PagerDuty alerts: feed_cache_hit_rate <95%, cdn_purge_p99 >55s, upload_failure_rate >3%. Datadog RUM in Flutter for crash rate and video player error rate.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales to zero for low-traffic services; estimated $2,400/month at 500K daily active users: GKE compute ($900), Cloud CDN egress for 5PB/month video ($1,100), PostgreSQL CloudSQL ($250), Cassandra (self-managed GKE StatefulSet, $150). Vertex AI Gemini Flash 1.5 embedding API: ~$0.0001 per 1K tokens, negligible for upload volume. Redis cluster ($120/month). Datadog ($200/month at this scale). Feed-scorer worker cost negligible (Go goroutine pool, <1 CPU continuous).
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (12 weeks): Decompose monolith feed-ranking and upload-processor services; Flutter gRPC client with Riverpod; Cassandra engagement_signals; Redis feed cache with 30s scorer; Vertex AI embedding pipeline (async); Cloud CDN with synchronous TakedownVideo purge; COPPA engagement interceptor; GDPR deletion coordinator; Datadog APM. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Social graph service with follower/following feed boost; trending topics Redis ZSET scorer; push notifications via FCM; content-moderation LLM scoring with PhotoDNA; DSA transparency report endpoint. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Ad placement service (excluding COPPA minors); creator analytics dashboard; live streaming architecture; AR face filters via TensorFlow Lite on-device.
target_output.development_guidelines: Proto-first API design: all service interfaces defined in .proto files in the shared proto repository before implementation. Go: golangci-lint, unit tests for all business logic (80% coverage), gRPC interceptor tests with mock JWT tokens including coppa_minor=true test cases. Flutter: flutter_test for widget tests, integration tests for feed load and upload flow. DSA audit trail: every TakedownVideo must include cdn_purge_job_id in response - tested in admin-api integration tests. Each service deployed independently; no cross-service database writes (services own their data stores). Datadog trace annotations on all gRPC handlers with feed_cache_hit span tag.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: 30-second feed re-scoring lag means newly uploaded viral content appears in feeds up to 30 seconds after embedding generation. Mitigation: hot-content detector publishes forced re-score event when video engagement velocity exceeds threshold. Risk: PostgreSQL pgvector cosine similarity query over 10M video_posts at 30s interval becoming a bottleneck. Mitigation: partition pgvector queries by content category tag; pre-filter candidates by creator follow graph before similarity scan. Tradeoff: synchronous CDN purge in TakedownVideo vs. async job - chosen synchronous to meet DSA 60s SLA guarantee; adds ~2s to moderator API response time which is acceptable. Risk: Cassandra fan-out read for feed-scorer on viral videos followed by millions: Mitigation: cap fan-out at top-500 followed creators per viewer; use Cassandra materialized view for pre-aggregated engagement counts.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: LLM embedding computed asynchronously at upload time, not at request time; GetFeed reads only Redis cache. CDN purge called synchronously in TakedownVideo gRPC handler with cdn_purge_job_id logged for DSA evidence. COPPA engagement blocking enforced via gRPC interceptor in engagement-collector service. pgvector in PostgreSQL chosen over Pinecone/Weaviate to reduce external service dependencies at current scale.

metadata.notes: Gen Z short-form video microservices decomposition; round 2 fixes LLM in request path (async embedding pre-computation) and CDN purge for DSA 60s takedown SLA.

project_goal.value: Cross-platform Electron desktop app for professional video editors to ingest, transcode, and organize raw footage before uploading to a cloud MAM system
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual FFmpeg scripting workflows with a structured GUI ingest pipeline

target_users.value: Professional video editors and post-production supervisors at broadcast and production companies
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery interviews across 3 production houses

project_class.rationale: Requires local GPU access for FFmpeg transcoding and direct filesystem access for camera card ingest

capabilities.rationale: Electron UI plus local Python FastAPI backend, SQLite persistence, S3 integration, and auto-update pipeline

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-process Electron architecture with FFmpeg subprocess management, local IPC, and cloud sync coordination

risk_level.rationale: Internal tooling with cloud credentials in play; SOC 2 audit scope requires chain-of-custody logging

data_sensitivity.rationale: Raw broadcast footage and XMP metadata are proprietary internal assets, not personal data

external_exposure.rationale: App authenticates to cloud MAM via SAML SSO; no unauthenticated endpoints

access_model.value: SAML SSO with per-user JWT for MAM API calls; local app requires OS login session
access_model.rationale: Enterprise SSO mandated by IT; local session tied to OS user for auditability

feature_scope.value: Camera card ingest with file hashing, FFmpeg-based proxy transcoding, XMP metadata editing, S3 upload with resume support, MAM webhook notification, auto-update
feature_scope.rationale: Full post-production ingest workflow from card reader to MAM

mvp_scope.value: Folder drop ingest, H.264 proxy generation, SQLite job tracking, S3 upload, and MAM webhook on completion
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot at two production houses in Q2

security_baseline.value: AWS credentials via IAM role-based short-lived tokens, TLS 1.3 for all cloud calls, SAML SSO, Electron contextIsolation enabled, no nodeIntegration in renderer
security_baseline.rationale: Electron security hardening and credential safety for SOC 2

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in React; Electron enables native filesystem and subprocess access

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI running as local sidecar process, communicating via localhost IPC
backend_stack.rationale: FFmpeg Python bindings and existing ML preprocessing scripts are in Python

data_platform.value: SQLite via SQLAlchemy for local job state; S3 for asset and proxy storage
data_platform.rationale: No network DB dependency for offline-capable desktop app; S3 for durable cloud storage

hosting_target.value: Electron packaged installers for macOS and Windows, distributed via S3 auto-update bucket
hosting_target.rationale: Enterprise distribution via signed installers; Squirrel.Mac and NSIS for update delivery

privacy_retention_policy.value: Local SQLite logs purged after 1 year; S3 raw footage retained per per-project archive policy set by MAM admin
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Broadcast archive chain-of-custody requirements vary by client contract

future_scope.value: AI-assisted scene tagging, LUT application during proxy generation, multi-user collaborative MAM review
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap post-pilot validation

constraints.value: Must run offline for ingest and transcoding; cloud calls only for upload and MAM sync; signed installers required by enterprise IT
constraints.rationale: Production sets often have unreliable connectivity; IT policy requires code signing

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting with source maps, structured local log files per session, FFmpeg job duration metrics logged to SQLite
observability_baseline.rationale: Desktop app cannot rely on always-on backend; local logs are primary observability

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with desktop beta releases every 4 weeks to pilot users
execution_preference.rationale: Rapid feedback loop with production house pilot partners

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II for cloud storage handling; chain-of-custody audit log for broadcast archive requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Cloud MAM vendor and broadcast clients require SOC 2 attestation

project.goal: Ingest, transcode, and organize raw camera footage for MAM upload
project.target_users: Professional video editors and post-production supervisors
project.timeline: Q2 pilot at two production houses

frontend.state: Zustand for ingest job state, React Query for MAM sync status
frontend.ipc: Electron contextBridge with typed IPC channels

backend.framework: FastAPI local sidecar
backend.ffmpeg: ffmpeg-python bindings for transcode subprocess management

security.auth: SAML SSO with short-lived JWT for MAM API, contextIsolation in Electron
security.credentials: AWS STS AssumeRole for S3 uploads via short-lived tokens
security.signing: Apple notarization and Windows Authenticode code signing

data.db: SQLite via SQLAlchemy for ingest_jobs, footage_clips, transcode_profiles
data.assets: S3 with multipart upload and resume support

devops.packaging: electron-builder for macOS DMG and Windows NSIS installer
devops.auto_update: Squirrel.Mac and electron-updater against S3 update bucket

constraints.offline: Full ingest and transcode offline; S3 upload only when network available

reasoner_reviews.product: The ingest and transcode workflow is well-scoped for MVP. Offline-first constraint correctly prioritizes local SQLite over network DB. The MAM webhook notification is a critical integration point that needs retry logic given unreliable set connectivity.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Multi-process Electron architecture with Python FastAPI sidecar is the right call given existing FFmpeg Python tooling. The contextBridge IPC pattern enforces security boundaries. Round 2 left the auto-update manifest versioning scheme underspecified — this must be addressed in round 3 to unblock the DevOps pipeline.
reasoner_reviews.security: Electron contextIsolation with no nodeIntegration correctly hardens the renderer. Short-lived STS tokens for S3 prevent long-lived credential exposure. The chain-of-custody log for SOC 2 needs a tamper-evident append-only design — round 2 used a mutable SQLite table which the auditor flagged.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Bundled static FFmpeg binaries per platform correctly removes runtime dependencies. The offline ingest requirement is met by local SQLite. Code signing pipeline with Xcode Cloud notarization and Windows Authenticode must be part of the GitHub Actions release workflow.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan had two unresolved issues: the chain-of-custody log was mutable (ISS-001) and the auto-update manifest lacked a version schema and signature verification (ISS-002). Both must be fully resolved in round 3 with concrete implementation details.

frontend.app_structure: Three Electron windows: MainWindow (ingest panel + job queue), PreviewWindow (proxy playback via media element), SettingsWindow (MAM endpoint config, codec presets). All renderer processes isolated via contextBridge.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Ingest Panel (drag-drop zone, file hash progress, job list), Transcode Queue (active FFmpeg jobs with progress bars and ETA), Upload Queue (S3 multipart progress per clip), MAM Sync Status (webhook delivery confirmations)
frontend.state_management: Zustand stores for ingest_jobs, transcode_jobs, upload_jobs. React Query for polling MAM sync status endpoint on FastAPI sidecar.
frontend.performance_notes: Proxy playback via HTML5 video element on localhost FastAPI file server to avoid loading large files into JS heap. Virtual list for job queues exceeding 500 items.

backend.service_design: FastAPI sidecar on localhost:18472 with lifespan startup that validates FFmpeg binary path and SQLite schema. Modules: ingest_router, transcode_router, upload_router, mam_router.
backend.api_patterns: REST on localhost; Electron preload calls via contextBridge IPC map to FastAPI endpoints. Long-running jobs return job IDs and expose GET /jobs/{id}/status for polling.
backend.background_jobs: FFmpeg transcode jobs run as Python subprocess.Popen managed by a thread pool of size=2 (configurable). S3 multipart upload via boto3 TransferManager with resume via UploadId tracking in SQLite.
backend.failure_handling: FFmpeg failures captured from stderr, written to ingest_jobs.error_log, status set to failed. S3 upload retries with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts. MAM webhook retries stored in upload_queues.retry_count with max 10 retries.

security.auth_design: SAML SSO redirect opens system browser; SAML assertion exchanged for app JWT stored in OS keychain (Keytar). JWT attached to MAM API calls. AWS STS AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity for S3 upload tokens, refreshed 15 minutes before expiry.
security.secrets_management: No credentials on disk; AWS tokens in memory only, refreshed via STS. SAML JWT in OS keychain via Keytar. SQLite DB not encrypted but contains no credentials.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: chain_of_custody_log table with append-only trigger enforced at SQLite level (INSERT-only, no UPDATE/DELETE via trigger). Logs: file_hash, ingest_timestamp, operator_id, s3_key, mam_ack_timestamp. Exported to JSON for SOC 2 audit delivery.

data.entities: ingest_jobs (id, folder_path, total_files, hashed_files, status ENUM queued/hashing/transcoding/uploading/complete/failed, created_at, completed_at), footage_clips (id, job_id, file_path, file_size_bytes, sha256_hash, duration_seconds, codec, s3_key, proxy_s3_key, mam_asset_id), transcode_profiles (id, name, codec ENUM h264/prores/hevc, resolution, bitrate_kbps, container), proxy_renditions (id, clip_id, profile_id, local_path, duration_seconds, transcode_duration_ms, status), upload_queues (id, clip_id, s3_key, upload_id, parts_completed, total_parts, retry_count, status), chain_of_custody_log (id, clip_id, operator_id, action ENUM ingested/transcoded/uploaded/mam_acked, file_hash, timestamp)
data.schema_notes: SQLite WAL mode for concurrent reads during active transcode. chain_of_custody_log enforced append-only via SQLite trigger blocking UPDATE and DELETE.
data.migration_notes: SQLAlchemy Alembic for SQLite schema migrations on app launch, versioned by app release.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions release workflow: build React renderer, bundle FastAPI sidecar via PyInstaller, package with electron-builder for macOS DMG (notarized) and Windows NSIS (Authenticode signed). Artifacts uploaded to S3 update bucket under /releases/{version}/
devops.ci_cd_design: PR CI: ESLint + mypy + pytest for FastAPI, React unit tests via Vitest. Release CI: version bump, electron-builder for both platforms in matrix, Apple notarization via notarytool, Windows signing via Azure Key Vault codesigning action, S3 publish, update-manifest.json bump with SHA256 checksums.
devops.rollback_strategy: Squirrel.Mac and electron-updater pin to previous version entry in update-manifest.json. Manual rollback by reverting manifest S3 object version.
devops.devops_risks: Notarization turnaround from Apple averaging 2-10 minutes can block release pipeline. Mitigation: async notarization job with webhook callback before manifest publish.

ISS-001.title: Chain-of-custody log is mutable — UPDATE and DELETE not prevented
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 plan used a plain SQLite table with no write restrictions. Auditor flagged that SOC 2 chain-of-custody log must be append-only; any operator or application code could modify historical records.

history.note: First identified in round 1 audit
Round 2 plan did not add SQLite trigger or WAL constraint

ISS-002.title: Auto-update manifest lacks version schema and signature verification
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 described auto-update delivery via S3 bucket but did not specify the update-manifest.json schema, how clients verify the integrity of downloaded installers, or how rollback is triggered programmatically.

history.note: First identified in round 1 audit
Still no manifest schema or checksum verification in round 2 plan

focus_issues.title: Chain-of-custody log is mutable — UPDATE and DELETE not prevented
focus_issues.detail: SOC 2 audit requires tamper-evident append-only log; current SQLite table permits modification of historical records.

focus_issues.title: Auto-update manifest lacks version schema and signature verification
focus_issues.detail: electron-updater cannot safely verify installer integrity without SHA256 checksums and a signed manifest.

latest_recommendations: Add SQLite INSERT-only trigger to chain_of_custody_log blocking UPDATE and DELETE at DB level
Define update-manifest.json schema with version, platform URLs, SHA256 checksums, and electron-updater signature field
Add SHA256 checksum verification step in CI before publishing manifest to S3

previous_plan.title: Professional Video Ingest Desktop App — Round 2 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Cross-platform Electron 31 desktop application for professional video ingest, proxy generation, and MAM upload. Python FastAPI sidecar manages FFmpeg transcoding and S3 uploads. SQLite tracks job state locally for offline operation.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Electron main process manages two renderer processes (ingest panel, settings) via contextBridge IPC. Python FastAPI sidecar on localhost:18472 handles FFmpeg subprocess management, S3 multipart upload, and MAM webhook delivery. SQLite in WAL mode persists all job state.
previous_plan.data_model: ingest_jobs, footage_clips, transcode_profiles, proxy_renditions, upload_queues, chain_of_custody_log (mutable plain table — flagged by auditor).
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions builds electron-builder artifacts for macOS and Windows. Auto-update via S3 bucket. Manifest schema and checksum verification not yet specified.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must resolve both high-severity issues: ISS-001 requires a SQLite INSERT-only trigger on chain_of_custody_log to enforce tamper-evident SOC 2 audit log, and ISS-002 requires a fully specified update-manifest.json schema matching electron-updater format with SHA256 checksums and a CI verification step before manifest publication. The Electron multi-process architecture with Python FastAPI sidecar is solid and carries forward unchanged. Offline-first design with STS token refresh and S3 multipart resume is retained.

fix_report.action_taken: Added SQLite trigger CREATE TRIGGER prevent_coc_mutation BEFORE UPDATE ON chain_of_custody_log BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT,'chain_of_custody_log is append-only'); END and a parallel BEFORE DELETE trigger. chain_of_custody_log is now INSERT-only at the database layer, enforced independently of application code. Documented in data_model and security_and_compliance sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SOC 2 auditor can verify chain-of-custody log integrity without relying on application-level access controls; any attempt to modify records raises a DB-level ABORT.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined update-manifest.json schema: {version, releaseDate, files:[{platform:'darwin'|'win32', url, sha256, size}], releaseNotes}. CI release workflow now computes SHA256 of each installer artifact, embeds in manifest, and signs the manifest JSON with the release GPG key. electron-updater verifies SHA256 before applying update. Rollback procedure: revert update-manifest.json to prior version S3 object via AWS CLI.
fix_report.expected_outcome: electron-updater clients verify installer integrity before applying; any corrupted or tampered artifact is rejected. Rollback is one S3 CLI command.

target_output.title: Professional Video Ingest Desktop App — Round 3 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: Cross-platform Electron 31 desktop application enabling professional video editors to ingest raw camera card footage, generate H.264 and ProRes proxy renditions via bundled FFmpeg, and upload assets to a cloud MAM system with chain-of-custody logging for SOC 2 compliance. Python FastAPI sidecar manages transcoding subprocesses and S3 multipart uploads. SQLite with tamper-evident triggers provides offline-capable job tracking. Signed installers auto-update via a SHA256-verified manifest.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron 31 main process spawns a Python FastAPI sidecar on localhost:18472 at launch and manages three renderer windows (IngestPanel, ProxyPreview, Settings) via typed contextBridge IPC channels. The main process owns the SQLite database handle; all DB writes from the renderer flow through IPC to the main process, ensuring single-writer access. FastAPI sidecar manages FFmpeg subprocess pool (2 concurrent transcode workers), S3 multipart uploads via boto3 TransferManager, and MAM webhook delivery with retry. SAML SSO opens the system browser; the resulting JWT is stored in OS keychain via Keytar and attached to all MAM API calls. AWS STS AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity provides short-lived S3 credentials refreshed 15 minutes before expiry. All cloud traffic over TLS 1.3. Electron contextIsolation enabled with nodeIntegration disabled in all renderer processes.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop: Electron 31, React 18, TypeScript, Zustand, React Query, electron-builder, electron-updater, Keytar. Backend sidecar: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2, ffmpeg-python, boto3 TransferManager. Local DB: SQLite 3.45 in WAL mode with Alembic migrations. Packaging: PyInstaller for sidecar binary, electron-builder for DMG (macOS) and NSIS (Windows). CI: GitHub Actions with Apple notarytool and Azure Key Vault Authenticode signing. Monitoring: Sentry with Electron and Python SDK, source map upload in CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Footage Ingest: drag-and-drop camera card folder, per-file SHA256 hashing with progress, duplicate detection via hash lookup. Proxy Transcoding: configurable transcode profiles (H.264 1080p, ProRes LT 4K, HEVC 4K), FFmpeg subprocess per clip with real-time progress from stderr parsing, ETA display. S3 Upload: multipart upload with UploadId resume on reconnect, per-clip progress bars, retry on 5xx. MAM Sync: webhook POST to MAM on upload completion, retry queue with exponential backoff, sync status per asset. Chain-of-Custody Log: INSERT-only SQLite log of all ingest, transcode, upload, and MAM-ack events, exportable to JSON for SOC 2 delivery. Auto-Update: electron-updater checks S3 manifest on launch, downloads and verifies SHA256, prompts editor to restart.
target_output.system_components: Electron Main Process (SQLite owner, IPC hub, Squirrel update listener), IngestPanel Renderer (drag-drop UI, job list, hash progress via contextBridge), ProxyPreview Renderer (HTML5 video on localhost FastAPI file server), Settings Renderer (MAM endpoint, codec presets, SAML re-auth), Python FastAPI Sidecar (transcode router, upload router, MAM webhook router, file server for proxy preview), SQLite DB (WAL mode, Alembic versioned schema, chain_of_custody_log with INSERT-only triggers), S3 Update Bucket (update-manifest.json, per-platform installer artifacts), Sentry (crash reports from both Electron and Python processes).
target_output.workflows: Camera Card Ingest: Editor drops /Volumes/CardA onto IngestPanel. Main process creates ingest_job record, sends IPC to FastAPI sidecar. Sidecar enumerates MXF and MOV files, computes SHA256 per file (streaming 4 MB chunks), writes footage_clips rows, updates ingest_job.hashed_files. Duplicate clips detected by hash lookup against existing footage_clips.sha256_hash — flagged in UI without blocking new files. On hash completion, transcode queue populated from default profile. Proxy Transcode and Upload: FastAPI thread pool picks transcode jobs, spawns ffmpeg-python subprocess per clip, parses stderr for frame/duration to compute progress percentage. On completion, proxy_renditions row written, status updated. Upload worker picks completed proxies, initiates S3 multipart via boto3 with UploadId stored in upload_queues for resume. On all parts complete, CompleteMultipartUpload called, clip s3_key updated, chain_of_custody_log INSERT for 'uploaded' action. MAM webhook POST dispatched with clip metadata and s3_key; on 200 response, chain_of_custody_log INSERT for 'mam_acked'.
target_output.data_model: ingest_jobs (id INTEGER PK, folder_path TEXT, total_files INTEGER, hashed_files INTEGER, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('queued','hashing','transcoding','uploading','complete','failed')), created_at TEXT, completed_at TEXT). footage_clips (id INTEGER PK, job_id INTEGER FK ingest_jobs, file_path TEXT, file_size_bytes INTEGER, sha256_hash TEXT UNIQUE, duration_seconds REAL, codec TEXT, s3_key TEXT, proxy_s3_key TEXT, mam_asset_id TEXT). transcode_profiles (id INTEGER PK, name TEXT, codec TEXT CHECK(codec IN ('h264','prores','hevc')), resolution TEXT, bitrate_kbps INTEGER, container TEXT). proxy_renditions (id INTEGER PK, clip_id INTEGER FK, profile_id INTEGER FK, local_path TEXT, transcode_duration_ms INTEGER, status TEXT). upload_queues (id INTEGER PK, clip_id INTEGER FK, s3_key TEXT, upload_id TEXT, parts_completed INTEGER, total_parts INTEGER, retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0, status TEXT). chain_of_custody_log (id INTEGER PK, clip_id INTEGER FK, operator_id TEXT, action TEXT CHECK(action IN ('ingested','transcoded','uploaded','mam_acked')), file_hash TEXT, timestamp TEXT). TRIGGER prevent_coc_update BEFORE UPDATE ON chain_of_custody_log BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT,'chain_of_custody_log is append-only'); END. TRIGGER prevent_coc_delete BEFORE DELETE ON chain_of_custody_log BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT,'chain_of_custody_log is append-only'); END.
target_output.api_design: FastAPI sidecar on localhost:18472. POST /ingest/jobs {folder_path} -> {job_id}. GET /ingest/jobs/{id} -> {status, hashed_files, total_files}. POST /transcode/jobs {clip_id, profile_id} -> {job_id}. GET /transcode/jobs/{id}/status -> {progress_pct, eta_seconds, status}. POST /upload/jobs {clip_id} -> {upload_queue_id}. GET /upload/jobs/{id}/status -> {parts_completed, total_parts, status}. POST /mam/sync {clip_id} -> {webhook_status, retry_count}. GET /files/proxy/{clip_id} -> file stream for HTML5 preview. GET /audit/chain-of-custody -> JSON export of chain_of_custody_log for SOC 2.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Electron security: contextIsolation=true, nodeIntegration=false, sandbox=true for all renderers. contextBridge exposes typed IPC methods only — no direct Node access from renderer. SAML SSO JWT in OS keychain via Keytar, never written to disk or localStorage. AWS STS short-lived tokens in memory, refreshed proactively 15 minutes before expiry via scheduled IPC call. SOC 2 chain-of-custody: chain_of_custody_log enforced INSERT-only at SQLite trigger layer independent of application code — BEFORE UPDATE and BEFORE DELETE triggers RAISE(ABORT). Audit log exportable to signed JSON for SOC 2 evidence delivery. macOS notarization via Apple notarytool ensures Gatekeeper approval. Windows Authenticode signing via Azure Key Vault prevents OS security warnings.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions release workflow triggered on version tag. Matrix strategy: {os: [macos-14, windows-2022]}. Steps: install Node and Python deps, run React build and PyInstaller sidecar bundle, run electron-builder (DMG for macOS, NSIS for Windows), compute SHA256 of each artifact via sha256sum, submit macOS DMG to Apple notarytool (async, poll for completion), sign Windows NSIS installer via Azure Key Vault Authenticode action. Publish artifacts to s3://releases-bucket/releases/{version}/. Generate update-manifest.json: {version, releaseDate, files:[{platform:'darwin',url,sha256,size},{platform:'win32',url,sha256,size}], releaseNotes}. Upload manifest to s3://releases-bucket/update-manifest.json. electron-updater on client checks manifest on app launch, compares local version, downloads installer if newer, verifies SHA256 before applying. Rollback: aws s3 cp s3://releases-bucket/releases/{prev-version}/update-manifest.json s3://releases-bucket/update-manifest.json.
target_output.observability: Sentry Electron SDK captures renderer exceptions with React error boundaries and source maps uploaded in CI. Sentry Python SDK captures FastAPI sidecar exceptions including FFmpeg subprocess stderr on failure. Local session log files at ~/Library/Logs/VideoIngest/ (macOS) and %APPDATA%/VideoIngest/logs/ (Windows) capture structured JSON per ingest session: job_id, clip counts, transcode durations, upload speeds, MAM webhook outcomes. FFmpeg transcode duration per clip logged to proxy_renditions.transcode_duration_ms for performance regression detection. Sentry performance tracing on POST /ingest/jobs and POST /transcode/jobs to track p95 hash and transcode latency.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: S3 storage cost scales with raw footage volume — estimated $23/TB/month standard tier, $4/TB for Glacier archival after 90 days via lifecycle policy. EC2/compute costs are zero — all processing runs on editor workstations. Auto-update S3 bucket with CloudFront: ~$2/month for installer delivery. Sentry free tier covers 5K errors/month; upgrade to team plan at $26/month if pilot exceeds threshold. Scaling is horizontal by adding editor workstations — no server-side compute to provision.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Folder ingest with SHA256 hashing, H.264 proxy transcoding, SQLite job tracking, S3 multipart upload, MAM webhook on completion, SAML SSO with Keytar, signed macOS DMG and Windows installer, Sentry crash reporting, chain_of_custody_log with INSERT-only triggers (ISS-001 resolved), SHA256-verified auto-update manifest (ISS-002 resolved). Phase 2 (4 weeks): Transcode profile editor in Settings UI, ProRes LT and HEVC codec support, proxy preview with chapter navigation, duplicate detection UI with override. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Batch XMP metadata editing, MAM sync status dashboard, offline queue resume UI with per-clip retry controls, Alembic migration for schema versioning on update.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in renderer and main process. mypy strict for FastAPI sidecar. ESLint + Prettier enforced in CI. React unit tests via Vitest with happy-dom. FastAPI endpoint tests via pytest with SQLite in-memory fixture. Electron E2E via Playwright with electron-playwright-helpers for IPC mocking. PR requires 1 approval. Never store AWS tokens or SAML JWT outside OS keychain. chain_of_custody_log: application code must never execute UPDATE or DELETE — SQLite trigger is the enforcement layer but code review is the second line of defense.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Apple notarization latency (2-10 min) blocks release pipeline. Mitigation: async notarization with webhook callback; CI waits with 15-minute timeout before failing. Risk: PyInstaller sidecar binary size (~45 MB) increases installer footprint. Mitigation: exclude unused Python stdlib modules via PyInstaller spec file. Risk: FFmpeg subprocess crash leaves ingest_job in transcoding state. Mitigation: main process monitors sidecar heartbeat; on sidecar restart, reconciliation query sets stalled jobs back to queued. Tradeoff: SQLite single-writer via main process IPC adds latency vs direct DB access from sidecar — accepted to guarantee WAL integrity and avoid write conflicts. Tradeoff: bundled static FFmpeg binary per platform (~60 MB each) eliminates runtime dependency at cost of installer size.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Bundled static FFmpeg binary chosen over system FFmpeg for reproducible codec support. SQLite WAL mode selected over journal mode for concurrent reads during active transcode. SAML SSO via system browser chosen over embedded webview to avoid Electron webview SAML compatibility issues. S3 multipart upload chosen with UploadId persistence for resume on network interruption. Apple notarytool async submission chosen over legacy altool for Xcode 15+ compatibility.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving SOC 2 chain-of-custody append-only log and auto-update manifest schema for Electron video ingest desktop app.

project_goal.value: AI-driven sports highlight extraction system that ingests full-game broadcast recordings, identifies key moments using audio and visual cues, and produces shareable clip packages for rights holders
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual highlight editor workflow that takes 4-6 hours per game with automated pipeline completing in under 30 minutes

target_users.value: Broadcast rights holders, sports league digital teams, and OTT platform content managers
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of highlight packages for distribution to social, broadcast, and streaming channels

project_class.rationale: Core value proposition is ML-based audio and visual event detection; inference pipeline is the primary system concern

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, batch_jobs, integrations, analytics
capabilities.rationale: FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL data layer, LLM-assisted event classification, SQS batch jobs, partner SFTP/API delivery, Grafana analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Full-game recordings are 90-180 minute 4K files; parallel GPU inference on multiple games simultaneously; partner SLA requires clips within 30 minutes of final whistle

risk_level.rationale: Internal broadcast footage handling; SOC 2 scope; no PII; partner API exposure requires mTLS

data_sensitivity.rationale: Raw broadcast recordings are proprietary internal assets under rights agreement; no personal data processed

external_exposure.rationale: Rights holder portal and delivery API exposed to external partners; requires mTLS and API key authentication

access_model.value: Partner portal via Auth0 with org-scoped API keys; internal operators via Auth0 SSO; mTLS for partner delivery API endpoints
access_model.rationale: Partners need scoped access to their own league's content only; mTLS required for automated distribution integrations

feature_scope.value: S3 broadcast ingest trigger, audio crowd-reaction classifier, visual action-density scorer, highlight window assembly, multi-aspect-ratio export (16:9 broadcast, 9:16 social, 1:1 OTT), partner review portal, SFTP and API delivery, analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end pipeline from ingest to partner delivery with human review gate

mvp_scope.value: Single sport (football), audio classifier + visual scorer, 16:9 broadcast export, partner portal review, S3 and SFTP delivery
mvp_scope.rationale: Football has largest rights holder base; proves pipeline before expanding to basketball and cricket

security_baseline.value: mTLS for partner delivery API, Auth0 for portal authentication, TLS 1.3 for all endpoints, S3 server-side encryption, IAM role-based access to GPU instances and S3 buckets
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 Type II and broadcast rights agreement requirements

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript (operator review UI and partner portal)
frontend_stack.rationale: Review portal requires video playback with frame-accurate clip boundary editing

backend_stack.rationale: ML inference libraries (PyTorch, Whisper, CLIP) are Python-native; FastAPI for REST API and webhook delivery

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for job and clip metadata; S3 for raw recordings and rendered clip assets
data_platform.rationale: Relational metadata for complex query patterns; S3 for large binary assets

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for API and orchestration; GPU spot instances (g4dn.xlarge) for inference workers; ECS Fargate for export renderer
hosting_target.rationale: GPU spot instances minimize inference cost; Fargate for stateless API and renderer

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw broadcast recordings retained 7 days after highlight package delivery; rendered clips retained 90 days; audit logs retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Rights agreements limit storage duration; SOC 2 requires audit log retention

future_scope.value: Multi-sport expansion (basketball, cricket, F1), real-time live broadcast highlight detection, LLM-generated commentary for clips, automated social caption generation
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap after football pipeline validated at scale

constraints.value: 30-minute SLA from recording availability to partner portal delivery; GPU spot instance budget cap $8,000/month; mTLS mandatory for all partner delivery endpoints
constraints.rationale: Partner SLA contractually defined; GPU budget approved by finance; mTLS is security non-negotiable for external API

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for pipeline metrics (inference latency, clip count per game, SLA adherence); CloudWatch for ECS and Lambda logs; PagerDuty for SLA breach alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Pipeline SLA monitoring is operationally critical; partner-facing SLA requires proactive alerting

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; ML model evaluation cycles separate from API release cycle
execution_preference.rationale: Model training and evaluation cadence decoupled from software release cadence

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II for cloud storage and access controls; broadcast rights licensing agreements govern clip distribution scope per partner
compliance_context.rationale: SOC 2 required by enterprise rights holders; licensing scope per partner enforced at API layer

llm_integration.value: LLM-assisted event type classification: detected audio/visual windows classified by GPT-4o into event taxonomy (goal, save, red_card, penalty, corner, foul) with confidence scores; LLM also generates short clip description text for partner portal display
llm_integration.rationale: Rule-based classification had 67% accuracy on event taxonomy; GPT-4o with structured output achieves 91% on validation set

project.goal: Automated sports highlight extraction pipeline from broadcast recording to partner-deliverable clip package
project.target_users: Rights holders, league digital teams, OTT content managers
project.timeline: MVP football pipeline in 10 weeks; multi-sport expansion in Q3

backend.framework: FastAPI with async endpoints
backend.ml: PyTorch 2.3, OpenAI Whisper large-v3 for audio, CLIP ViT-L/14 for visual scoring
backend.llm: GPT-4o via OpenAI API with structured output for event classification

security.auth: Auth0 for portal and operator SSO, org-scoped API keys for partner integrations
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for all endpoints, mTLS mandatory for partner delivery API
security.credentials: IAM roles for ECS tasks, Secrets Manager for Auth0 and OpenAI credentials

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 via SQLAlchemy async, RDS Multi-AZ
data.assets: S3 with lifecycle policies, pre-signed URLs for clip delivery to portal

devops.hosting: ECS Fargate (API, orchestrator, renderer), GPU spot g4dn.xlarge (audio+visual inference)
devops.environments: dev, staging, production with separate S3 buckets and DB instances

constraints.sla: 30 minutes from recording upload to portal delivery
constraints.mtls: Mandatory for partner delivery API endpoints

reasoner_reviews.product: The 30-minute SLA from recording to partner portal is the primary design constraint. Audio classification followed by visual scoring in sequence would take too long — these must run in parallel on overlapping windows. The partner review gate with override capability is essential for rights holder trust before enabling fully automated delivery.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: GPU spot instance interruption is the main reliability risk for inference workers. The orchestrator must handle spot interruption by checkpointing window-level inference progress and retrying on a new instance. PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ is correct for the pipeline state; avoid DynamoDB here since query patterns include range scans by game_id and event_type. mTLS for the partner delivery API is architecturally straightforward — ACM Private CA with per-partner client certificate issuance.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS for partner delivery endpoints requires ACM Private CA and per-partner client certificate provisioning workflow. Org-scoped API keys must be rate-limited and rotated on partner request. S3 pre-signed URLs for clip delivery should expire in 1 hour to limit unauthorized forwarding of rights-protected content. IAM roles per ECS task family with least-privilege S3 access scoped to specific bucket prefixes per league.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 30-minute SLA for a 90-minute 4K football match requires parallel audio and visual inference. Audio (Whisper) on a g4dn.xlarge processes 90 minutes in approximately 8 minutes. Visual CLIP scoring at 2-second sampling rate processes 2,700 frames in approximately 12 minutes. Parallel execution leaves 10 minutes for assembly, LLM classification, rendering, and delivery — feasible with tight orchestration.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The LLM classification step using GPT-4o introduces external API latency and potential rate limiting. Structured output with response_format JSON schema is critical to avoid parsing failures. Fallback to rule-based classification when GPT-4o returns non-200 must be specified to protect SLA.

backend.service_design: Orchestrator service (ECS Fargate, always-on) manages game pipeline state machine: INGESTED -> AUDIO_INFERENCE -> VISUAL_INFERENCE -> LLM_CLASSIFICATION -> ASSEMBLY -> RENDERING -> REVIEW_PENDING -> DELIVERED. Inference worker pool (GPU spot g4dn.xlarge, auto-scaling 0-8) pulls jobs from SQS. FastAPI API service (ECS Fargate, always-on) serves partner portal and delivery API.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0. Partner delivery API uses mTLS with client certificate CN mapped to rights_profile_id. Internal orchestrator API uses IAM auth. Webhook callbacks to partner systems on delivery completion.
backend.business_modules: AudioClassifier (Whisper large-v3 transcription + crowd reaction detection via energy and keyword patterns), VisualScorer (CLIP ViT-L/14 frame embedding similarity against action template library), EventClassifier (GPT-4o structured output with fallback rule engine), ClipAssembler (window merging with minimum gap coalescing, max clip duration enforcement), MultiFormatRenderer (FFmpeg for 16:9/9:16/1:1 aspect ratio exports).
backend.background_jobs: SQS queues: audio-inference-queue, visual-inference-queue, llm-classification-queue, rendering-queue. Dead-letter queues with max_receive_count=3. Spot instance interruption handler checkpoints window-level progress to S3 state prefix before termination.
backend.llm_integration_design: GPT-4o called per detected candidate window with context: audio transcript excerpt, visual score percentile, surrounding commentary keywords. System prompt specifies football event taxonomy with confidence threshold. response_format={type:json_schema} with schema enforcing event_type ENUM and confidence FLOAT. Fallback: if GPT-4o returns non-200 or confidence <0.4, rule-based classifier assigns event_type from audio keyword match.
backend.scaling_notes: GPU spot pool scales 0-8 instances via ECS Service auto-scaling on SQS queue depth (target 5 messages per instance). Fargate API and orchestrator scale on CPU 70% target. Peak load: 12 simultaneous game pipelines on match day — requires 6-8 GPU instances concurrently.

data.entities: broadcast_recordings (id, league_id, game_id, sport ENUM football/basketball/cricket, s3_key, duration_seconds, file_size_bytes, status ENUM ingested/processing/complete/failed, created_at). detected_events (id, recording_id, window_start_seconds, window_end_seconds, audio_confidence FLOAT, visual_score FLOAT, event_type ENUM goal/save/red_card/penalty/corner/foul/unknown, llm_confidence FLOAT, llm_reasoning TEXT, approved BOOLEAN). highlight_clips (id, recording_id, event_ids INTEGER[], start_seconds FLOAT, end_seconds FLOAT, duration_seconds FLOAT, title TEXT, s3_key_16x9 TEXT, s3_key_9x16 TEXT, s3_key_1x1 TEXT, delivery_status). sport_event_metadata (id, recording_id, home_team, away_team, competition, match_date, venue, final_score). model_inference_jobs (id, recording_id, job_type ENUM audio/visual/llm, sqs_message_id, status, instance_id, checkpoint_s3_key, started_at, completed_at, error_log). rights_profiles (id, partner_id, league_id, allowed_event_types TEXT[], max_clips_per_game INTEGER, delivery_method ENUM sftp/api/s3, watermark_required BOOLEAN). distribution_packages (id, recording_id, rights_profile_id, clip_ids INTEGER[], delivery_timestamp, delivery_status, partner_ack).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ for all metadata. S3 bucket structure: s3://recordings/{league}/{year}/{game_id}/raw.mp4, s3://clips/{game_id}/{clip_id}/{format}.mp4. Pre-signed GET URLs (1-hour expiry) for portal video playback. S3 lifecycle: raw recordings to Glacier after 7 days, clips to Glacier after 90 days.
data.analytics_events: game_pipeline_completed (recording_id, total_events_detected, clips_produced, end_to_end_minutes), clip_approved (clip_id, operator_id, event_type), clip_rejected (clip_id, operator_id, rejection_reason), partner_delivery_completed (distribution_package_id, delivery_latency_seconds), sla_breach (recording_id, elapsed_minutes).

security.auth_design: Auth0 M2M applications for partner API integrations with org-scoped API keys. Auth0 regular web app for partner portal and operator dashboard with SAML SSO federation. mTLS via ACM Private CA: per-partner client certificate issued during partner onboarding, CN=partner-{partner_id}, validated at ALB listener layer.
security.authorization_model: Rights profiles enforced at clip delivery layer: delivery API validates that requested clip's league_id matches requesting partner's rights_profile.league_id. Operators have full access to all leagues. Partners see only their org's games and clips.
security.secrets_management: OpenAI API key in AWS Secrets Manager, injected as environment variable into ECS inference tasks. Auth0 client secrets in Secrets Manager. Partner SFTP credentials in Secrets Manager per partner. 30-day automatic rotation for internal service credentials.
security.security_risks: GPT-4o structured output could include broadcast transcript content in API payloads — ensure no raw audio transcript sent to OpenAI beyond short keyword windows. mTLS certificate revocation on partner offboarding must be tested in staging.

devops.deployment_topology: VPC with public subnet (ALB, NAT gateway) and private subnets (ECS Fargate tasks, RDS, GPU instances). GPU instances in private subnet with NAT for outbound S3 and OpenAI API calls. ALB with mTLS listener for partner delivery API (ACM Private CA), separate ALB listener for portal (Auth0 JWT). ECS clusters: api-cluster (Fargate), orchestrator-cluster (Fargate), inference-cluster (GPU spot EC2).
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: PR lint (ruff, mypy), pytest with RDS test container, Docker build and push to ECR, deploy ECS rolling update. ML model updates: separate GitHub Actions workflow triggers model evaluation job on staging GPU instance, gates promotion on held-out test set accuracy threshold (>=88%).
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping ECS task custom metrics endpoint: active_inference_jobs, queue_depth_audio, queue_depth_visual, sla_minutes_elapsed per game, clip_detection_rate. Grafana dashboards: Pipeline SLA Board, GPU Utilization, Event Type Distribution per League. PagerDuty alert on sla_minutes_elapsed > 25 (5-minute warning before SLA breach).
devops.devops_risks: GPU spot interruption during inference checkpoint window. Mitigation: interruption handler writes window-level checkpoint to S3 before instance termination; orchestrator detects stale SQS message visibility and requeues.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a high-scale AI inference pipeline with a hard 30-minute SLA from recording availability to partner delivery. The critical path is parallel audio (Whisper) + visual (CLIP) inference on GPU spot instances, then LLM classification via GPT-4o with structured output and rule-based fallback, followed by clip assembly, multi-format rendering, and mTLS partner delivery. The architecture centers on SQS-driven GPU worker pools with spot interruption checkpointing, PostgreSQL pipeline state, and rights-scoped delivery via partner-specific mTLS certificates issued by ACM Private CA. The operator review gate before delivery is the trust-building mechanism for rights holders during initial rollout.
target_output.title: AI Sports Highlight Extraction Pipeline Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An AI-driven pipeline that ingests full-game broadcast recordings from S3, runs parallel Whisper audio classification and CLIP visual scoring on GPU spot instances, classifies detected event windows via GPT-4o structured output with rule-based fallback, assembles highlight clips, renders multi-aspect-ratio exports, and delivers partner-specific packages via mTLS API or SFTP. PostgreSQL tracks all pipeline state. Partner rights profiles scope clip access and delivery permissions. The system targets a 30-minute SLA from recording upload to partner portal availability, with an optional operator review gate during initial rollout.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven pipeline architecture on AWS. An S3 ObjectCreated trigger on the recordings bucket fires an SQS message to the orchestrator service running on ECS Fargate. The orchestrator manages a per-recording state machine (INGESTED -> AUDIO_INFERENCE -> VISUAL_INFERENCE -> LLM_CLASSIFICATION -> ASSEMBLY -> RENDERING -> REVIEW_PENDING -> DELIVERED) by publishing jobs to dedicated SQS queues consumed by GPU spot inference workers and Fargate renderer tasks. Audio inference (Whisper large-v3) and visual inference (CLIP ViT-L/14) run in parallel on separate SQS queues against the same recording. Results merge in the orchestrator for LLM classification. A FastAPI service fronts the partner portal and delivery API; the delivery API enforces per-partner mTLS using ACM Private CA client certificates, and rights profiles gate which clips each partner can retrieve. Grafana monitors SLA adherence per game pipeline in real time.
target_output.technology_stack: Inference: Python 3.12, PyTorch 2.3, OpenAI Whisper large-v3, CLIP ViT-L/14 (OpenAI), GPT-4o via OpenAI API with JSON schema structured output. API and orchestrator: FastAPI with async SQLAlchemy 2, Pydantic v2. Data: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, S3 with lifecycle policies. Messaging: SQS standard queues with DLQ. Compute: ECS Fargate (API, orchestrator, renderer), GPU spot EC2 g4dn.xlarge (inference workers). Rendering: FFmpeg 6 for multi-format export. Auth: Auth0 M2M and SAML, ACM Private CA for mTLS. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, ECS rolling deployments. Observability: Prometheus custom metrics, Grafana dashboards, PagerDuty, CloudWatch.
target_output.functional_feature_map: S3 Ingest Trigger: ObjectCreated on recordings bucket creates broadcast_recordings row and enqueues orchestration start. Audio Classification: Whisper large-v3 transcribes commentary track and crowd audio; energy-based crowd reaction detector flags high-intensity windows; results written to detected_events with audio_confidence. Visual Scoring: CLIP ViT-L/14 scores 2-second frame samples against action template embeddings; high-scoring windows marked as visual candidates. LLM Event Classification: GPT-4o classifies merged audio+visual windows into football event taxonomy with structured JSON output; fallback rule engine on API failure. Clip Assembly: high-confidence events merged, minimum gap coalescing applied (events within 8 seconds merged), max clip duration enforced (90 seconds). Multi-Format Rendering: FFmpeg renders 16:9 broadcast, 9:16 social, 1:1 OTT variants per clip. Operator Review Portal: React UI with video player, clip boundary adjustment, event type override, approve/reject controls. Partner Delivery: mTLS API and SFTP delivery scoped by rights_profile; webhook notification on package availability.
target_output.system_components: S3 Recordings Bucket (ObjectCreated trigger, lifecycle to Glacier after 7 days), SQS audio-inference-queue + visual-inference-queue + llm-classification-queue + rendering-queue (each with DLQ), Orchestrator Service (ECS Fargate, state machine coordinator, SQS consumer/producer, RDS writer), Audio Inference Workers (GPU spot g4dn.xlarge pool, Whisper large-v3, SQS consumer, checkpoint to S3), Visual Inference Workers (GPU spot g4dn.xlarge pool, CLIP ViT-L/14, SQS consumer, checkpoint to S3), LLM Classifier (ECS Fargate, GPT-4o structured output + rule fallback), Clip Assembler (ECS Fargate, event merging and duration enforcement), Renderer (ECS Fargate, FFmpeg multi-format export), FastAPI API Service (ECS Fargate, partner portal backend and mTLS delivery API), PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ (pipeline metadata), ACM Private CA (mTLS client certificates for partners), S3 Clips Bucket (rendered clip assets, pre-signed URLs), Grafana + Prometheus (SLA monitoring).
target_output.workflows: Game Pipeline: S3 ObjectCreated event on recordings/{league}/{year}/{game_id}/raw.mp4 triggers Lambda that creates broadcast_recordings row (status=INGESTED) and sends orchestrator SQS message. Orchestrator publishes to audio-inference-queue and visual-inference-queue simultaneously. Audio worker downloads recording, runs Whisper on 5-minute sliding chunks with 30-second overlap, detects crowd energy peaks, writes detected_events rows (audio_confidence > 0.6 threshold). Visual worker samples every 2 seconds, computes CLIP similarity to action template set, writes visual_score to detected_events rows. Orchestrator waits for both queue completions (polling detected_events.status), merges windows, sends to llm-classification-queue. LLM classifier calls GPT-4o with event context and structured output schema, writes event_type and llm_confidence, applies rule fallback if GPT-4o unavailable. Assembler merges events, enforces 8-second gap coalescing and 90-second max duration, creates highlight_clips rows. Renderer processes rendering-queue, runs FFmpeg for each format, uploads to S3 clips bucket, updates highlight_clips with s3_keys. Orchestrator sets status=REVIEW_PENDING, notifies operators. Partner Delivery: Operator approves clips in review portal. Orchestrator creates distribution_packages per rights_profile matching game league. Partner API caller authenticates via mTLS (CN validated against rights_profile_id), calls GET /v1/packages/{id}/clips to receive pre-signed S3 URLs (1-hour expiry) or SFTP push is triggered to partner endpoint.
target_output.data_model: broadcast_recordings (id UUID PK, league_id UUID FK, game_id TEXT UNIQUE, sport TEXT, s3_key TEXT, duration_seconds INTEGER, file_size_bytes BIGINT, status TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). detected_events (id UUID PK, recording_id UUID FK, window_start_seconds FLOAT, window_end_seconds FLOAT, audio_confidence FLOAT, visual_score FLOAT, event_type TEXT, llm_confidence FLOAT, llm_reasoning TEXT, approved BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). highlight_clips (id UUID PK, recording_id UUID FK, event_ids UUID[], start_seconds FLOAT, end_seconds FLOAT, duration_seconds FLOAT, title TEXT, s3_key_16x9 TEXT, s3_key_9x16 TEXT, s3_key_1x1 TEXT, delivery_status TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). sport_event_metadata (id UUID PK, recording_id UUID FK, home_team TEXT, away_team TEXT, competition TEXT, match_date DATE, venue TEXT, final_score TEXT). model_inference_jobs (id UUID PK, recording_id UUID FK, job_type TEXT, sqs_message_id TEXT, status TEXT, instance_id TEXT, checkpoint_s3_key TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, error_log TEXT). rights_profiles (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, league_id UUID FK, allowed_event_types TEXT[], max_clips_per_game INTEGER, delivery_method TEXT, watermark_required BOOLEAN). distribution_packages (id UUID PK, recording_id UUID FK, rights_profile_id UUID FK, clip_ids UUID[], delivery_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, delivery_status TEXT, partner_ack BOOLEAN).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI REST API with OpenAPI 3.0 at /api/v1. Operator endpoints (Auth0 JWT): GET /recordings?league_id=&status= (pipeline queue), GET /recordings/{id}/events (detected events with confidence scores), PATCH /events/{id} {approved:true,event_type:'goal'} (operator override), GET /recordings/{id}/clips (assembled clips for review), POST /clips/{id}/approve, POST /clips/{id}/reject. Partner delivery API (mTLS, Auth0 M2M API key): GET /v1/packages?game_id= (available packages for partner's leagues), GET /v1/packages/{id}/clips (returns array of {clip_id, event_type, duration, presigned_url_16x9, presigned_url_9x16, presigned_url_1x1} with 1-hour URL expiry), POST /v1/delivery/acknowledge {package_id} (partner confirms receipt). Webhook: POST to partner-registered URL on package availability with package_id and game metadata.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS: ACM Private CA issues per-partner client certificates (CN=partner-{partner_id}) during onboarding. ALB listener for /api/v1/* requires client certificate with trust store anchored to ACM Private CA. Certificate revocation on partner offboarding via CRL update. Auth0: operator dashboard uses SAML SSO org; partner portal uses Auth0 regular web app with org-scoped API keys. Rights enforcement: delivery API checks clip.recording.league_id == requesting_partner.rights_profile.league_id before returning pre-signed URLs. S3 pre-signed URLs expire in 1 hour to limit redistribution window. IAM task roles scoped per ECS task family: inference-worker-role (s3:GetObject on recordings bucket, s3:PutObject on inference-checkpoint prefix), renderer-role (s3:PutObject on clips bucket), api-role (s3:GetObject on clips bucket for pre-signed URL generation). OpenAI API key in Secrets Manager — inference task role grants secretsmanager:GetSecretValue for openai-api-key ARN only. SOC 2: CloudTrail for all AWS API calls; application audit log for operator approvals and partner deliveries written to PostgreSQL audit_events table; log retention 1 year.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff + mypy + pytest (RDS test container) on PR. Docker build + ECR push on merge to main. ECS rolling update for api-service and orchestrator-service. Inference worker AMI baked with PyTorch 2.3 + model weights pre-loaded on GPU volume for fast cold start. ECR image for GPU workers updated via separate model-update workflow gated on evaluation accuracy threshold (>=88% on held-out football validation set). Environments: dev (Fargate Spot, single AZ, RDS t3.medium), staging (Fargate Spot, multi-AZ, RDS t3.large, full GPU test with sample recordings), production (Fargate On-Demand for API/orchestrator, GPU spot g4dn.xlarge pool 0-8, RDS m6g.large Multi-AZ). ECS Service Connect for internal service discovery. Secrets Manager for all credentials with 30-day rotation. S3 lifecycle: raw recordings to S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval after 7 days, clips to Glacier after 90 days.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scraping custom metrics endpoint on each ECS task: active_audio_inference_jobs, active_visual_inference_jobs, sla_elapsed_minutes{game_id} (critical metric), clip_detection_rate{league}, llm_classification_latency_seconds, gpu_utilization_pct, sqs_queue_depth{queue_name}. Grafana dashboards: Pipeline SLA Board (per-game elapsed time with 25-minute warning threshold, 30-minute breach threshold), GPU Fleet (utilization, spot interruption count, active instances), Event Detection Quality (event_type distribution, avg llm_confidence by sport, fallback_rate). PagerDuty: sla_elapsed_minutes > 25 -> warning page to on-call; sla_elapsed_minutes > 30 -> critical page with game_id. CloudWatch Logs Insights for ECS task and Lambda logs. Sentry for FastAPI exception tracking with game_id context tag.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GPU inference: g4dn.xlarge spot ~$0.16/hr; 30-minute pipeline per game = $0.08 per game per inference instance. 100 games/week with 2 parallel inference instances = ~$800/month GPU cost, well under $8,000 cap. ECS Fargate: API + orchestrator + renderer ~$200/month. RDS m6g.large Multi-AZ ~$250/month. S3 storage (7-day raw + 90-day clips): ~$150/month for 100 games/week. OpenAI GPT-4o: ~$0.04 per game (avg 25 events x 8 events x $0.002) = ~$160/month. Total estimated: ~$1,600/month at 100 games/week. Scaling: GPU pool auto-scales on SQS depth (target 5 messages per instance) up to 8 instances; handles 16 simultaneous games. Cost ceiling enforced via AWS Budgets alert at $7,500.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Football pipeline only — S3 ingest trigger, Whisper audio classifier, CLIP visual scorer, parallel SQS processing, GPT-4o LLM classification with rule fallback, clip assembler, 16:9 broadcast FFmpeg renderer, operator review portal (React + video player + approve/reject), partner S3 pre-signed URL delivery with mTLS, PostgreSQL pipeline state, Prometheus + Grafana SLA dashboard, PagerDuty SLA alert. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Multi-aspect-ratio rendering (9:16 social, 1:1 OTT), SFTP delivery for partners requiring push delivery, partner webhook notification, rights_profiles enforcement with max_clips_per_game, Analytics dashboard (event type distribution, detection quality by league). Phase 3 (6 weeks): Basketball and cricket sport expansion (new CLIP action templates, updated LLM taxonomy), real-time live stream highlight detection research spike, auto-delivery mode (bypass operator review for high-confidence events), LLM-generated clip description text for partner portal.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python ruff + mypy strict in CI. pytest with 85% coverage target for orchestrator and inference modules. Docker multi-stage builds: model weights baked into inference worker image to eliminate download latency on cold start. Feature flags (LaunchDarkly) gate new sport expansions and auto-delivery mode. All SQS consumers implement idempotent processing keyed on sqs_message_id to handle at-least-once delivery. GPT-4o calls must always specify response_format with JSON schema and include timeout=30s and max_retries=2 before triggering rule fallback. Never log broadcast transcript content to application logs — only log event_type and confidence scores.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPU spot interruption mid-inference misses SLA for a game. Mitigation: Whisper processes audio in 5-minute chunks with S3 checkpoint after each chunk; CLIP checkpoints after each 10-minute visual window; orchestrator requeues from last checkpoint on spot interruption. Risk: GPT-4o API rate limit during match-day peak (12 simultaneous games). Mitigation: LLM classification SQS queue with per-game concurrency limit; rule-based fallback activated automatically on non-200. Tradeoff: Whisper large-v3 vs small-v3 — large-v3 achieves 94% crowd reaction recall vs 81% for small-v3 at 3x GPU time; accepted given SLA headroom. Tradeoff: operator review gate adds latency but is required for rights holder trust during initial rollout — auto-delivery mode planned for Phase 3 after 3-month accuracy validation period.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Parallel audio and visual inference chosen over sequential to meet 30-minute SLA — adds orchestration complexity but halves inference time. PostgreSQL chosen over DynamoDB for pipeline state due to complex range query patterns (events by recording_id and confidence range). ACM Private CA chosen for mTLS over self-signed CA for operational simplicity and CRL management. GPT-4o structured output with JSON schema chosen over function calling for cleaner event taxonomy enforcement.

metadata.notes: High-scale AI sports highlight extraction pipeline with parallel GPU inference, LLM event classification, mTLS partner delivery, and 30-minute SLA.

project_goal.value: AI-powered multi-language subtitle generation system for streaming platforms, with independent microservices for ASR transcription, machine translation, and subtitle formatting and burn-in
project_goal.rationale: Decomposes a tightly coupled monolith that could not scale ASR and translation independently; independent scaling reduces processing cost by 40%

target_users.value: Streaming platform operators and their internal content localization teams submitting video assets for subtitle generation
target_users.rationale: B2B platform serving content operations teams at streaming services

project_class.rationale: Core value is ASR transcription and MT translation pipelines; microservices decomposition is secondary structural concern

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, batch_jobs, integrations, public_api
capabilities.rationale: FastAPI microservices, MongoDB persistence, LLM post-editing, async batch processing, S3/Blob delivery, public REST API for job submission

complexity_level.rationale: Fan-out translation to N language pods, timing offset correction, multi-format subtitle export, GDPR for voice data

risk_level.rationale: Internal content handling with GDPR exposure for content containing identifiable voices; Azure AD auth for all endpoints

data_sensitivity.rationale: Video content is proprietary; transcripts may contain identifiable voice/likeness data triggering GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: Public REST API exposed only to authenticated streaming platform customers via Azure AD M2M tokens

access_model.value: Azure AD (Entra ID) M2M client credentials for API access; role-based scopes for job submission, status read, and delivery retrieval
access_model.rationale: Enterprise IAM standard across all Azure-hosted services

feature_scope.value: REST API for job submission with target language codes, ASR transcription service (Whisper large-v3), fan-out MT translation per language (Helsinki-NLP OPUS-MT + LLM post-editing), timing offset correction, SRT/VTT/TTML export, delivery to S3 or Azure Blob, quality review flag propagation
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end subtitle pipeline with multi-format delivery and quality gates
