mvp_scope.value: English ASR, Spanish and French MT, SRT and VTT export, Azure Blob delivery, REST API for job submission and status polling
mvp_scope.rationale: Most-requested language pair covers 60% of backlog; proves microservices decomposition before expanding to 20 languages

security_baseline.value: Azure AD M2M tokens, TLS 1.3, AKS network policies isolating service pods, Azure Key Vault for all model API secrets, no raw transcript data in application logs
security_baseline.rationale: GDPR requires transcript data minimization; AKS network policies enforce microservice isolation

frontend_stack.value: None — API-only system with no UI
frontend_stack.rationale: B2B API service; streaming platform customers integrate via REST API; no operator UI in scope

backend_stack.rationale: ASR and MT libraries (Transformers, Faster-Whisper) are Python-native; consistent language across all microservices

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for job and segment metadata; Azure Blob for video assets and subtitle output files
data_platform.rationale: Flexible document schema for variable-length transcript segments; Azure Blob co-located with AKS for low-latency asset access

hosting_target.value: AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service) with namespace-per-service isolation
hosting_target.rationale: AKS Autopilot handles GPU node provisioning for ASR pods; namespace isolation enforces microservice boundaries

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw transcript text retained 30 days post-delivery then hard-deleted; subtitle output files retained 180 days; job metadata retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimization for transcript content that may contain identifiable voices

future_scope.value: LLM-based subtitle post-editing quality scoring, speaker diarization, real-time caption streaming for live events, 20-language expansion
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap after MVP language pairs validated

constraints.value: Azure-only deployment per enterprise cloud policy; GPU node pools capped at 4 concurrent ASR pods; GDPR transcript deletion must execute within 48 hours of deletion request
constraints.rationale: Cloud policy and GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure deadline

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor + Application Insights for all microservices; custom metrics for ASR word error rate proxy and MT BLEU score distribution; Azure DevOps Pipelines
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate Azure monitoring standard; translation quality metrics are business KPIs

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; microservices deployed independently via separate Azure Pipelines per service
execution_preference.rationale: Independent deployment cadence is a core benefit of the microservices decomposition

compliance_context.value: GDPR for content containing identifiable voices or likenesses; transcript data subject to right-to-erasure within 48 hours
compliance_context.rationale: Video content may include speaker voices that qualify as biometric data under GDPR Article 9 in some jurisdictions

llm_integration.value: GPT-4o used for MT post-editing: after Helsinki-NLP OPUS-MT translation, GPT-4o reviews subtitle segments for colloquialisms, idioms, and speaker register consistency; corrected segments flagged with llm_edited=true for quality reviewer sampling
llm_integration.rationale: OPUS-MT BLEU scores on colloquial dialogue average 0.61; GPT-4o post-editing raises to 0.74 on held-out validation set

project.goal: Decompose monolithic subtitle pipeline into independently scalable ASR, MT, and formatting microservices
project.target_users: Streaming platform content localization teams
project.timeline: MVP (English->ES/FR, SRT/VTT) in 8 weeks; full decomposition in 12 weeks

backend.framework: FastAPI microservices
backend.mt: Helsinki-NLP OPUS-MT models per language pair + GPT-4o post-editing
backend.async: Azure Service Bus for inter-service message passing

security.auth: Azure AD Entra ID M2M client credentials, JWT validation per service
security.network: AKS network policies blocking cross-namespace pod communication except defined ingress
security.secrets: Azure Key Vault CSI driver for all model API credentials and DB connection strings

data.db: MongoDB 7 Atlas on Azure for job metadata and transcript segments
data.assets: Azure Blob Storage for video input and subtitle output files

devops.hosting: AKS Autopilot with GPU node pool (Standard_NC4as_T4_v3) for ASR pods
devops.environments: dev, staging, prod with separate AKS namespaces and MongoDB Atlas clusters

reasoner_reviews.product: The decomposition into ASR, translation, and formatting services correctly addresses the scaling bottleneck — ASR is GPU-bound while translation is CPU-bound, and they have fundamentally different scaling curves. Round 1 left the fan-out coordination pattern underspecified: when a job fans out to 8 language pods simultaneously, how does the orchestrator detect completion of all language tracks before triggering formatting? This must be explicit.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Azure Service Bus topic subscriptions per language pod is the correct fan-out pattern. The orchestrator should track expected vs received translation_pairs per subtitle_job and trigger formatting only when all language tracks are complete. MongoDB's document model suits this aggregate-completion tracking well. Round 1 plan left ISS-001 open: the timing offset correction algorithm for handling ASR segment boundaries that split mid-word was not specified. This is a correctness requirement that affects SRT output quality.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR transcript deletion requires a deletion cascade across MongoDB subtitle_jobs, transcript_segments, and Azure Blob transcript text files. Round 1 plan mentioned GDPR but did not specify the deletion workflow or how deletion_requested_at triggers the 48-hour countdown. ISS-002 covers this gap.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure-only constraint is respected. GPU node pool cap at 4 ASR pods means concurrent job capacity is 4 audio tracks simultaneously; job queue depth monitoring with back-pressure to API is required. GPT-4o post-editing adds ~3-5 seconds per subtitle segment batch — acceptable for async jobs but must be bounded by timeout.

backend.service_design: Five services in separate AKS namespaces: api-gateway (FastAPI, job submission and status), asr-service (FastAPI + faster-whisper GPU pods), translation-service (FastAPI + per-language OPUS-MT worker pods + GPT-4o post-edit), formatting-service (FastAPI, SRT/VTT/TTML assembly and timing correction), delivery-service (FastAPI, Azure Blob and S3 push with signed URL generation).
backend.api_patterns: Internal service communication via Azure Service Bus topics. External public API via api-gateway only. Each service exposes a /health and /metrics endpoint scraped by Azure Monitor. api-gateway is the sole public entry point — no direct inter-service HTTP in production.
backend.llm_integration_design: translation-service calls GPT-4o after OPUS-MT translation of each subtitle_job. Segments grouped into batches of 20 for GPT-4o context efficiency. System prompt instructs GPT-4o to review for register consistency and idiomatic accuracy, return corrected array matching input index. Timeout 45s per batch. On timeout or non-200, OPUS-MT output used without post-editing, llm_edited=false.
backend.background_jobs: Azure Service Bus subscription per language worker pod. ASR job messages land on asr-topic; ASR service processes and publishes transcript to translation-topic with language fan-out (one message per target_language_code). Translation service publishes per-language result to formatting-topic. Formatting service assembles complete track and publishes to delivery-topic.
backend.failure_handling: Dead-letter queue per topic with max_delivery_count=3. Poison message handler writes failed job to MongoDB with error detail and sets subtitle_job.status=failed. Circuit breaker on GPT-4o post-edit calls: after 3 consecutive timeouts, bypass post-editing for remainder of job batch and set llm_available=false flag.

data.entities: subtitle_jobs (job_id, caller_id, video_asset_url, target_language_codes[], status ENUM queued/asr_processing/translating/formatting/delivering/complete/failed, created_at, deletion_requested_at, deleted_at). transcript_segments (segment_id, job_id, start_ms INTEGER, end_ms INTEGER, text TEXT, confidence FLOAT, word_timings ARRAY). translation_pairs (pair_id, job_id, source_language, target_language, segment_id, translated_text TEXT, llm_edited BOOLEAN, bleu_proxy FLOAT). language_models (model_id, language_pair, model_name, model_version, loaded_at, avg_bleu_last_100). timing_offsets (offset_id, job_id, segment_id, original_start_ms, corrected_start_ms, offset_reason TEXT). burn_in_profiles (profile_id, font_family, font_size, position ENUM bottom_center/top_center, color_hex, background_opacity). quality_review_flags (flag_id, job_id, language_code, segment_id, flag_type ENUM low_confidence/llm_correction/timing_gap, created_at). delivery_formats (format_id, job_id, language_code, format ENUM srt/vtt/ttml, blob_url TEXT, delivered_at).
data.schema_notes: MongoDB Atlas with separate collections per entity. subtitle_jobs indexed on caller_id and status for queue depth queries. transcript_segments indexed on job_id for bulk retrieval by ASR service. translation_pairs compound index on (job_id, target_language) for fan-out completion tracking.
data.retention_and_deletion: GDPR deletion workflow: deletion_requested_at timestamp set on subtitle_jobs on caller deletion request. Scheduled job checks deletion_requested_at < NOW() - 30 days for auto-deletion AND deletion_requested_at != null for on-demand deletion. Deletion cascade: DELETE transcript_segments WHERE job_id, DELETE translation_pairs WHERE job_id, DELETE quality_review_flags WHERE job_id, DELETE Azure Blob transcript files, SET subtitle_jobs.deleted_at. Deletion must complete within 48 hours of deletion_requested_at.

security.auth_design: Azure AD M2M client credentials. Each streaming platform caller has an Entra ID app registration with client_id/client_secret. api-gateway validates JWT (aud=subtitle-api, tid=tenant) on all requests. Caller identity extracted from JWT sub claim as caller_id for job scoping.
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault CSI driver mounts secrets as pod environment variables: MongoDB Atlas connection string, GPT-4o API key, Azure Blob storage account key. No secrets in Kubernetes ConfigMaps or environment variable literals. Key Vault access via AKS pod managed identity.
security.privacy_controls: No raw transcript text in Application Insights telemetry — log only job_id, language_code, segment_count. Azure Blob transcript files encrypted with customer-managed key (CMK) in Key Vault. transcript_segments.text field encrypted at application layer using Azure Key Vault encrypt operation before MongoDB write.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS Autopilot cluster with node pools: system-pool (Standard_D2s_v5), gpu-pool (Standard_NC4as_T4_v3, max 4 nodes). Each microservice in dedicated namespace: api-gateway, asr-service, translation-service, formatting-service, delivery-service. Ingress via Azure Application Gateway with WAF for api-gateway only.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure Pipelines per service: unit tests (pytest), Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, Helm chart lint and deploy to staging AKS namespace, smoke test, promote to production. Services deploy independently — no shared release train.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor + Application Insights SDK in each FastAPI service. Custom metrics: asr_processing_duration_ms, translation_latency_ms_per_language, llm_post_edit_latency_ms, queue_depth_per_topic, gdpr_deletion_lag_hours. Dashboards: Job Pipeline Health, Translation Quality (BLEU proxy distribution), GDPR Compliance (deletion_lag alert if > 24 hours).
devops.devops_risks: GPU node pool scaling latency: AKS Autopilot provisions new T4 nodes in 3-5 minutes; if ASR queue depth spikes above 4 jobs simultaneously, incoming jobs wait. Mitigation: pre-warm 1 node during business hours via scheduled HPA min replica.

ISS-001.title: ASR segment boundary timing correction algorithm unspecified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described timing_offsets table but did not specify how the formatting service detects and corrects mid-word segment splits from Whisper output, which produces subtitle flicker and readability issues in SRT output.

history.note: Identified in round 1 audit — formatting service correctness gap

ISS-002.title: GDPR transcript deletion cascade workflow not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan noted GDPR retention policy but did not specify the deletion trigger mechanism, deletion_requested_at tracking, 48-hour SLA enforcement, or Azure Blob file deletion coordination with MongoDB document deletion.

history.note: Identified in round 1 audit — compliance gap

focus_issues.title: ASR segment boundary timing correction algorithm unspecified
focus_issues.detail: Formatting service must detect mid-word Whisper segment splits and apply minimum display duration rules before SRT/VTT assembly.

focus_issues.title: GDPR transcript deletion cascade workflow not specified
focus_issues.detail: 48-hour GDPR erasure SLA requires explicit deletion_requested_at trigger, cascade across MongoDB and Azure Blob, and monitoring of deletion_lag_hours metric.

latest_recommendations: Specify timing correction algorithm: merge Whisper segments where gap < 250ms AND combined duration < 7 seconds; enforce minimum display duration of 1.2 seconds per segment

Define GDPR deletion workflow: deletion_requested_at set by DELETE /v1/jobs/{id} API call; scheduled Azure Function checks hourly; cascade deletes MongoDB collections then Azure Blob; deletion_lag metric alert at 24 hours
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Multi-Language Subtitle Generation System — Round 1 Architecture

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved after round 1

previous_audits.summary: Solid microservices decomposition with correct fan-out pattern via Azure Service Bus, but two high-severity correctness and compliance gaps must be resolved.
strengths: Azure Service Bus topic-per-language fan-out correctly models independent language scaling
MongoDB document schema well-suited for variable-length transcript segments with per-segment confidence
GPT-4o post-editing with OPUS-MT fallback is a pragmatic quality improvement approach
concerns: Timing correction for mid-word ASR boundaries not specified — affects SRT output correctness
GDPR deletion cascade not specified — compliance risk for transcript data containing identifiable voices
blocking_issues: ISS-001: Formatting service has no specified algorithm for detecting and correcting Whisper mid-word segment boundaries
ISS-002: No deletion trigger, cascade specification, or 48-hour SLA monitoring for GDPR transcript erasure
recommendations: Define segment boundary correction: merge segments where inter-segment gap < 250ms and merged duration < max_display_duration (7s); enforce minimum display duration 1.2s per subtitle event
Specify GDPR deletion: DELETE /v1/jobs/{id} sets deletion_requested_at; hourly Azure Function executes cascade; Application Insights alert if deletion_lag_hours > 24

issue_updates.title: ASR segment boundary timing correction unspecified
issue_updates.detail: formatting-service plan describes SRT assembly but no algorithm for handling Whisper output with <100ms inter-segment gaps that produce subtitle flicker

issue_updates.title: GDPR deletion cascade unspecified
issue_updates.detail: privacy_retention_policy mentions 30-day transcript deletion but no trigger mechanism, cascade order, or Azure Blob file deletion is specified

previous_plan.title: Multi-Language Subtitle Generation System — Round 1 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Microservices decomposition of monolithic subtitle pipeline into independently deployable ASR, translation, formatting, and delivery services on AKS. Azure Service Bus topics fan-out translation to per-language pods. MongoDB stores job and segment metadata.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: api-gateway (FastAPI) accepts job submissions. Azure Service Bus fans ASR output to N language translation pods. Formatting service assembles SRT/VTT. Delivery pushes to Azure Blob.
previous_plan.data_model: subtitle_jobs, transcript_segments, translation_pairs, timing_offsets — present but timing correction algorithm unspecified. GDPR deletion_requested_at field absent.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD M2M auth, Key Vault for secrets. GDPR retention noted but deletion cascade not specified.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 (Whisper segment boundary timing correction algorithm) and ISS-002 (GDPR deletion cascade with 48-hour SLA). For ISS-001, the formatting service algorithm merges segments where inter-segment gap < 250ms AND combined duration <= 7 seconds, then enforces minimum display duration of 1.2 seconds by extending end_ms of segments shorter than 1.2s (capped at next segment start minus 50ms gap). For ISS-002, DELETE /v1/jobs/{id} sets deletion_requested_at; an hourly Azure Function executes the cascade; Application Insights custom metric deletion_lag_hours triggers alert at 24 hours. The Azure Service Bus fan-out architecture and MongoDB schema carry forward from round 1.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Whisper segment boundary correction algorithm in formatting-service: (1) merge adjacent transcript_segments where inter-segment gap_ms < 250 AND merged duration_ms <= 7000; (2) enforce minimum display duration — if segment duration_ms < 1200, extend end_ms to start_ms + 1200 capped at next_segment.start_ms - 50; (3) log each correction to timing_offsets table with offset_reason (gap_merge or min_duration_extend). Added to data_model, workflows, and system_components sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SRT and VTT output will not contain subtitle flicker from sub-250ms gaps or illegible flash-duration segments; timing_offsets audit trail enables quality review of correction decisions.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified GDPR deletion workflow: DELETE /v1/jobs/{id} API endpoint sets subtitle_jobs.deletion_requested_at = NOW(); hourly Azure Function (deletion-sweeper) queries subtitle_jobs WHERE deletion_requested_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL; executes cascade in order: (1) delete Azure Blob transcript files, (2) deleteMany transcript_segments WHERE job_id, (3) deleteMany translation_pairs WHERE job_id, (4) deleteMany quality_review_flags WHERE job_id, (5) set subtitle_jobs.deleted_at = NOW(). Application Insights custom metric gdpr_deletion_lag_hours = (NOW() - deletion_requested_at) for all pending deletions. Alert fires if lag > 24 hours.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure met within 48-hour window; deletion_lag_hours metric provides continuous compliance monitoring; audit trail via subtitle_jobs.deleted_at timestamp.

target_output.title: Multi-Language Subtitle Generation System — Round 2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python FastAPI microservices system on AKS decomposing subtitle generation into independent ASR (faster-whisper GPU pods), machine translation (Helsinki-NLP OPUS-MT + GPT-4o post-editing), formatting (SRT/VTT/TTML assembly with Whisper boundary correction), and delivery (Azure Blob and S3) services. Azure Service Bus topics fan-out translation jobs to per-language pods. MongoDB 7 Atlas stores job and segment metadata. GDPR transcript deletion executes via hourly Azure Function cascade with 48-hour SLA. All services authenticate via Azure AD M2M tokens.
target_output.architecture_overview: api-gateway (FastAPI, AKS namespace api-gateway) receives POST /v1/jobs from authenticated streaming platform callers via Azure Application Gateway with WAF. Job document created in MongoDB subtitle_jobs collection, video asset URL resolved from Azure Blob or S3. Message published to asr-topic on Azure Service Bus. asr-service (FastAPI + faster-whisper large-v3, GPU node pool Standard_NC4as_T4_v3, max 4 pods) downloads video, extracts audio, runs transcription, writes transcript_segments to MongoDB, publishes to translation-topic with one message per target_language_code. translation-service (FastAPI, per-language worker pod running Helsinki-NLP OPUS-MT model) translates segments, calls GPT-4o post-edit API in batches of 20, writes translation_pairs to MongoDB, publishes to formatting-topic. formatting-service (FastAPI) applies Whisper boundary correction algorithm, assembles SRT/VTT/TTML files, writes to Azure Blob, publishes to delivery-topic. delivery-service (FastAPI) pushes completed subtitle files to caller's configured delivery destination and updates delivery_formats in MongoDB.
target_output.technology_stack: ASR: faster-whisper large-v3 (CTranslate2 optimized for T4 GPU). MT: Helsinki-NLP OPUS-MT transformer models per language pair via Hugging Face Transformers 4.41. LLM post-editing: GPT-4o via Azure OpenAI (same Azure tenant, lower latency). Orchestration: Azure Service Bus Standard tier, topics with per-language subscriptions. API: FastAPI 0.111, Pydantic v2. Data: MongoDB 7 Atlas on Azure (M30 replica set), Azure Blob Storage with CMK encryption. Compute: AKS Autopilot, GPU node pool Standard_NC4as_T4_v3, system node pool Standard_D4s_v5. Subtitle formatting: pysrt, webvtt-py, lxml for TTML. Auth: Azure AD Entra ID M2M, JWT validation via python-jose. CI/CD: Azure Pipelines per service, Azure Container Registry. Observability: Application Insights SDK, Azure Monitor Workbooks.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Job Submission API: POST /v1/jobs with video_url, target_language_codes[], delivery_config; returns job_id for polling. ASR Transcription: faster-whisper processes audio track, outputs word-level timings, writes transcript_segments. MT Fan-out: per-language translation pods consume language-specific Service Bus subscriptions; OPUS-MT translate, GPT-4o post-edit in batches of 20 with timeout 45s. Subtitle Boundary Correction: formatting-service merges sub-250ms gaps, enforces 1.2s minimum display duration, logs corrections to timing_offsets. Multi-Format Export: pysrt for SRT, webvtt-py for VTT, lxml template for TTML; all formats generated per language per job. Delivery: delivery-service pushes files to Azure Blob or S3, sends webhook to caller, generates signed URLs. GDPR Deletion: DELETE /v1/jobs/{id} triggers deletion_requested_at; hourly Azure Function cascades deletion across MongoDB and Blob.
target_output.system_components: Azure Application Gateway + WAF (api-gateway ingress), api-gateway pods (FastAPI, Azure AD JWT validation, job routing), Azure Service Bus Standard (asr-topic, translation-topic with per-language subscriptions, formatting-topic, delivery-topic, dead-letter queues), asr-service pods (faster-whisper GPU, max 4), translation-service pods (per-language OPUS-MT + GPT-4o post-edit, CPU-only), formatting-service pods (boundary correction + SRT/VTT/TTML assembly), delivery-service pods (Blob/S3 push + webhook), MongoDB 7 Atlas M30 (subtitle_jobs, transcript_segments, translation_pairs, timing_offsets, quality_review_flags, delivery_formats), Azure Blob Storage (video inputs, subtitle output files, transcript text blobs), Azure Key Vault (secrets via CSI driver), Azure Function deletion-sweeper (hourly GDPR cascade), Application Insights (telemetry + custom metrics).
target_output.workflows: Subtitle Job Submission and Processing: Caller sends POST /v1/jobs {video_url, target_language_codes:['es','fr'], delivery_config:{method:'blob',container_url:'...'}}. api-gateway validates Azure AD JWT (aud=subtitle-api), creates subtitle_jobs doc {status:'queued', deletion_requested_at:null}, publishes to asr-topic. asr-service pod picks up message, downloads audio stream from video_url, runs faster-whisper large-v3 with word_timestamps=True, writes transcript_segments (segment_id, start_ms, end_ms, text, confidence, word_timings) to MongoDB, publishes two messages to translation-topic: {job_id, target_language:'es'} and {job_id, target_language:'fr'}. ES translation pod and FR translation pod pick up messages concurrently. Each pod loads OPUS-MT model (cached in pod memory after first use), translates segments, groups into batches of 20 for GPT-4o post-edit call (system prompt: 'Review subtitle translation for register consistency and idiom accuracy; return corrected array same length as input'), writes translation_pairs with llm_edited flag. Publishes to formatting-topic on completion. formatting-service waits for all target_language translations (polls subtitle_jobs aggregate for translation_pairs count == num_targets * num_segments). Applies boundary correction: merges pairs with gap_ms < 250 AND merged_duration_ms <= 7000; extends segments with duration_ms < 1200 to 1200ms capped at next segment start - 50ms; writes each correction to timing_offsets. Assembles SRT (pysrt), VTT (webvtt-py), TTML (lxml). Uploads files to Azure Blob under /jobs/{job_id}/{language}/{format}.{ext}. Publishes to delivery-topic. delivery-service executes caller delivery_config, sends webhook POST to caller callback URL, writes delivery_formats rows, sets subtitle_jobs.status=complete. GDPR Deletion: Caller sends DELETE /v1/jobs/{id}. api-gateway sets subtitle_jobs.deletion_requested_at = NOW(), returns 202 Accepted. Hourly Azure Function deletion-sweeper queries subtitle_jobs WHERE deletion_requested_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL. For each: (1) delete Azure Blob files under /jobs/{job_id}/, (2) db.transcript_segments.deleteMany({job_id}), (3) db.translation_pairs.deleteMany({job_id}), (4) db.quality_review_flags.deleteMany({job_id}), (5) db.timing_offsets.deleteMany({job_id}), (6) subtitle_jobs.deleted_at = NOW(). Application Insights tracks deletion_lag_hours = (NOW() - deletion_requested_at) per pending deletion.
target_output.data_model: subtitle_jobs (job_id UUID, caller_id TEXT, video_asset_url TEXT, target_language_codes TEXT[], status TEXT ENUM queued/asr_processing/translating/formatting/delivering/complete/failed, created_at ISODate, deletion_requested_at ISODate nullable, deleted_at ISODate nullable). transcript_segments (segment_id UUID, job_id UUID, start_ms INTEGER, end_ms INTEGER, text TEXT, confidence FLOAT, word_timings ARRAY of {word, start_ms, end_ms}). translation_pairs (pair_id UUID, job_id UUID, source_language TEXT, target_language TEXT, segment_id UUID, translated_text TEXT, llm_edited BOOLEAN, bleu_proxy FLOAT). language_models (model_id UUID, language_pair TEXT, model_name TEXT, model_version TEXT, loaded_at ISODate, avg_bleu_last_100 FLOAT). timing_offsets (offset_id UUID, job_id UUID, segment_id UUID, original_start_ms INTEGER, original_end_ms INTEGER, corrected_start_ms INTEGER, corrected_end_ms INTEGER, offset_reason TEXT ENUM gap_merge/min_duration_extend). burn_in_profiles (profile_id UUID, font_family TEXT, font_size INTEGER, position TEXT, color_hex TEXT, background_opacity FLOAT). quality_review_flags (flag_id UUID, job_id UUID, language_code TEXT, segment_id UUID, flag_type TEXT ENUM low_confidence/llm_correction/timing_gap). delivery_formats (format_id UUID, job_id UUID, language_code TEXT, format TEXT ENUM srt/vtt/ttml, blob_url TEXT, delivered_at ISODate).
target_output.api_design: REST API via api-gateway with OpenAPI 3.0 at /v1. POST /v1/jobs {video_url, target_language_codes[], delivery_config:{method, destination_url, webhook_url}} -> {job_id, status:'queued', estimated_completion_iso}. GET /v1/jobs/{id} -> {job_id, status, progress:{asr_complete, translations_complete_by_language:{es:true,fr:false}, formatting_complete, delivered}, quality_flags_count}. GET /v1/jobs/{id}/subtitles -> [{language_code, format, download_url (signed 1h), size_bytes}]. DELETE /v1/jobs/{id} -> 202 Accepted, {deletion_scheduled_at, deadline_iso}. GET /v1/jobs/{id}/quality-flags -> [{segment_id, language, flag_type, translated_text, suggested_correction}]. All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer {Azure AD JWT}. Rate limit: 100 job submissions per hour per caller_id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD M2M: each caller has Entra ID app registration; api-gateway validates JWT with audience=subtitle-api and tenant check. Org-scoped: subtitle_jobs filtered by caller_id from JWT sub claim on all read/delete endpoints. AKS network policies: asr-service, translation-service, formatting-service, delivery-service pods can only receive traffic from Azure Service Bus (egress only); no direct inter-pod HTTP; api-gateway pods allow ingress from Application Gateway only. Key Vault CSI driver mounts secrets as mounted file volumes in each pod — no secrets in environment variables or ConfigMaps. GDPR Article 17: deletion_requested_at set immediately on DELETE /v1/jobs/{id}; deletion-sweeper Azure Function runs hourly with gdpr_deletion_lag_hours custom metric; Application Insights alert rule fires if lag_hours > 24. Transcript text in MongoDB Atlas encrypted with CMK in Key Vault; Azure Blob subtitle files encrypted with CMK. No raw transcript text in Application Insights telemetry — log job_id, language_code, segment_count only.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS Autopilot with two node pools: system-pool (Standard_D4s_v5), gpu-pool (Standard_NC4as_T4_v3, min 1 pre-warmed during business hours, max 4). Each service deployed via independent Azure Pipeline: push to Azure Container Registry -> Helm chart update in service-specific chart repo -> helm upgrade --atomic to staging namespace -> smoke test (POST /v1/jobs with test fixture) -> manual approval gate -> helm upgrade to production namespace. Horizontal Pod Autoscaler: asr-service scales on Azure Service Bus queue_depth_asr_topic (target 2 messages per pod), translation-service scales per language pod on per-subscription queue depth. Rollback: helm rollback to previous chart revision. Secrets rotated via Key Vault versioning — new secret version triggers pod restart via Key Vault CSI driver annotation.
target_output.observability: Application Insights SDK (opencensus-azure) in each FastAPI service. Custom metrics: asr_processing_duration_ms (per job), translation_latency_ms_per_language (per language_code), llm_post_edit_latency_ms, llm_post_edit_timeout_count (circuit breaker signal), queue_depth_asr_topic, queue_depth_translation_per_language, formatting_boundary_corrections_per_job, gdpr_deletion_lag_hours (critical compliance metric). Azure Monitor Workbooks: Job Pipeline Health (status distribution, avg end-to-end duration by language count), Translation Quality (avg bleu_proxy by language_pair, llm_edited rate), GDPR Compliance (pending deletions, deletion_lag_hours histogram). Alerts: queue_depth_asr_topic > 8 (capacity warning), gdpr_deletion_lag_hours > 24 (compliance critical), llm_post_edit_timeout_count > 10/hour (GPT-4o degradation warning).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Standard_NC4as_T4_v3 spot node: ~$0.11/hr; 45-minute ASR job = $0.08 per hour of content. 500 hours of content per month = ~$40 GPU cost. OPUS-MT translation is CPU-only — translation pods on system node pool Standard_D4s_v5 ~$0.19/hr; 4 concurrent translation pods = ~$55/month. GPT-4o post-editing: ~$0.006 per 20-segment batch; 500 hours content avg 900 segments/hr = 22,500 batches = ~$135/month. MongoDB Atlas M30: ~$190/month. Azure Blob: ~$25/month for subtitle file storage at 180-day retention. Total estimated: ~$450/month for 500 hours/month throughput. Scale ceiling: 4 GPU pods support 240 ASR-hours per day; translation service scales horizontally per language with no GPU constraint.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): English ASR with faster-whisper, Spanish and French OPUS-MT + GPT-4o post-edit, SRT and VTT export with Whisper boundary correction (ISS-001 resolved), Azure Blob delivery, GDPR deletion workflow with hourly sweeper and deletion_lag_hours metric (ISS-002 resolved), POST/GET/DELETE /v1/jobs API, Azure AD M2M auth, AKS deployment with GPU node pool, Application Insights pipeline health dashboard. Phase 2 (5 weeks): TTML export, 6 additional language pairs (German, Italian, Portuguese, Japanese, Korean, Arabic), per-language quality_review_flags API, S3 delivery option, signed URL download endpoint, burn_in_profile support for formatting service. Phase 3 (6 weeks): 20-language expansion, BLEU-proxy quality scoring dashboard, LLM quality scoring endpoint, speaker diarization integration with ASR service, real-time caption streaming research spike.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python ruff + mypy strict enforced in Azure Pipelines PR stage. pytest with 85% coverage for api-gateway, asr-service, and formatting-service. Service Bus consumer idempotency: each worker checks MongoDB for existing processing record keyed on (job_id, service_stage) before processing to handle duplicate deliveries. GPT-4o calls must use response_format={type:'json_object'} with schema, timeout=45s, max_retries=2; on failure set llm_edited=false and proceed with OPUS-MT output. Never write raw transcript_segment.text to Application Insights telemetry — emit only job_id, segment_count, and confidence distribution. GDPR deletion-sweeper must be idempotent: check deleted_at before executing cascade to handle re-runs.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPU node pool scale-out latency (3-5 min for new T4 node) causes ASR queue depth spike on simultaneous job bursts. Mitigation: pre-warm 1 node during peak hours (07:00-22:00 UTC) via scheduled HPA minimum replica; queue depth alert at depth > 8. Risk: Azure Service Bus message ordering for per-language fan-out — translation pods may complete out of order. Mitigation: formatting-service uses aggregate completion check (count translation_pairs per language == expected segment count) rather than relying on message ordering. Tradeoff: OPUS-MT per-language model loaded into pod memory (~1.5 GB per model) vs on-demand loading — accepted for lower translation latency at cost of higher baseline memory per pod. Tradeoff: hourly deletion-sweeper vs event-driven deletion — hourly sweep chosen for operational simplicity; 48-hour SLA has 47-hour headroom after first sweep.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Azure Service Bus topic subscriptions chosen over Kafka for inter-service fan-out — Azure-native and lower operational overhead on AKS. MongoDB Atlas chosen over CosmosDB — better BLEU score analytics query patterns with aggregation pipeline. faster-whisper CTranslate2 chosen over original Whisper for 4x inference speedup on T4 GPU. Hourly Azure Function deletion-sweeper chosen over event-driven for operational simplicity given 48-hour GDPR window provides ample headroom.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolving Whisper segment boundary correction algorithm and GDPR deletion cascade for AI subtitle generation microservices on AKS.

project_goal.value: Modernize nightly batch ETL for streaming service content performance reporting by migrating to near-real-time ingestion of play events for same-day audience analytics
project_goal.rationale: Nightly batch delivers reports at 06:00 the following day; same-day analytics enable content programming decisions during live premiere windows

target_users.value: Content programming analysts, rights acquisition team, and ad sales planners within the streaming service
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of audience metrics dashboards for content and ad inventory decisions

project_class.rationale: Core artifact is a streaming ingestion and transformation pipeline; no user-facing product UI

capabilities.rationale: Kafka ingestion, Spark transformations, ClickHouse analytical DB, Lambda reconciliation jobs, Grafana dashboards

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source CDN log ingestion, schema validation and enrichment, hourly Spark aggregation, late-arriving event backfill, anomaly detection

risk_level.rationale: Internal analytics data; GDPR exposure for EU viewer play events; no external API surface

data_sensitivity.rationale: Play event data contains viewer_id and IP address; internal proprietary data with GDPR implications for EU viewers

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline and dashboards accessible only within corporate VPC; no external endpoints

access_model.value: IAM roles for AWS service access; Grafana dashboards behind corporate SSO (Okta)
access_model.rationale: All pipeline components use IAM task roles; dashboard access gated by Okta SSO

feature_scope.value: Kafka consumers from CDN edge log streams, schema validation and content catalog enrichment, hourly Spark aggregation to ClickHouse, same-day Grafana dashboards, midnight reconciliation batch for late CDN log backfill, anomaly detection alerts for completion rate deviation
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full ingest-to-dashboard pipeline replacing existing nightly Spark ETL

mvp_scope.value: Kafka ingestion of play_events from primary CDN, hourly Spark aggregation for completion_rate and concurrent_viewers by title, ClickHouse storage, Grafana dashboard for content programming team
mvp_scope.rationale: Primary CDN delivers 80% of traffic; proves near-real-time pipeline before secondary CDN integration

security_baseline.value: IAM roles with least-privilege per EMR and Lambda function, S3 server-side encryption, ClickHouse TLS connections, GDPR viewer_id pseudonymization before ClickHouse write, VPC-only deployment
security_baseline.rationale: Internal pipeline with GDPR pseudonymization required for EU viewer data

frontend_stack.value: None — Grafana dashboards only, no custom frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: Analytics dashboards served by Grafana with ClickHouse data source plugin

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Spark on EMR Serverless; AWS Lambda for reconciliation and alerting jobs
backend_stack.rationale: Existing data engineering team has Spark expertise; EMR Serverless eliminates cluster management

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for analytical queries; S3 data lake with Apache Iceberg tables for raw and enriched event storage; Kafka MSK for streaming ingestion
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse sub-second query performance; Iceberg for time-travel and schema evolution; MSK for managed Kafka

hosting_target.value: AWS EMR Serverless for Spark jobs; Lambda for reconciliation; MSK for Kafka; EC2 for ClickHouse cluster; VPC-only
hosting_target.rationale: EMR Serverless scales to zero between hourly jobs; Lambda for lightweight nightly reconciliation

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw play_events with viewer_id retained 90 days in S3; pseudonymized ClickHouse aggregates retained 2 years; Kafka retention 48 hours
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimization: raw events with viewer_id purged at 90 days; aggregates contain no individual viewer identifiers

future_scope.value: Real-time dashboard with sub-5-minute latency, secondary CDN integration, predictive churn scoring, ad campaign performance overlay
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap after hourly pipeline validated

constraints.value: EMR Serverless hourly job must complete within 40 minutes; ClickHouse cluster budget cap $1,200/month; GDPR pseudonymization must occur before any ClickHouse write
constraints.rationale: 40-minute completion window fits hourly schedule with 20-minute buffer; budget approved; GDPR compliance non-negotiable

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for EMR Serverless job metrics and Lambda duration; Grafana for pipeline health dashboard; PagerDuty for job failure and SLA breach alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: CloudWatch native for AWS services; Grafana reused for operational and analytics visibility

execution_preference.value: Infrastructure as code via Terraform; 2-week sprints; pipeline code in separate repo from dashboard config
execution_preference.rationale: IaC for reproducibility; dashboard config versioned separately for analyst self-service

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU viewer play events: viewer_id must be pseudonymized (SHA-256 HMAC with rotating salt) before ClickHouse write; raw S3 events purged at 90 days; GDPR subject access request exports must be supportable from raw S3 layer within 90-day window
compliance_context.rationale: EU viewer play events qualify as personal data under GDPR; pseudonymization is the chosen compliance strategy

project.goal: Near-real-time play event ingestion and hourly aggregation replacing nightly batch ETL
project.target_users: Content programming analysts, rights acquisition team, ad sales planners
project.timeline: MVP in 8 weeks; secondary CDN and anomaly detection in 12 weeks

backend.framework: PySpark on EMR Serverless, Lambda for reconciliation
backend.streaming: MSK Kafka 3.6 with Kafka Connect for CDN log sources

security.iam: Per-job EMR execution role with least-privilege S3 and ClickHouse access
security.pseudonymization: SHA-256 HMAC with rotating salt applied to viewer_id before Iceberg enriched write
security.encryption: S3 SSE-S3, ClickHouse TLS, MSK TLS in-transit

data.lake: S3 Iceberg tables: play_events_raw, play_events_enriched, aggregates_hourly
data.olap: ClickHouse for engagement_metrics and content_performance_daily materialized views

devops.iac: Terraform for MSK, EMR Serverless, ClickHouse cluster, Lambda, S3 lifecycle
devops.environments: staging (MSK Express broker, EMR Serverless small), production (MSK Provisioned, EMR Serverless large)

constraints.job_sla: EMR Serverless hourly job completes in under 40 minutes
constraints.gdpr: viewer_id pseudonymized before any ClickHouse write

reasoner_reviews.product: The hourly aggregation cadence correctly targets same-day analytics. The midnight reconciliation for late CDN log backfill is essential as CDN edge logs from some regions arrive with up to 3-hour delay. The anomaly detection alert for completion rate deviation is a high-value feature for the programming team during premiere windows.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spark on EMR Serverless is correct for the hourly aggregation job with cold start under 90 seconds. ClickHouse materialized views for audience_metrics and content_performance_daily are the right abstraction for Grafana. Round 1 left ISS-001 open: GDPR pseudonymization salt rotation strategy was described but the Secrets Manager versioning mechanism and salt_version tracking in enriched records were not specified.
reasoner_reviews.security: GDPR pseudonymization via SHA-256 HMAC with rotating salt is appropriate. Round 1 mentioned rotation but did not specify salt storage in Secrets Manager, rotation frequency, or historical compatibility via salt_version. ISS-002 covers the raw S3 event automated deletion gap — it must be a Terraform-managed lifecycle rule, not a manual process.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: EMR Serverless 40-minute SLA is achievable for 50M events per hour at 20 vCPUs. ClickHouse 3-node r6g.large cluster costs approximately $360/month, well within the $1,200 budget. Terraform IaC for lifecycle rules is non-negotiable given audit requirements.

backend.service_design: Four pipeline components: Kafka Connect S3 Sink writing play_events_raw Parquet to S3 Iceberg; Hourly PySpark aggregation job on EMR Serverless; Reconciliation Lambda at midnight backfilling late CDN logs; Anomaly Detector Lambda post-aggregation comparing completion rate to 7-day baseline.
backend.background_jobs: EMR Serverless hourly job triggered by EventBridge Scheduler at xx:05. Reconciliation Lambda triggered at 00:30 UTC. Anomaly detection Lambda triggered by EMR job success EventBridge event. Dead-letter S3 prefix for malformed events that fail schema validation.
backend.failure_handling: EMR Serverless failure publishes CloudWatch emr_job_failed metric triggering PagerDuty P2 alert. Lambda DLQ for reconciliation failures. Kafka Connect transient error retry with exponential backoff.

data.entities: play_events_raw (viewer_id, title_id, content_type, playback_quality, device_type, region_iso2, event_type ENUM play_start/play_complete/buffer_start/error, position_seconds, session_id, timestamp_utc). play_events_enriched (pseudonymized_viewer_id, salt_version INTEGER, title_id, title_name, genre, content_type, license_expiry_date, device_category, region_iso2, event_type, position_seconds, session_id, timestamp_utc). engagement_metrics (title_id, hour_utc DateTime, completion_rate Float32, concurrent_viewers_peak UInt32, avg_watch_duration_seconds UInt32, buffer_rate Float32, error_rate Float32, total_plays UInt32). geographic_rollups (title_id, hour_utc DateTime, region_iso2 FixedString(2), total_plays UInt32, completion_rate Float32). report_runs (run_id, job_type, hour_utc, status, rows_written UInt64, duration_seconds, started_at, completed_at, anomaly_detected UInt8).
data.storage_design: S3 Iceberg tables partitioned year/month/day/hour. ClickHouse partitioned by toYYYYMM(hour_utc). S3 lifecycle rule on play_events_raw/ prefix: Expiration Days=90. play_events_enriched S3 Intelligent-Tiering with 2-year retention.
data.analytics_events: pipeline_job_completed (run_id, job_type, rows_written, duration_seconds), late_event_backfilled (count, hours_late), anomaly_detected (title_id, hour_utc, completion_rate, baseline_rate, deviation_pct).

security.auth_design: EMR Serverless execution role with s3:GetObject on play_events_raw, s3:PutObject on play_events_enriched, secretsmanager:GetSecretValue for pseudonymization-salt ARN. Lambda execution roles scoped to specific S3 prefixes and ClickHouse security group ingress.
security.privacy_controls: viewer_id pseudonymization: SHA-256 HMAC(viewer_id, salt) where salt from Secrets Manager AWSCURRENT version. salt_version INTEGER stored alongside pseudonymized_viewer_id in play_events_enriched. Salt rotation quarterly: new secret version created, AWSCURRENT label moved, AWSPREVIOUS retained 90 days. S3 lifecycle rule (Terraform) deletes play_events_raw partition files at 90 days.
security.secrets_management: Pseudonymization salt in Secrets Manager pseudonymization-salt with versioned labels. No credentials in EMR job configuration or S3 event notifications.

devops.deployment_topology: VPC private subnets only. MSK and ClickHouse in private subnets. EMR Serverless in same VPC. Lambda in VPC for ClickHouse INSERT. S3 VPC endpoint for private S3 routing. Grafana on EC2 t3.medium with Okta SAML SSO.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff, mypy, pytest (PySpark local mode) on PR. Terraform plan on PR, apply on merge. EMR Serverless job deployed as Python wheel to S3 job bucket. Lambda deployed via SAM CLI. ClickHouse schema migrations via clickhouse-migrations tool targeting staging then production.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch custom metrics: emr_job_duration_seconds, emr_rows_written, emr_job_status. Lambda metrics: reconciliation_rows_backfilled, anomaly_alerts_fired. Grafana Pipeline Health dashboard. PagerDuty: emr_job_status=1 or emr_job_duration_seconds > 2400.

ISS-001.title: GDPR pseudonymization salt rotation breaks historical viewer join consistency
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described SHA-256 HMAC pseudonymization with rotating salt but did not specify Secrets Manager versioning, rotation mechanism, or salt_version tracking in enriched records for historical join compatibility.

history.note: First identified in round 1 audit

ISS-002.title: Raw play_events S3 partition deletion not specified as automated lifecycle rule
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 mentioned 90-day retention but did not specify the S3 lifecycle policy Terraform configuration or partition prefix structure enabling per-partition automatic expiry.

history.note: First identified in round 1 audit

focus_issues.title: GDPR pseudonymization salt rotation breaks historical viewer join consistency
focus_issues.detail: salt_version must be stored with each enriched record; Secrets Manager versioned secret required; rotation procedure must preserve historical compatibility.

focus_issues.title: Raw play_events S3 partition deletion not specified as automated lifecycle rule
focus_issues.detail: S3 lifecycle Expiration rule with Filter.Prefix=play_events_raw/ and Days=90 must be Terraform-managed.

latest_recommendations: Store pseudonymization salt in Secrets Manager with versioned labels AWSCURRENT and AWSPREVIOUS; store salt_version INTEGER in every play_events_enriched record; on rotation create new secret version and move label; retain old version 90 days
Configure S3 lifecycle rule via Terraform: aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration with Filter.Prefix=play_events_raw/ and Expiration.Days=90
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Streaming Content Performance Pipeline — Round 1 Architecture

convergence_state.reason: High-severity GDPR salt rotation and medium-severity lifecycle deletion unresolved after round 1

previous_audits.summary: Solid near-real-time Kafka-Iceberg-ClickHouse pipeline design but GDPR pseudonymization salt rotation and automated raw event deletion are underspecified.
strengths: Apache Iceberg time-travel layer enables late CDN log backfill via snapshot queries
ClickHouse materialized views well-suited for concurrent viewer aggregation queries
EMR Serverless eliminates cluster management for intermittent hourly batch jobs
concerns: Salt rotation mechanism not specified — historical viewer session analysis would break across rotation boundary
S3 raw event deletion must be automated, not manual, to ensure GDPR compliance
blocking_issues: ISS-001: Pseudonymization salt rotation lacks Secrets Manager versioning and salt_version tracking
ISS-002: 90-day raw event deletion must be automated S3 lifecycle rule, not manual process
recommendations: Use Secrets Manager versioned secrets for salt; store salt_version in enriched Iceberg records; retain AWSPREVIOUS 90 days on rotation
Terraform aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration with Prefix=play_events_raw/ Expiration Days=90

issue_updates.title: GDPR salt rotation unspecified
issue_updates.detail: Round 1 mentioned rotating salt but no Secrets Manager storage, versioning, or salt_version column defined

issue_updates.title: S3 raw event lifecycle rule missing
issue_updates.detail: Retention policy states 90 days but no Terraform lifecycle configuration or partition prefix structure specified

previous_plan.title: Streaming Content Performance Pipeline — Round 1 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Near-real-time play event ingestion via Kafka MSK, hourly PySpark aggregation on EMR Serverless, ClickHouse for analytical queries, Grafana dashboards. Replaces nightly batch ETL.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: MSK Kafka consumers ingest CDN play events. Kafka Connect S3 Sink writes to Iceberg raw layer. Hourly EMR Serverless job enriches and aggregates to ClickHouse. Midnight Lambda reconciles late CDN logs.
previous_plan.data_model: play_events, content_catalog, engagement_metrics, geographic_rollups present. GDPR pseudonymization mentioned but salt_version tracking absent. S3 lifecycle not specified.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: IAM roles per component, GDPR pseudonymization with SHA-256 HMAC. Salt rotation mentioned but Secrets Manager versioning and salt_version tracking unspecified.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves two GDPR-related gaps. ISS-001: pseudonymization salt stored as versioned Secrets Manager secret with AWSCURRENT and AWSPREVIOUS labels; salt_version INTEGER added to play_events_enriched Iceberg schema populated from the secret version label; on quarterly rotation new secret version created, AWSPREVIOUS retained 90 days for historical record compatibility; analysts must group by salt_version for cross-period viewer continuity. ISS-002: Terraform aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration with Prefix=play_events_raw/ and Expiration Days=90 automates GDPR raw event deletion at partition level. Core Kafka-Iceberg-ClickHouse pipeline architecture carries forward unchanged.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined pseudonymization salt storage and rotation: salt stored in AWS Secrets Manager as pseudonymization-salt with versioned labels AWSCURRENT and AWSPREVIOUS. EMR aggregation job retrieves AWSCURRENT at job start via secretsmanager:GetSecretValue. salt_version INTEGER field added to play_events_enriched Iceberg schema. On quarterly rotation: new secret version created, AWSCURRENT label moved, AWSPREVIOUS retained 90 days. Analysts querying multi-rotation session continuity group by salt_version. Updated data_model, security_and_compliance, workflows, and development_guidelines.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GDPR pseudonymization rotation-safe; historical enriched records remain queryable with salt_version context; salt never in EMR job configuration or S3 object metadata.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Terraform S3 lifecycle rule: aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration resource with id=expire-raw-play-events, Filter.Prefix=play_events_raw/, Expiration.Days=90, Status=Enabled. Partition structure year=/month=/day=/hour= ensures individual object files expire per-partition as they age. Added CloudWatch daily monitoring Lambda emitting gdpr_raw_events_age_days_max metric with alert at 85 days. Updated deployment_and_operations and security_and_compliance.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Raw play_events including viewer_id automatically deleted at GDPR 90-day boundary; Terraform codifies lifecycle rule as version-controlled auditable IaC; 85-day CloudWatch alert provides 5-day compliance warning.

target_output.title: Streaming Content Performance Pipeline — Round 2 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: Near-real-time audience analytics pipeline replacing a nightly batch ETL for a streaming service. Kafka MSK consumers stream CDN play events into S3 Iceberg raw tables partitioned by hour. Hourly PySpark on EMR Serverless enriches events with content catalog metadata, pseudonymizes viewer_id via SHA-256 HMAC with a Secrets Manager versioned salt (salt_version stored per record), and writes engagement aggregates to ClickHouse. Midnight Lambda reconciles late CDN backfill against the Iceberg time-travel layer. Grafana dashboards surface same-day completion rates and concurrent viewer peaks. GDPR pseudonymization salt versioning and automated S3 raw event lifecycle deletion are fully specified in this round.
target_output.architecture_overview: Kafka Connect S3 Sink consumes the MSK play_events_raw topic and writes Parquet files to s3://analytics-lake/play_events_raw/ as an Apache Iceberg table partitioned by year/month/day/hour. EventBridge Scheduler triggers EMR Serverless PySpark job at xx:05 each hour. The job reads new Iceberg partitions, validates schema (drops records missing title_id or timestamp_utc to dead-letter prefix), joins against content_catalog Iceberg snapshot, pseudonymizes viewer_id (SHA-256 HMAC with AWSCURRENT salt from Secrets Manager, writes salt_version INTEGER), and writes play_events_enriched Iceberg partition. PySpark then aggregates completion_rate, concurrent_viewers_peak via session windows, avg_watch_duration_seconds, buffer_rate, and error_rate per title per hour and batch-INSERTs into ClickHouse engagement_metrics and geographic_rollups. Reconciliation Lambda at 00:30 UTC reads Iceberg time-travel snapshots for the past 24 hours, compares row counts to ClickHouse aggregates, and triggers EMR reprocess for divergent hour partitions. Anomaly detector Lambda fires on EMR success events and computes Z-scores against 7-day rolling completion rate baseline. All pipeline components run in a VPC with private subnets; S3 accessed via VPC endpoint. Grafana on EC2 with Okta SAML SSO connects to ClickHouse HTTP interface on port 8123.
target_output.technology_stack: Streaming: MSK Kafka 3.6 Provisioned, Kafka Connect S3 Sink with Iceberg format. Transformation: PySpark 3.5 on EMR Serverless Python 3.12, Iceberg Spark runtime 1.5. Lake: Apache Iceberg 1.5 on S3 with Glue Data Catalog. OLAP: ClickHouse 24.3 on 3x EC2 r6g.large with EBS gp3 1TB per node. Reconciliation and alerting: Python 3.12 Lambda with clickhouse-driver. Orchestration: EventBridge Scheduler. Pseudonymization: Python hmac SHA-256 with Secrets Manager versioned salt. IaC: Terraform 1.8. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: CloudWatch custom metrics, Grafana 10.3 ClickHouse plugin, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: CDN Play Event Ingestion: Kafka Connect streams CDN edge log play events (viewer_id, title_id, event_type, position_seconds, device_type, region_iso2, session_id, timestamp_utc) into Iceberg play_events_raw. Content Catalog Enrichment: PySpark joins title_id to content_catalog snapshot for genre, content_type, license_expiry_date, device_category. GDPR Pseudonymization: viewer_id replaced with HMAC(viewer_id, salt_v{N}); salt_version INTEGER stored per record in play_events_enriched. Hourly Aggregation: completion_rate, concurrent_viewers_peak, avg_watch_duration_seconds, buffer_rate, error_rate per title per hour written to ClickHouse engagement_metrics. Geographic Rollup: completion_rate and total_plays per title per hour per region_iso2. Late CDN Backfill: midnight Lambda reprocesses hours where Iceberg row count diverges from ClickHouse aggregates. Anomaly Detection: post-aggregation Lambda flags titles where completion rate Z-score exceeds 2.5 standard deviations from 7-day baseline.
target_output.system_components: MSK Kafka Provisioned (3 brokers, play_events_raw topic 12 partitions, 48h retention), Kafka Connect S3 Sink on ECS Fargate (writes Iceberg Parquet to S3), S3 analytics-lake bucket (Iceberg: play_events_raw with 90-day lifecycle, play_events_enriched, aggregates_hourly), Glue Data Catalog (Iceberg table registry), EMR Serverless Application (Python 3.12 hourly aggregation job), EventBridge Scheduler (xx:05 hourly trigger), ClickHouse Cluster 3x r6g.large (engagement_metrics, stream_quality_snapshots, geographic_rollups tables; audience_metrics_mv and content_performance_daily materialized views), Secrets Manager (pseudonymization-salt versioned secret), Reconciliation Lambda (00:30 UTC, VPC, clickhouse-driver), Anomaly Detector Lambda (EMR success event trigger), CloudWatch GDPR monitoring Lambda (daily, gdpr_raw_events_age_days_max metric), Grafana EC2 t3.medium (ClickHouse plugin, Okta SSO).
target_output.workflows: Hourly Play Event Aggregation: EventBridge fires at xx:05. EMR Serverless job starts (cold start ~75s). Reads play_events_raw Iceberg partition for preceding hour. Schema validation drops records missing title_id or timestamp_utc to dead-letter S3 prefix. Retrieves pseudonymization salt from Secrets Manager AWSCURRENT version, extracts salt_version from ARN. Applies HMAC(viewer_id, salt). Joins to content_catalog broadcast snapshot. Writes play_events_enriched Iceberg partition with salt_version column. Aggregates completion_rate (play_complete / play_start per session), concurrent_viewers_peak (max active sessions per 1-minute session window), avg_watch_duration_seconds, buffer_rate, error_rate per title per hour. Batch-INSERTs to ClickHouse engagement_metrics and geographic_rollups. Writes report_runs row. Emits CloudWatch metrics. Anomaly Detector Lambda fires on EMR success event: reads 7-day completion_rate history from ClickHouse for titles with total_plays > 5000 in current hour; computes Z-score; if |Z| > 2.5, sends Slack alert to content-analytics-alerts channel. Late Backfill: Reconciliation Lambda at 00:30 UTC queries ClickHouse report_runs for hours in past 24h with rows_written below expected threshold. For divergent hours, reads Iceberg time-travel snapshot at TIMESTAMP AS OF hour+3h, counts delta rows. If delta > 0, triggers EMR Serverless reprocess using MERGE INTO play_events_enriched ON session_id to prevent duplicates.
target_output.data_model: play_events_raw (Iceberg S3, partitioned year/month/day/hour, lifecycle Expiration 90 days): viewer_id TEXT, title_id TEXT, content_type TEXT, playback_quality TEXT, device_type TEXT, region_iso2 TEXT, event_type TEXT, position_seconds INTEGER, session_id TEXT, timestamp_utc TIMESTAMP. play_events_enriched (Iceberg S3, partitioned year/month/day/hour, 2-year retention): pseudonymized_viewer_id TEXT, salt_version INTEGER, title_id TEXT, title_name TEXT, genre TEXT[], content_type TEXT, license_expiry_date DATE, device_category TEXT, region_iso2 TEXT, event_type TEXT, position_seconds INTEGER, session_id TEXT, timestamp_utc TIMESTAMP. engagement_metrics (ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree, partitioned toYYYYMM(hour_utc)): title_id String, hour_utc DateTime, completion_rate Float32, concurrent_viewers_peak UInt32, avg_watch_duration_seconds UInt32, buffer_rate Float32, error_rate Float32, total_plays UInt32. geographic_rollups (ClickHouse): title_id String, hour_utc DateTime, region_iso2 FixedString(2), total_plays UInt32, completion_rate Float32. report_runs (ClickHouse): run_id UUID, job_type String, hour_utc DateTime, status String, rows_written UInt64, duration_seconds UInt16, started_at DateTime, completed_at DateTime, anomaly_detected UInt8. S3 lifecycle (Terraform aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration): rule id=expire-raw-play-events, Filter.Prefix=play_events_raw/, Expiration.Days=90, Status=Enabled.
target_output.api_design: No external API. Internal interfaces: EMR Serverless job triggered by EventBridge Scheduler event payload containing hour_utc and iceberg_table_path. Lambda functions triggered by EventBridge rules. ClickHouse accessed via native TCP port 9000 from EMR and Lambda within VPC. Grafana queries ClickHouse via HTTP port 8123 with read-only service account. Kafka Connect REST API port 8083 for health checks from monitoring Lambda. Dead-letter events published to S3 dead_letter/ prefix with SQS notification for alerting.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IAM: emr-aggregation-role with s3:GetObject (play_events_raw), s3:PutObject (play_events_enriched), secretsmanager:GetSecretValue (pseudonymization-salt ARN only), glue:GetTable (content_catalog). Lambda roles scoped to specific S3 prefixes and ClickHouse security group. No cross-role assumption. GDPR pseudonymization: SHA-256 HMAC(viewer_id, salt) from Secrets Manager AWSCURRENT. salt_version INTEGER written to every play_events_enriched record — enforced by pytest fixture. Quarterly salt rotation: new Secrets Manager version, move AWSCURRENT label, retain AWSPREVIOUS 90 days. Analysts cross-referencing sessions across rotation boundaries group by salt_version. Raw play_events_raw deletion: Terraform aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration Expiration Days=90 on play_events_raw/ prefix. Daily monitoring Lambda emits CloudWatch gdpr_raw_events_age_days_max; alert at 85 days. ClickHouse security group: inbound TCP 9000 and 8123 from EMR SG and Lambda SG only. Grafana: Okta SAML SSO, read-only ClickHouse account.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform modules: msk_cluster, emr_serverless_app, clickhouse_cluster (EC2 ASG + EBS), lambda_reconciliation, lambda_anomaly, lambda_gdpr_monitor, s3_analytics_lake (bucket + lifecycle configuration), grafana_instance, eventbridge_schedules. GitHub Actions PR: terraform plan (staging workspace) + ruff + mypy + pytest (PySpark local mode). Merge to main: terraform apply staging, smoke test (EventBridge trigger with 1000 synthetic play events, verify ClickHouse row within 5 minutes of job completion). Production deploy: manual approval gate, terraform apply production. ClickHouse schema migrations via clickhouse-migrations targeting staging then production. EMR Serverless job: Python wheel deployed to S3 job bucket, application references versioned S3 path. Lambda: SAM CLI package and deploy. Rollback: EMR Serverless revert to previous wheel via S3 path update in EventBridge input transformer.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom metrics (emitted by PySpark job via boto3 put_metric_data): emr_job_duration_seconds, emr_rows_written_enriched, emr_schema_validation_drops, emr_job_status (0=success,1=fail). Lambda: reconciliation_rows_backfilled, anomaly_alerts_fired. Daily Lambda: gdpr_raw_events_age_days_max (scans play_events_raw/ prefix for oldest object). Grafana Pipeline Health dashboard: hourly job duration trend with 2400s SLA line, rows written per hour, dead-letter drop rate, reconciliation backfill volume. Grafana Analytics: Completion Rate by Genre (hourly 7-day), Concurrent Viewer Peak by Title, Geographic Breakdown (region_iso2 heatmap). PagerDuty: emr_job_status=1 (P2), emr_job_duration_seconds > 2400 (P3 SLA warning), anomaly Z-score > 2.5 on premiere title (P2 programming alert), gdpr_raw_events_age_days_max > 85 (P3 compliance warning).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EMR Serverless: ~$0.052/vCPU-hr at 20 vCPUs for 15 min = $0.26/job x 720 hours/month = ~$190/month. ClickHouse 3x r6g.large On-Demand ($0.126/hr x 3) = ~$272/month plus 3TB gp3 EBS ~$90/month. MSK Provisioned 3x kafka.m5.large ~$180/month. Lambda reconciliation and alerting ~$5/month. Total ~$740/month within $1,200 ClickHouse sub-budget (ClickHouse portion $362/month). EMR Serverless auto-scales vCPUs per job up to 200; current 50M events/hour processed at 20 vCPUs in 15 minutes — 4x headroom. ClickHouse scales by adding nodes via Terraform; horizontal sharding on title_id range for tables exceeding 10B rows.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): MSK Kafka topic and Kafka Connect S3 Sink for primary CDN, EMR Serverless hourly job with GDPR pseudonymization including salt_version tracking (ISS-001 resolved), play_events_raw S3 lifecycle Expiration 90 days via Terraform (ISS-002 resolved), play_events_enriched and engagement_metrics ClickHouse write, geographic_rollups aggregation, Grafana completion rate and concurrent viewer dashboards with Okta SSO, PagerDuty job failure and SLA alerts, midnight reconciliation Lambda, Terraform IaC for all components. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Secondary CDN connector, stream_quality_snapshots aggregation (startup latency, rebuffer rate), anomaly detection Lambda with Slack alert, content_performance_daily ClickHouse materialized view, report_runs tracking. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Real-time sub-5-minute latency via ClickHouse Kafka table engine direct ingestion for concurrent viewer gauge, audience segment dashboards, ad campaign performance overlay joining ad_events topic, predictive churn scoring spike.
target_output.development_guidelines: PySpark: ruff + mypy strict, pytest with PySpark local mode, 85% coverage for enrichment and aggregation transforms. Iceberg MERGE INTO for reconciliation re-runs to prevent duplicates — never DELETE+INSERT. EMR Serverless job reads pseudonymization salt once at startup and passes to all tasks — never re-fetch per record due to Secrets Manager API rate limits. salt_version must be written to every play_events_enriched record — pytest fixture asserts salt_version IS NOT NULL on enriched output. Lambda: pytest with mocked boto3 and clickhouse-driver. Terraform workspaces for staging and production; all S3 lifecycle rules version-controlled. No hard-coded AWS account IDs in job code.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EMR Serverless cold start (~75 seconds) may cause CDN log events arriving within first minute of hour to miss the job window. Mitigation: xx:05 schedule provides 5-minute drain window; dead-letter monitoring and reconciliation Lambda catch any late events. Risk: ClickHouse INSERT from EMR during a node failure creates partial write. Mitigation: ReplacingMergeTree engine on engagement_metrics deduplicates on (title_id, hour_utc); reconciliation detects row count divergence and triggers EMR reprocess. Tradeoff: EMR Serverless cold start over persistent cluster — eliminates cluster management at 75-second startup cost; acceptable for hourly cadence. Tradeoff: ClickHouse over Redshift — 180ms p95 completion rate query vs 1.2s p95 on same data volume; chosen for query performance at lower cost.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Apache Iceberg chosen over Hudi for better Spark 3.5 integration and snapshot-based time-travel queries for reconciliation. EMR Serverless chosen over Glue ETL for direct PySpark API access and lower cold start. ClickHouse chosen over Redshift for sub-second analytical query performance on completion rate aggregations. Salt rotation frequency quarterly chosen to balance GDPR pseudonymization freshness with cross-period viewer continuity analytical cost.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolving GDPR pseudonymization salt versioning and automated S3 raw event lifecycle deletion for streaming service content analytics pipeline.

project_goal.value: Native macOS desktop app for podcast producers to record multi-track audio sessions, apply noise reduction, and export to multiple streaming platform formats from a single workflow
project_goal.rationale: Replaces fragmented workflow of separate DAW, noise reduction plugin, and export scripts with an integrated producer tool

target_users.value: Independent podcast producers and small studio teams recording multi-guest remote and in-person sessions on macOS
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in user research across 15 podcast producers

project_class.rationale: Native macOS app requiring CoreAudio device access, AVFoundation, and Metal-accelerated waveform rendering

capabilities.rationale: SwiftUI UI, CoreAudio recording backend, platform export integrations (Spotify, Apple Podcasts, RSS), Xcode Cloud CI and Mac App Store delivery

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-track CoreAudio recording with AVFoundation, noise reduction pipeline, multi-format export, SQLite session storage — moderate complexity for a native macOS app

risk_level.rationale: No PII, no network-facing endpoints, no payment processing; optional iCloud sync with Apple Sign-In is isolated feature

data_sensitivity.rationale: User-owned audio content stored locally or in their own iCloud; app holds no personal data on behalf of users

external_exposure.rationale: Optional cloud sync via Apple Sign-In; all other app functionality is local-only with no network exposure

access_model.value: No authentication required for local recording functionality; Apple Sign-In (optional) for iCloud sync; no user accounts managed by the app
access_model.rationale: Local-first app; auth only for optional cloud sync feature

feature_scope.value: Multi-track recording via CoreAudio with per-track level meters, noise profile capture and reduction via AVAudioEngine, per-track mixing with volume and pan, chapter marker insertion, export to AAC/MP3/Opus with embedded ID3 tags, Mac App Store distribution with Xcode Cloud CI
feature_scope.rationale: Full podcast production workflow from recording to distribution-ready export

mvp_scope.value: 2-track recording (host + single guest), basic noise reduction, MP3 and AAC export with ID3 tags, SQLite session storage, Mac App Store submission
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers 70% of solo and duo podcast format; proves CoreAudio and App Store delivery pipeline

security_baseline.value: App Sandbox enabled, Hardened Runtime for notarization, microphone and audio input entitlements only, no outbound network entitlement for local recording features, optional iCloud entitlement isolated to sync module
security_baseline.rationale: Mac App Store requires App Sandbox; Hardened Runtime required for notarization; minimal entitlements principle

frontend_stack.rationale: SwiftUI for declarative macOS UI; native look and feel; Metal-backed waveform view via Canvas API

backend_stack.value: Swift 5.10 with CoreAudio and AVFoundation for local audio processing
backend_stack.rationale: CoreAudio for low-latency multi-track device access; AVFoundation for noise reduction and export pipeline

data_platform.value: SQLite via GRDB.swift for local session, track, and chapter marker storage
data_platform.rationale: Lightweight local persistence; GRDB.swift provides type-safe Swift API over SQLite without network dependency

hosting_target.value: Mac App Store as primary distribution; direct download DMG via S3 as secondary for users unable to use App Store
hosting_target.rationale: App Store provides notarization, update delivery, and billing; S3 DMG for enterprise and developer users

privacy_retention_policy.value: All audio data stored locally on user device; app does not transmit audio content; iCloud sync stores encrypted audio in user's personal iCloud container
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: App stores no user data on its own servers; privacy policy reflects local-only data handling

future_scope.value: Video podcast recording (camera + audio sync), cloud collaboration for remote co-host recording, AI transcript generation via Whisper, cross-platform iPad support
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap after macOS MVP validated with producers

constraints.value: Must pass Mac App Store review; App Sandbox limits entitlements; minimum macOS 14 Sonoma for AVFoundation noise reduction APIs; Xcode Cloud CI for builds
constraints.rationale: App Sandbox enforced by App Store; macOS 14 required for AVAudioEngine noise removal node; Xcode Cloud for notarization integration

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting with Swift symbolication; local diagnostic log files in ~/Library/Logs/PodcastStudio/; no remote telemetry without user consent
observability_baseline.rationale: Crash reporting is essential for App Store apps; no analytics without explicit user opt-in per App Store guidelines

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; TestFlight beta for internal testing; App Store review submission monthly
execution_preference.rationale: TestFlight enables rapid beta feedback from pilot producers; monthly App Store submission aligns with review cadence

project.goal: Integrated macOS app for multi-track podcast recording, noise reduction, and multi-format export
project.target_users: Independent podcast producers and small studio teams
project.timeline: MVP (2-track, MP3/AAC, App Store submission) in 10 weeks; full feature set in 16 weeks

frontend.waveform: Metal Canvas for real-time waveform rendering
frontend.state: @Observable (Swift 5.9 Observation framework) for session and track state

backend.framework: CoreAudio for multi-track input device management
backend.audio_engine: AVAudioEngine for signal graph: input nodes, noise removal node (AVAudioUnitEffect wrapping custom AU), mixer node, file output node
backend.export: AVAssetExportSession for AAC; lame Swift wrapper for MP3; libopus via Swift Package Manager for Opus

security.icloud: NSUbiquitousContainers entitlement isolated to optional sync module

data.db: SQLite via GRDB.swift for recording_sessions, audio_tracks, chapter_markers, noise_profiles, export_presets, podcast_episodes

devops.ci: Xcode Cloud workflow triggered on PR and tag push
devops.distribution: Mac App Store (primary), S3 DMG (secondary via notarytool)
devops.crash_reporting: Sentry Swift SDK with dsym upload in Xcode Cloud post-action

constraints.entitlements: App Sandbox, Hardened Runtime, audio-input only
constraints.store: Must pass Mac App Store review

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope of 2-track recording with MP3/AAC export is well-scoped for initial App Store submission. Chapter marker support is a differentiating feature that podcast producers expect; it must be integrated at the recording phase, not only at export. The noise reduction workflow using AVAudioEngine noise removal node is the correct native approach for macOS 14.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: CoreAudio for device management and AVAudioEngine for the signal processing graph is the standard macOS audio architecture. The GRDB.swift SQLite layer is appropriate for session metadata. Round 3 must address ISS-001: the export pipeline for MP3 uses a third-party Swift wrapper around libmp3lame, but the App Sandbox restricts outbound entitlements and dynamic library loading — the libmp3lame build must be verified as a static library embedded in the app bundle, not a dynamically loaded library that would require com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation.
reasoner_reviews.security: App Sandbox + Hardened Runtime is the correct security posture for Mac App Store. Round 3 must verify ISS-001: com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation would allow loading of unsigned code, violating Hardened Runtime. libopus and libmp3lame must both be statically linked Swift packages that compile from source as part of the app target. ISS-002: the plan describes per-track WAV recording to ~/Library/Application Support/ but does not specify how the app requests user permission to access files outside the sandbox container via NSOpenPanel or security-scoped bookmarks — needed for producers who want to save recordings to external drives.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: macOS 14 minimum is correct for AVAudioEngine noise removal node (AVAudioUnitEffect with kAudioUnitSubType_SpeechChannel or custom noise removal AU). App Sandbox container at ~/Library/Containers/com.studio.podcaststudio/ provides the default file access scope. Security-scoped bookmarks are required for persistent access to user-selected directories outside the sandbox.

frontend.app_structure: Single-window macOS app with NavigationSplitView: sidebar (session library), detail (recording studio view with per-track waveform, level meters, transport controls), inspector panel (track settings, chapter markers). RecordingStudioView uses Metal Canvas for real-time waveform rendering via WaveformRenderer drawing into GraphicsContext at 60fps.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Session Library (browse recording_sessions, create new session), Recording Studio (per-track input selector, level meters, recording transport, waveform playback), Noise Reduction Panel (capture noise profile, preview reduction strength, apply to track), Export Sheet (format selection AAC/MP3/Opus, bitrate, embedded ID3 metadata, chapter markers toggle), Episode Manager (podcast_episodes list with RSS feed URL, export history).
frontend.state_management: @Observable SessionState for active session (tracks, recording status, playhead position). @Observable TrackState per audio track. @AppStorage for global preferences (default sample rate, default export format). GRDB.swift ValueObservation for real-time UI updates on SQLite changes.
frontend.performance_notes: WaveformRenderer pre-computes waveform peak arrays from WAV files in a background Task at 1 sample per 100 frames, caching in memory. Metal Canvas rendering dispatched on main actor. CoreAudio tap callback on high-priority real-time thread — never touch SwiftUI state from tap; use ring buffer + Timer at 20ms to drain to @Observable state.

backend.service_design: AudioEngine actor manages AVAudioEngine lifecycle: installs per-track input node, noise removal node (AVAudioUnitEffect), mixer, and file output node. RecordingController coordinates CoreAudio device enumeration and input tap installation. ExportPipeline handles format-specific export: AVAssetExportSession for AAC, swift-lame SPM package for MP3, swift-opus SPM package for Opus.
backend.api_patterns: Swift actor concurrency model: AudioEngine is a global actor for CoreAudio operations. All CoreAudio callbacks route to the AudioEngine actor via AsyncStream. ExportPipeline operations are async functions returning export progress via AsyncStream.
backend.failure_handling: CoreAudio device disconnection during recording: AVAudioEngine route change notification triggers graceful stop, finalizes partial WAV file, updates recording_session status to interrupted.
