Export failures: ExportPipeline throws typed errors (ExportError.encoderFailed, ExportError.insufficientDisk) caught in ExportSheet view for user-facing error display.

data.entities: recording_sessions (id INTEGER PK, title TEXT, created_at TEXT, duration_seconds REAL, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('recording','paused','complete','interrupted')), notes TEXT). audio_tracks (id INTEGER PK, session_id INTEGER FK, track_index INTEGER, input_device_uid TEXT, label TEXT, wav_path TEXT, sample_rate INTEGER, channel_count INTEGER). chapter_markers (id INTEGER PK, session_id INTEGER FK, position_seconds REAL, title TEXT, artwork_path TEXT). noise_profiles (id INTEGER PK, track_id INTEGER FK, profile_path TEXT, captured_at TEXT). export_presets (id INTEGER PK, name TEXT, format TEXT CHECK(format IN ('aac','mp3','opus')), bitrate_kbps INTEGER, sample_rate INTEGER, stereo_downmix BOOLEAN). podcast_episodes (id INTEGER PK, session_id INTEGER FK, title TEXT, description TEXT, rss_episode_url TEXT, published_at TEXT). waveform_snapshots (id INTEGER PK, track_id INTEGER FK, peaks_json TEXT, generated_at TEXT).
data.schema_notes: GRDB.swift migrations for schema versioning on app launch. WAV files stored in ~/Library/Containers/com.studio.podcaststudio/Data/Library/Application Support/Recordings/ (sandbox container). Security-scoped bookmark stored as Data blob in recording_sessions.external_bookmark for producer-selected external drive paths.
data.migration_notes: GRDB.swift DatabaseMigrator with numbered migrations. On first launch, migrator runs all pending migrations. App version stored in UserDefaults; schema version tracked in user_version PRAGMA.

security.auth_design: No authentication for local features. Optional Apple Sign-In for iCloud sync isolated in CloudSyncModule. App Sandbox entitlements: com.apple.security.device.audio-input (required), com.apple.security.files.user-selected.read-write (for NSOpenPanel file access), com.apple.security.temporary-exception.files.absolute-path.read-write limited to sandbox container path, iCloud NSUbiquitousContainers (optional sync only).
security.secrets_management: No secrets or credentials. Sentry DSN stored in Info.plist (not sensitive). Apple Sign-In token stored in Keychain when iCloud sync is enabled.
security.abuse_prevention: Hardened Runtime enforced: no com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation. libmp3lame and libopus embedded as Swift Package Manager packages that compile from source as part of app target — no dynamically loaded unsigned libraries. This satisfies ISS-001.

ISS-001.title: libmp3lame dynamic library loading violates Hardened Runtime
ISS-001.detail: Round 2 plan specified MP3 export via a Swift wrapper around libmp3lame but used a pre-built dynamic library loaded at runtime. This requires com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation which violates Hardened Runtime and blocks App Store submission.

Round 2 plan described dynamic library approach — still non-compliant

ISS-002.title: Security-scoped bookmarks not specified for external drive recording paths
ISS-002.detail: Producers commonly save recordings to external SSDs. App Sandbox blocks persistent access to paths outside the sandbox container. Security-scoped bookmarks via NSOpenPanel and startAccessingSecurityScopedResource must be specified.

previous_plan.title: macOS Podcast Studio — Round 2 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Native SwiftUI macOS app for multi-track podcast recording using CoreAudio and AVAudioEngine with noise reduction, multi-format export, and SQLite session management. Xcode Cloud CI for App Store delivery.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: SwiftUI NavigationSplitView frontend. AudioEngine actor manages CoreAudio input and AVAudioEngine signal graph. GRDB.swift SQLite persistence. Export via AVAssetExportSession (AAC) and dynamic libmp3lame wrapper — Hardened Runtime violation present.
previous_plan.data_model: recording_sessions, audio_tracks, chapter_markers, noise_profiles, export_presets, podcast_episodes. external_bookmark column absent from recording_sessions.

target_output.thinking_summary: INTENTIONALLY FLAWED round 3: This plan claims to resolve ISS-001 and ISS-002 in the fix_report but actually fails to do so in the plan sections. The security_and_compliance section still mentions disable-library-validation as the approach, and the data_model section still lacks the external_bookmark column. The development_guidelines section contradicts the fix_report by describing the dynamic library approach. This models a common training failure pattern where the architect claims fixes but does not propagate them through all relevant plan sections.

fix_report.expected_outcome: Producers can save recordings to external SSDs with persistent App Sandbox access across app launches.

target_output.title: macOS Podcast Studio — Round 3 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: Native SwiftUI macOS app for multi-track podcast production. CoreAudio enumerates input devices per track; AVAudioEngine signal graph handles noise reduction via the native macOS 14 noise removal node and routes to per-track WAV file output nodes. Export pipeline uses AVAssetExportSession for AAC, swift-lame SPM for MP3, and swift-opus SPM for Opus with embedded ID3 tags and chapter markers. GRDB.swift manages SQLite session, track, chapter marker, and preset storage. Xcode Cloud builds and submits to App Store via TestFlight.
target_output.architecture_overview: SwiftUI NavigationSplitView presents a session library sidebar and a multi-track recording studio detail view with Metal-rendered waveforms per track. A global AudioEngine Swift actor manages the AVAudioEngine instance: it installs one input node per selected CoreAudio device, chains a noise removal AVAudioUnitEffect per track, routes through a MixerNode, and writes per-track isolation to AVAudioFile WAV output nodes. A RecordingController coordinates device enumeration via AudioObjectGetPropertyData and installs buffer taps for real-time level metering. ExportPipeline is an async actor using AVAssetExportSession for AAC, LameEncoder from swift-lame for MP3, and OpusEncoder from swift-opus for Opus — all compiled from source as SPM package dependencies, avoiding any dynamic library loading. GRDB.swift DatabaseQueue handles all SQLite reads and writes on a dedicated serial queue. Sentry Swift SDK captures crashes with dSYM symbolication uploaded during Xcode Cloud post-action.
target_output.technology_stack: UI: Swift 5.10, SwiftUI macOS 14, Metal Canvas API for waveform rendering, Swift Observation framework (@Observable). Audio: CoreAudio (AudioHardware API for device enumeration), AVFoundation AVAudioEngine, AVAudioUnitEffect (noise removal), AVAudioFile, AVAssetExportSession. Export: swift-lame (SPM, compiles libmp3lame from source), swift-opus (SPM, compiles libopus from source), AVAssetExportSession (AAC), ID3TagEditor (SPM) for ID3 tag embedding. Data: GRDB.swift 6.x on SQLite 3. CI/CD: Xcode Cloud, TestFlight, Mac App Store Connect API. Crash reporting: Sentry Swift SDK 8.x with dSYM upload post-action. Distribution: Mac App Store primary, notarized DMG via S3 secondary.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Multi-Track Recording: producer selects CoreAudio input device per track (up to 8 tracks), records isolated WAV files with real-time waveform display and level meters. Noise Profile Capture: producer records 5 seconds of room noise; AudioEngine samples buffer for spectral noise floor; profile stored as noise_profiles row. Noise Reduction: AVAudioUnitEffect with noise removal AU applied per track before file output node during recording. Chapter Marker Insertion: producer taps chapter button during recording or playback; position_seconds computed from AVAudioEngine.lastRenderTime; stored in chapter_markers. Mix and Export: SwiftUI ExportSheet selects format (AAC/MP3/Opus), bitrate, stereo downmix; ExportPipeline runs export and embeds ID3 tags (title, artist, episode number, chapter markers) and writes to user-selected destination. External Drive Support: NSOpenPanel lets producer select recording directory outside sandbox container; security-scoped bookmark stored in recording_sessions for persistent access.
target_output.system_components: SwiftUI App (NavigationSplitView, RecordingStudioView with WaveformRenderer, ExportSheet, NoiseReductionPanel, SessionLibrary), AudioEngine Actor (AVAudioEngine, per-track input+noise removal+file output chain, level meter ring buffer), RecordingController (CoreAudio device enumeration, buffer tap management), ExportPipeline Actor (AAC via AVAssetExportSession, MP3 via swift-lame LameEncoder, Opus via swift-opus OpusEncoder, ID3 embedding via ID3TagEditor), GRDB DatabaseQueue (recording_sessions, audio_tracks, chapter_markers, noise_profiles, export_presets, podcast_episodes), Sentry Swift SDK (crash reporting, dSYM symbolication), Xcode Cloud Workflow (build, test, TestFlight upload, dSYM upload, App Store submission on tag).
target_output.workflows: Multi-Track Recording Session: Producer opens app, creates new session (recording_sessions INSERT), selects CoreAudio input devices for Host and Guest tracks from device picker populated via AudioObjectGetPropertyData. AudioEngine installs input nodes and noise removal chain. Producer clicks Record; AudioEngine starts AVAudioEngine, opens AVAudioFile WAV output nodes per track, starts buffer taps for waveform ring buffers. WaveformRenderer drains ring buffer every 20ms via Timer on main actor, draws peak array to Metal Canvas. Producer inserts chapter markers during recording — chapter_markers INSERT with position from engine.lastRenderTime. Producer stops recording; AVAudioEngine stops, WAV files finalized, recording_session duration_seconds updated. External Drive Save: Producer clicks 'Save to External Drive'; NSOpenPanel sheet opens; on selection, app calls url.bookmarkData(options:.withSecurityScope, includingResourceValuesForKeys:nil, relativeTo:nil); bookmark Data stored in recording_sessions.external_bookmark. On next app launch, app retrieves bookmark from GRDB, calls NSURL(resolvingBookmarkData:options:.withSecurityScope) then startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() before accessing recording files. stopAccessingSecurityScopedResource() called on session close. Export: Producer opens ExportSheet, selects MP3 at 128kbps stereo downmix. ExportPipeline mixes WAV tracks via AVMutableComposition, passes to LameEncoder (swift-lame) with ID3 tags from podcast_episodes row and chapter markers from chapter_markers — exports to user-selected destination. LameEncoder is compiled from source in app bundle — no external dynamic library.
target_output.data_model: recording_sessions (id INTEGER PK, title TEXT, created_at TEXT, duration_seconds REAL, status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('recording','paused','complete','interrupted')), notes TEXT, external_bookmark BLOB). audio_tracks (id INTEGER PK, session_id INTEGER FK, track_index INTEGER, input_device_uid TEXT, label TEXT, wav_path TEXT, sample_rate INTEGER DEFAULT 48000, channel_count INTEGER DEFAULT 1). chapter_markers (id INTEGER PK, session_id INTEGER FK, position_seconds REAL, title TEXT, artwork_path TEXT). noise_profiles (id INTEGER PK, track_id INTEGER FK, profile_path TEXT, captured_at TEXT). export_presets (id INTEGER PK, name TEXT, format TEXT CHECK(format IN ('aac','mp3','opus')), bitrate_kbps INTEGER, sample_rate INTEGER, stereo_downmix INTEGER DEFAULT 1). podcast_episodes (id INTEGER PK, session_id INTEGER FK, title TEXT, description TEXT, episode_number INTEGER, rss_episode_url TEXT, published_at TEXT). waveform_snapshots (id INTEGER PK, track_id INTEGER FK, peaks_json TEXT, generated_at TEXT). GRDB migrations numbered 001 through current version. recording_sessions.external_bookmark BLOB stores security-scoped bookmark Data for external drive sessions.
target_output.api_design: No network API. Internal Swift actor interfaces: AudioEngine.startRecording(session:tracks:) async throws -> RecordingHandle. AudioEngine.stopRecording(handle:) async throws. AudioEngine.captureNoiseProfile(track:duration:) async throws -> NoiseProfile. ExportPipeline.export(session:preset:destination:) async throws -> AsyncStream<ExportProgress>. GRDB ValueObservation<[RecordingSession]>.publisher(in:) for session library reactive updates. RecordingController.availableInputDevices() -> [AudioDevice] (synchronous, calls AudioObjectGetPropertyData).
target_output.security_and_compliance: App Sandbox entitlements: com.apple.security.device.audio-input (required for CoreAudio microphone access), com.apple.security.files.user-selected.read-write (required for NSOpenPanel external drive selection and security-scoped bookmarks). Hardened Runtime: no com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation — swift-lame and swift-opus SPM packages compile from source as part of app target, producing no external unsigned dylibs. INTENTIONAL FLAW: The security_and_compliance section here still incorrectly states that the pre-built libmp3lame.dylib is loaded at runtime via dlopen, requiring com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation to be set. This contradicts the fix_report claim of resolving ISS-001. Apple Sign-In token stored in Keychain for iCloud sync module. No user PII collected or transmitted. Privacy manifest (PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy) declares audio-input usage string; no tracking domains.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Xcode Cloud workflow configuration: PR trigger runs swift test and xcodebuild test scheme PodcastStudioTests on macOS 14 simulator. Tag trigger (v*) runs full build for macOS, uploads dSYMs to Sentry via post-action script (sentry-cli upload-dif), uploads to TestFlight, and submits to App Store review via App Store Connect API when tag matches release/* pattern. Direct download DMG: Xcode Cloud post-action runs notarytool staple on exported DMG and uploads to S3 releases bucket. GitHub Actions secondary workflow handles S3 upload from Xcode Cloud artifact. GRDB migrations run on app launch in AppDelegate.applicationDidFinishLaunching. TestFlight internal group receives builds on every tag push for QA before App Store submission.
target_output.observability: Sentry Swift SDK captures unhandled Swift errors and NSExceptions with full stack traces. dSYM files uploaded in Xcode Cloud post-action for symbolicated crash reports. Local diagnostic logs written via Logger (OSLog subsystem com.studio.podcaststudio) to ~/Library/Logs/PodcastStudio/session_{id}.log: recording start/stop events, CoreAudio device change notifications, export completion with duration and file size, AVAudioEngine route change events. Sentry performance tracing for ExportPipeline.export() operations to track p95 export duration by format (AAC/MP3/Opus) and input duration. No remote analytics without explicit user opt-in via Settings toggle.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No server infrastructure cost for local recording features. S3 DMG distribution: ~$2/month for installer delivery via CloudFront. Sentry free tier (5K errors/month) covers indie launch scale; Team plan at $26/month on growth. App Store developer program $99/year. Total infrastructure: ~$30/month. Scaling is entirely client-side — each macOS installation runs independently. Multi-track recording at 8 tracks (48kHz/32-bit float WAV) writes ~9 MB/minute per track, 72 MB/minute total — standard SSD throughput with no bottleneck.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): 2-track CoreAudio recording with WAV output, AVAudioEngine noise removal node, MP3 and AAC export via swift-lame and AVAssetExportSession, ID3 tag embedding, SQLite session storage with GRDB migrations, security-scoped bookmark workflow for external drives (ISS-002 resolved), App Sandbox and Hardened Runtime with no disable-library-validation (ISS-001 resolved — NOTE: contradicted in security_and_compliance section, intentional flaw), Xcode Cloud CI with TestFlight, Sentry crash reporting, App Store submission. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Up to 8 tracks, Opus export via swift-opus, chapter marker recording and export, noise profile capture UI, waveform snapshot caching for large sessions, per-track volume and pan mixing in AVAudioEngine. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Episode manager with RSS feed metadata, export history tracking, iCloud sync via Apple Sign-In for session metadata (audio files remain local), export preset library, episode artwork embedding.
target_output.development_guidelines: Swift strict concurrency (Swift 6 mode in Xcode 16) for all audio actor boundaries. CoreAudio buffer tap callbacks must never allocate memory or touch SwiftUI state — write to ring buffer only, drain on main actor via Timer. GRDB DatabaseQueue configured as serial writer; all writes from main actor or background Task with explicit queue dispatch. Swift Package Manager only — no CocoaPods or Carthage. swift-lame and swift-opus SPM dependencies must compile from source; verify via swift package show-dependencies that no pre-built binary target is included. INTENTIONAL FLAW: development_guidelines section here continues to describe the dynamic libmp3lame.dylib dlopen approach as the correct implementation, directly contradicting the fix_report. XCTest for GRDB migration tests and ExportPipeline unit tests (mock AVAudioEngine). Xcode Cloud runs xcodebuild test on every PR.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AVAudioEngine route change (Bluetooth headset connect/disconnect) during recording interrupts audio graph. Mitigation: observe AVAudioEngineConfigurationChange notification; call reset() and rebuild signal graph from saved track configuration; WAV output nodes reopened and recording resumed from last known position. Risk: App Store review rejection for audio background mode if app requests com.apple.security.device.audio-input without clear reviewer justification. Mitigation: App Store review notes explain multi-track podcast recording use case; demo video provided with submission. Tradeoff: swift-lame SPM compile-from-source adds ~45 seconds to Xcode Cloud build time vs pre-built dylib — accepted to maintain Hardened Runtime compliance. Tradeoff: GRDB.swift over Core Data — type-safe SQL queries over CoreData's NSPredicate string-based API; smaller binary size; no iCloud Core Data sync needed since audio files remain local.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: swift-lame SPM package chosen over AudioToolbox AudioConverter for MP3 — AudioConverter does not natively produce MP3 on macOS without the Fraunhofer codec (not available for App Store). swift-opus SPM chosen for Opus export; libopus has permissive BSD license compatible with App Store distribution. GRDB.swift chosen over Core Data for type-safe Swift query API and simpler migration management. Security-scoped bookmarks chosen over NSFileCoordinator for external drive access — bookmarks provide persistence across app launches within sandbox constraints.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed round 3: fix_report claims ISS-001 and ISS-002 resolved but security_and_compliance and development_guidelines sections still describe the non-compliant dynamic library approach, modeling a plan that fails to propagate fixes through all sections.

project_goal.value: Publish a static developer documentation site for the video transcoding API covering endpoint references, quickstart guides, language-specific code samples, and an interactive API explorer
project_goal.rationale: Developers adopting the transcoding API need a single authoritative reference with runnable examples

target_users.value: External developers integrating the video transcoding REST API using Python, Node.js, or Go SDKs
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are backend engineers building media pipelines

project_class.rationale: No server-side runtime needed; content is fully static with client-side API explorer

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery with usage analytics; no auth, payments, or backend compute

complexity_level.rationale: Pure static site with MDX content and a client-side interactive widget

risk_level.rationale: No user data stored; public documentation with no sensitive content

data_sensitivity.rationale: Documentation only; no PII, credentials, or proprietary data published

external_exposure.rationale: Public-facing developer docs with no access restrictions

access_model.value: Fully public, no authentication required
access_model.rationale: Developer docs must be freely accessible without sign-in friction

feature_scope.rationale: Full coverage needed for developer self-service adoption

mvp_scope.value: Endpoint reference for jobs and outputs resources, Node.js quickstart guide, interactive API explorer, changelog, error code catalog
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the most common integration path for launch

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Vercel; no user-submitted data stored; CSP headers configured to allow API explorer sandbox calls only
security_baseline.rationale: Standard secure-by-default static hosting; sandbox API calls need explicit CSP allowlist

frontend_stack.rationale: Best-in-class static docs framework with versioning, search, and MDX support

backend_stack.rationale: No server-side compute; API explorer calls go directly to the transcoding sandbox

data_platform.value: None; no database required
data_platform.rationale: All content is file-based MDX and static JSON; no persistence layer needed

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with global CDN distribution
hosting_target.rationale: Zero-config static hosting with preview deployments per PR and instant cache invalidation

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data collected; Vercel Analytics uses cookie-free edge analytics with no PII retention

observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight observability appropriate for a static docs site

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development with PR preview deployments; releases tied to API versioning cadence
execution_preference.rationale: Keeps docs in sync with API releases through automated pipelines

project.goal: Static developer documentation site for video transcoding API
project.target_users: Backend developers integrating via Python, Node.js, and Go SDKs
project.timeline: Launch with API v1 GA; updated on each API release

frontend.interactive_explorer: Redoc with try-it-out against sandbox base URL

security.headers: CSP allowing sandbox API domain, HSTS, X-Frame-Options DENY

data.content: MDX files in /docs directory versioned in Git
data.openapi_spec: openapi.yaml in repo root; code samples generated from spec
data.changelog: CHANGELOG.md rendered as Docusaurus doc page

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: validate OpenAPI spec, generate code samples, build Docusaurus, deploy to Vercel
devops.environments: Production on main; PR previews per branch

constraints.deploy_time: Under 5 minutes from merge to live

reasoner_reviews.product: Docusaurus 3 is the right framework for this API documentation site. The versioning capability is essential for when v2 launches. The interactive Redoc explorer against the sandbox is a strong developer adoption driver -- engineers can test POST /v1/jobs calls and inspect webhook payloads without leaving the browser.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Auto-generating code samples from openapi.yaml in CI eliminates the chronic drift problem between documented examples and actual API behavior. The pipeline should cache the Docusaurus .cache directory between runs and validate the OpenAPI spec with swagger-codegen validate before running generation, so a malformed spec fails fast rather than producing broken snippets.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers must explicitly allowlist the transcoding sandbox domain (sandbox.api.example.com) for the Redoc try-it-out to function. HSTS preloading and X-Frame-Options DENY should be set as Vercel response headers. No cookies are set by the docs site itself, so GDPR consent banners are not triggered. Sandbox API keys entered by visitors in the explorer must never be logged or sent to analytics.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 5-minute deploy target is achievable with Vercel incremental builds and GitHub Actions caching of node_modules and .docusaurus. OpenAPI spec validation should be the first step in the pipeline to fail early. The openapi-generator Mustache templates should be versioned in the repo to ensure consistent snippet formatting across language targets.

security.auth_design: No authentication; fully public site with no login surface or session management
security.abuse_prevention: Vercel DDoS protection at edge; rate limiting on sandbox API enforced by the transcoding service, not this docs site
security.privacy_controls: Cookie-free Vercel Analytics; no user tracking scripts; Algolia search queries are not linked to any visitor identity; sandbox API keys entered by visitors are stored only in browser session memory and never transmitted to docs infrastructure
security.security_risks: Risk: sandbox API credentials visible in browser network tab during explorer calls -- acceptable since sandbox is rate-limited and isolated from production data

data.entities: api_endpoints (path, method ENUM get/post/delete/patch, resource STRING, description TEXT, request_schema_ref, response_schema_ref -- sourced from openapi.yaml), code_samples (language ENUM python/nodejs/go, endpoint_path, http_method, snippet TEXT -- generated artifacts), guide_pages (slug, title, section ENUM quickstart/concepts/guides, content_mdx -- authored files), changelog_entries (version SEMVER, release_date, breaking_changes TEXT[], additions TEXT[], fixes TEXT[] -- parsed from CHANGELOG.md), error_code_catalog (http_status INT, error_code STRING e.g. TRANSCODE_INPUT_UNREACHABLE, description, resolution TEXT -- static JSON)
data.storage_design: All content stored as MDX and JSON files in the Git repository; no database; Algolia index built from Docusaurus output at deploy time via DocSearch scraper
data.migration_notes: Initial content authored manually for v1 endpoints; subsequent code samples regenerated from openapi.yaml on each release; changelog sourced from existing CHANGELOG.md with backfill to v1.0.0

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repo triggers GitHub Actions on push to main or PR open; Actions validates OpenAPI spec, generates code samples for python/nodejs/go targets, runs bun run build, deploys to Vercel Edge Network via vercel deploy CLI
devops.environments: Production: docs.example.com on main branch; Preview: auto-generated Vercel URLs per PR posted as GitHub PR comment
devops.ci_cd_design: Steps: 1) swagger-codegen validate --fail-fast on openapi.yaml, 2) openapi-generator-cli generate for python/nodejs/go, 3) copy generated snippets to /docs/code-samples/, 4) bun install with cache, 5) bun run build, 6) vercel deploy --prod for main or vercel deploy for PR
devops.rollback_strategy: Vercel instant rollback to any prior deployment via dashboard; git revert plus push triggers automatic redeploy for content errors
devops.cost_controls: Vercel Pro plan ~$20/month; Algolia DocSearch free for public open-source documentation; GitHub Actions within free tier for public repos

frontend.app_structure: Docusaurus 3 with MDX content and TypeScript; versioned docs plugin for API version history; Algolia DocSearch for search
frontend.pages_and_flows: Getting Started, Endpoint Reference per Resource (jobs/outputs/webhooks), Quickstart Guides, Webhook Event Reference, SDK Downloads
frontend.performance_notes: Docusaurus builds static HTML with optimized asset hashing; Algolia search loaded asynchronously; code blocks use Prism with only required language grammars
frontend.frontend_risks: API key examples in code samples must use placeholder values and be clearly marked as non-functional to prevent credential leakage in docs

target_output.thinking_summary: Static docs site for a video transcoding API using Docusaurus 3 on Vercel. Key decisions: auto-generate code samples from openapi.yaml in CI to prevent drift, use Algolia DocSearch for search without any backend, configure CSP headers to allow the interactive Redoc explorer to call the sandbox API, and cache Docusaurus build artifacts to hit the 5-minute deploy target.
target_output.title: Video Transcoding API Documentation Site Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static developer documentation site built with Docusaurus 3 and MDX, deployed to Vercel's edge network. Code samples for Python, Node.js, and Go are auto-generated from openapi.yaml on each API release via a GitHub Actions pipeline, eliminating manual drift. An embedded Redoc explorer lets developers send live requests to the transcoding sandbox without leaving the browser. Algolia DocSearch provides full-text search with no backend infrastructure required.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-origin static site architecture with no server-side runtime. Docusaurus 3 builds MDX content files and the openapi.yaml specification into a React SPA that hydrates on the client. GitHub Actions orchestrates the release pipeline: OpenAPI spec validation via swagger-codegen validate, code sample generation for Python, Node.js, and Go targets via openapi-generator-cli, Docusaurus build, and deployment via vercel deploy CLI. Vercel Edge Network distributes static assets globally with automatic CDN cache invalidation on each deploy. The interactive API explorer is a client-side Redoc component configured to call the transcoding sandbox base URL; Content-Security-Policy headers explicitly allowlist that domain. Algolia DocSearch indexes the built site and serves search results from its own CDN. No cookies, no user accounts, no server-side state of any kind.
target_output.technology_stack: Static Site: Docusaurus 3 with MDX, TypeScript, React 18 for custom components. Code Generation: openapi-generator-cli with custom Mustache templates for Python (requests), Node.js (axios), and Go (net/http) SDK snippets. Search: Algolia DocSearch free tier. Interactive Explorer: Redoc with try-it-out mode pointed at https://sandbox.api.example.com/v1. Build Tool: Bun for package management and build scripts. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with bun and .docusaurus cache layers. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network with custom domain. Analytics: Vercel Analytics cookie-free aggregate tracking.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Endpoint Reference: auto-generated pages for POST /v1/jobs (submit transcoding job), GET /v1/jobs/{id} (poll status), GET /v1/outputs/{id} (retrieve output), POST /v1/webhooks (register callback), DELETE /v1/webhooks/{id} (remove callback) -- each page shows path, method, request schema, response schema, and generated code samples in a tabbed panel. Quickstart Guide: Node.js walkthrough covering API key setup, submitting an H.264 transcode job, polling for COMPLETED status, and downloading the output URL. Interactive Explorer: Redoc panel with try-it-out enabled; visitor enters their sandbox API key in the auth dialog, sends real requests, and sees formatted responses. Error Code Catalog: full table of error codes including TRANSCODE_INPUT_UNREACHABLE, CODEC_NOT_SUPPORTED, QUOTA_EXCEEDED with resolution steps. Changelog: versioned entries from CHANGELOG.md with anchor links per release.
target_output.system_components: GitHub Repository: single source of truth for MDX content, openapi.yaml, Mustache templates, and Docusaurus config. GitHub Actions Runner: validates spec, generates code samples, builds site, deploys to Vercel. Vercel Edge Network: serves static assets from 40+ PoPs with automatic cache invalidation on deploy. Algolia DocSearch Crawler: indexes the live docs site nightly and on post-deploy webhook trigger. Transcoding Sandbox API: external service at sandbox.api.example.com; the Redoc explorer sends authenticated requests using the visitor's own sandbox key, entered client-side only.
target_output.workflows: API Release Deployment: an engineer merges a PR bumping openapi.yaml to v1.4.0; GitHub Actions triggers the pipeline -- swagger-codegen validate fails the build immediately if the spec has schema errors; openapi-generator-cli generates Python, Node.js, and Go snippets into /docs/code-samples/{lang}/; bun run build compiles the Docusaurus site; vercel deploy --prod pushes to Vercel and invalidates the global CDN cache; Algolia re-indexes within 5 minutes via the deploy webhook; total elapsed time under 5 minutes. PR Preview Workflow: any PR branch triggers the same pipeline with vercel deploy (no --prod flag), producing a unique preview URL automatically posted to the PR as a GitHub Actions comment, allowing content reviewers to verify rendered MDX before merge.
target_output.data_model: api_endpoints (path STRING e.g. /v1/jobs/{id}, method ENUM get/post/delete/patch, resource STRING e.g. jobs/outputs/webhooks, description TEXT, request_schema_ref STRING, response_schema_ref STRING -- sourced from openapi.yaml components/schemas). code_samples (language ENUM python/nodejs/go, endpoint_path STRING, http_method STRING, snippet TEXT -- generated artifacts committed to /docs/code-samples/). guide_pages (slug STRING, title STRING, section ENUM quickstart/concepts/guides/reference, content_mdx TEXT -- authored MDX files in /docs/guides/). changelog_entries (version SEMVER e.g. 1.4.0, release_date DATE, breaking_changes TEXT[], additions TEXT[], fixes TEXT[] -- parsed from CHANGELOG.md). error_code_catalog (http_status INT, error_code STRING, description TEXT, resolution TEXT -- static /src/data/errors.json).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API is exposed by this docs site. Client-side Redoc explorer calls the external transcoding sandbox directly. Explorer configuration: baseUrl set to https://sandbox.api.example.com/v1 via docusaurus.config.ts customFields; visitor enters their sandbox API key into the Redoc auth dialog; key is stored only in browser session memory. Vercel response headers set via vercel.json: Content-Security-Policy allows connect-src to sandbox.api.example.com and *.algolia.net only; no other external fetch targets permitted. No proxying of sandbox API calls through any docs infrastructure.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' (required by Docusaurus React hydration); connect-src 'self' https://sandbox.api.example.com https://*.algolia.net; img-src 'self' data: blob:. HSTS: Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains set as Vercel response header. X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking of the docs iframe. No cookies set by the docs site; Vercel Analytics uses cookie-free edge-side page view counting. Sandbox API keys entered by visitors are never logged, never sent to analytics events, and never persisted beyond the browser session.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow triggered on push to main or pull_request. Job steps: checkout repo, setup-bun, restore cache (node_modules key and .docusaurus key), run swagger-codegen validate --fail-fast openapi.yaml (exits non-zero on schema error), run openapi-generator-cli generate for python/nodejs/go targets outputting to /tmp/gen, copy snippets to /docs/code-samples/{lang}/, run bun run build (Docusaurus), save build cache, run vercel deploy --prod --token $VERCEL_TOKEN for main branch or vercel deploy for PR branches. PR branches: Vercel returns preview URL; GitHub Actions posts it as a PR comment via actions/github-script. Total pipeline target under 5 minutes with warm cache. Rollback: Vercel dashboard one-click rollback to any prior deployment; for content errors git revert plus push triggers automatic redeploy. Domain: docs.example.com via Vercel custom domain with auto-renewed TLS certificate.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: daily active visitors, top pages by view count, geographic distribution -- no PII, cookie-free. Algolia DocSearch dashboard: search queries with zero results (reveals missing documentation coverage), click-through rate per search result position. GitHub Actions: Slack notification to #docs-deploys on workflow success or failure, including deploy URL for previews. Docusaurus built-in broken-link checker runs in CI and fails the build on any internal dead link, preventing regressions from endpoint page renames.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan: approximately $20/month covering custom domain, team access, and analytics. Algolia DocSearch: free for public open-source documentation projects. GitHub Actions: within free tier for public repositories at current PR cadence. Total infrastructure cost approximately $20/month with no per-request or per-visitor scaling cost -- Vercel Edge Network auto-scales to any traffic volume without capacity planning.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Launch (4 weeks): Docusaurus 3 scaffold with brand theme and custom CSS variables, endpoint reference pages for POST /v1/jobs and GET /v1/jobs/{id} and GET /v1/outputs/{id} generated from openapi.yaml, Node.js quickstart guide, error code catalog with TRANSCODE_INPUT_UNREACHABLE and CODEC_NOT_SUPPORTED entries, changelog, Redoc interactive explorer wired to sandbox, GitHub Actions pipeline with OpenAPI validation and Vercel deployment, Algolia DocSearch integration. Phase 2 - SDK Coverage (3 weeks): Python and Go quickstart guides, code samples for POST /v1/webhooks and DELETE /v1/webhooks/{id}, Docusaurus versioned docs scaffold for upcoming v2 API, broken-link checker in CI. Phase 3 - Community (ongoing): contribution guide for external doc PRs, localization scaffold for Japanese and German, automated Algolia re-crawl on every deploy via webhook.
target_output.development_guidelines: All content authored in MDX; raw HTML blocks in documentation pages are forbidden. Code samples must never be hand-edited -- modify openapi.yaml and regenerate via the CI pipeline or locally with bun run generate-samples. TypeScript strict mode enforced for any custom Docusaurus React components. Prettier formatting checked in CI. PR title must reference the API version when openapi.yaml changes (e.g. 'docs: update for API v1.4.0'). Preview deployments are mandatory before merging any content change; do not merge without a preview URL review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: openapi-generator output quality for code samples may require significant Mustache template overrides for idiomatic Python and Go. Mitigation: maintain generator-templates/ directory in the repo with per-language Mustache overrides, validated in CI. Risk: Algolia DocSearch free tier could be discontinued if the project loses open-source classification. Mitigation: evaluate Docusaurus built-in lunr.js search as fallback -- lower relevance but zero cost. Tradeoff: Redoc try-it-out exposes the sandbox base URL in the page source; acceptable because the sandbox is rate-limited and entirely isolated from production data and credentials.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Docusaurus 3 over MkDocs Material for superior TypeScript and React extensibility needed for the custom Redoc explorer component. Chose Algolia DocSearch over Pagefind for hosted indexing and better relevance ranking on API terminology searches. Confirmed Redoc over Swagger UI for the cleaner three-panel reference layout suited to the transcoding API endpoint schemas. Resolved to call the sandbox API directly from the client rather than proxying through a docs server to maintain a fully static architecture.

metadata.notes: Static Docusaurus docs site for video transcoding API with CI-driven OpenAPI code sample generation and embedded Redoc explorer.

project_goal.value: Real-time video metadata enrichment API that ingests live stream manifests and returns continuously updated chapter markers, ad insertion cues, and closed caption availability signals to partner player clients
project_goal.rationale: Core product capability enabling partner players to deliver synchronized interactive experiences during live broadcasts

target_users.value: Partner engineering teams integrating player clients into broadcast and OTT platforms
target_users.rationale: B2B API product consumed by downstream player SDK developers

project_class.rationale: Pure API surface with no browser UI; partner-facing service without frontend delivery requirement

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, realtime, public_api, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Requires SSE push, partner JWT auth, PostgreSQL persistence, and upstream SCTE-35 ingest webhook integrations

complexity_level.rationale: Fan-out SSE delivery at scale plus SCTE-35 parsing and multi-stream state management in Redis requires careful design

risk_level.rationale: Partner SLA contractual obligations on ad insertion accuracy and FCC captioning compliance elevate operational risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stream metadata and SCTE-35 cue data are internal operational data without personal information

external_exposure.rationale: API is only accessible to authenticated partners via issued JWT credentials, not open to the public internet

access_model.value: Partner API keys issued as JWTs with per-key channel scope claims; key rotation every 90 days
access_model.rationale: Partners need scoped access limited to their own stream channels to prevent cross-tenant data leakage

feature_scope.value: SSE subscription feeds per stream session, SCTE-35 ingest webhook, chapter marker management, closed caption track availability signals, ad break window scheduling
feature_scope.rationale: Covers the full real-time metadata lifecycle from upstream ingest through player delivery

mvp_scope.value: SSE push for chapter and ad cue events, SCTE-35 webhook ingest, partner JWT auth, Redis stream state, PostgreSQL audit log
mvp_scope.rationale: Core SSE delivery and SCTE-35 parsing are the primary value; caption signals can follow in phase two

security_baseline.value: JWT partner authentication with channel-scoped claims, TLS 1.3, rate limiting per API key, HMAC webhook signature verification for SCTE-35 ingest
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API must prevent unauthorized stream access and validate all ingest payloads

frontend_stack.rationale: api_service project class requires no browser frontend

backend_stack.rationale: Axum provides async SSE primitives and Rust ownership model gives memory-safe concurrent state management for high fan-out SSE delivery

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for persistent metadata and audit, Redis 7 for live stream state and SSE fan-out pub/sub
data_platform.rationale: Postgres handles durable storage of chapter markers and compliance audit logs; Redis pub/sub drives low-latency SSE fan-out to subscribers

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 and eu-west-1
hosting_target.rationale: Multi-region Fargate deployment meets partner SLA latency requirements for North American and European broadcast markets

privacy_retention_policy.value: SCTE-35 cue audit logs retained 1 year; stream session metadata purged after 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Audit retention supports FCC compliance investigations; session data beyond 30 days has no operational value

future_scope.value: WebSocket upgrade option for bidirectional metadata negotiation, HLS interstitial cue injection, multi-CDN metadata mirroring
future_scope.rationale: Partners have requested bidirectional control and interstitial support for advanced ad scenarios

constraints.value: p99 SSE event latency under 500 ms from SCTE-35 ingest to player delivery; no external ML inference in the critical path
constraints.rationale: Ad insertion timing accuracy requires sub-500 ms fan-out; ML inference latency would violate this bound

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, custom Datadog metrics for per-stream SSE subscriber count and SCTE-35 processing latency, PagerDuty for SLA breach alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Partner SLA requires operational visibility into per-stream delivery health and cue timing accuracy

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with 2-week sprints; SSE core shipped first, SCTE-35 ingest in sprint 3
execution_preference.rationale: SSE infrastructure is the foundational dependency; all other features build on it

compliance_context.value: FCC closed captioning availability signaling obligations for US broadcast streams; partner contractual SLAs for ad insertion cue accuracy within 500 ms
compliance_context.rationale: FCC mandates that caption availability be signaled to players; partner contracts include financial penalties for missed ad cue delivery

project.goal: Deliver real-time chapter, ad cue, and caption availability signals to partner player clients via SSE
project.target_users: Partner engineering teams building OTT and broadcast player integrations
project.timeline: MVP in 8 weeks, caption signals in 12 weeks

backend.framework: Axum with tokio-stream SSE
backend.api: REST for management endpoints, SSE for event delivery, webhook for SCTE-35 ingest

security.auth: JWT with partner channel-scoped claims

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for chapter markers and audit log
data.cache: Redis 7 pub/sub for SSE fan-out and live stream state

constraints.latency: p99 SSE delivery under 500ms from SCTE-35 receipt

open_questions.caption_signal_source: Determine whether caption availability comes from manifest scraping or separate caption ingest API

confirmed_decisions.sse_over_websocket: SSE chosen for simplicity and HTTP/2 multiplexing compatibility
confirmed_decisions.redis_pubsub: Redis pub/sub for fan-out avoids Axum in-process broadcast limits

reasoner_reviews.product: The SSE delivery model correctly decouples the player from polling the manifest directly, which reduces per-player origin load and enables sub-500 ms cue delivery. Ad break windows and chapter markers are the highest-value signals for partner monetization workflows.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust with Axum is an excellent fit here: tokio-stream SSE support is mature, Axum tower middleware handles JWT validation cleanly, and Rust async model handles high SSE fan-out without thread-per-connection overhead. Redis pub/sub is the right fan-out mechanism to decouple the SCTE-35 ingest path from individual SSE connections.
reasoner_reviews.security: HMAC signature verification on the SCTE-35 ingest webhook is critical to prevent injection of fake ad cues. JWT claims must be scoped to specific channel IDs so a partner cannot subscribe to another partner's stream. Rate limiting on the SSE connection endpoint prevents resource exhaustion from misconfigured player clients.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 500 ms p99 latency budget is achievable with Redis pub/sub fan-out if Axum SSE handlers avoid blocking on Postgres writes in the hot path. The audit log write to Postgres must be async and non-blocking relative to the SSE publish.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Caption signal availability needs a concrete source design before MVP; the open question must be resolved in sprint 1 or caption signals will slip past 12 weeks.

backend.service_design: Single Axum service with three handler groups: /sessions (partner stream registration and SSE subscribe), /ingest (SCTE-35 webhook), /admin (chapter marker CRUD for operators). SSE connections held open via tokio-stream ReceiverStream per subscriber.
backend.api_patterns: REST for session management and chapter CRUD; SSE endpoint GET /v1/streams/{channel_id}/events returns text/event-stream with event types: chapter_marker, ad_cue_start, ad_cue_end, caption_available. Webhook POST /v1/ingest/scte35 with HMAC-SHA256 validation.
backend.failure_handling: Redis pub/sub consumer reconnects with exponential backoff on connection loss. SSE handler detects client disconnect via channel close and cleans up subscriber state. SCTE-35 ingest endpoint returns 202 Accepted immediately and processes asynchronously to avoid blocking upstream automation.
backend.scaling_notes: Axum SSE handlers are async and share a single tokio runtime; horizontal scaling via ECS task count increase. Each task subscribes to the same Redis channel so fan-out is distributed across tasks. Redis cluster mode not required at current scale.

security.auth_design: JWT issued to partners at onboarding with claims: partner_id, allowed_channel_ids[], exp. Axum middleware validates signature and channel_id claim on every SSE and management request.
security.secrets_management: JWT signing keys stored in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day automatic rotation. SCTE-35 webhook HMAC secret stored per-integration in Secrets Manager.
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limit SSE connections to 50 concurrent per API key via Redis counter. Webhook endpoint accepts only allowlisted upstream IP ranges per partner configuration.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every SCTE-35 cue ingested and every SSE event dispatched written to Postgres audit_events table with timestamp, channel_id, event_type, and partner_id for FCC compliance retrieval.

data.entities: stream_manifests (id, channel_id, partner_id, hls_url, status, created_at), chapter_markers (id, channel_id, timecode_ms, title, inserted_by, created_at), scte35_cue_points (id, channel_id, splice_event_id, cue_type ENUM splice_in/splice_out, pts_time, duration_ms, received_at, published_at), caption_tracks (id, channel_id, language_code, availability ENUM available/unavailable, detected_at), partner_channels (id, partner_id, channel_name, ingest_hmac_secret_ref, created_at), audit_events (id, channel_id, partner_id, event_type, payload_json, created_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ for chapter markers, cue audit log, and partner channel config. Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster for live stream state key-value (current ad break status, active chapter) and pub/sub channels keyed by channel_id.
data.retention_and_deletion: scte35_cue_points and audit_events retained 1 year via pg_partman monthly partitions with automated drop. stream_manifests and session data purged after 30 days via Lambda scheduled cleanup.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service in us-east-1 and eu-west-1, each behind an ALB. Route53 latency-based routing directs partners to nearest region. RDS Multi-AZ per region. ElastiCache Redis 7 per region.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: cargo test with testcontainers-rs for Postgres and Redis, cargo clippy --deny warnings, Docker build, push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy with 100% minimum healthy percent.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM via datadog-agent sidecar. Custom DogStatsD metrics: sse_subscriber_count per channel, scte35_ingest_latency_ms histogram, sse_event_publish_latency_ms histogram. PagerDuty alert when p99 publish latency exceeds 450 ms.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS deployment circuit breaker enabled; failed deployments auto-roll back to previous task definition revision within 3 minutes.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a Rust/Axum SSE fan-out API for live broadcast metadata delivery. The critical path is SCTE-35 ingest to SSE publish under 500 ms p99 - Redis pub/sub fan-out with async Postgres audit writes decoupled from the hot path achieves this. JWT with channel-scoped claims prevents cross-tenant data access and FCC caption compliance requires durable audit logging. Multi-region Fargate with Route53 latency routing keeps EU and NA partners under their latency budget.
target_output.title: Live Stream Metadata Enrichment API
target_output.executive_summary: A Rust/Axum API service delivering real-time chapter markers, SCTE-35 ad insertion cues, and closed caption availability signals to partner player clients via Server-Sent Events. Upstream broadcast automation systems post SCTE-35 commands to an HMAC-verified webhook; the service parses cue points, updates live stream state in Redis, and fans out events to all active SSE subscribers within the 500 ms p99 SLA. Deployed on ECS Fargate in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 with Route53 latency routing, PostgreSQL 16 for durable audit storage, and Datadog for per-stream operational visibility.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-layer architecture: an ingest layer accepting SCTE-35 splice commands via HMAC-signed webhook, a state layer maintaining live stream metadata in Redis with pub/sub fan-out, and a delivery layer streaming events to partner player clients over SSE. Rust with Axum serves all layers from a single binary with distinct handler groups. The SCTE-35 webhook handler validates signature, parses cue type and PTS timestamp, writes state to Redis, and publishes to a per-channel Redis pub/sub topic. SSE handlers subscribe to the Redis channel and stream events to connected player clients. Postgres receives async audit writes of every published cue for FCC compliance retrieval. ECS Fargate tasks scale horizontally; Route53 latency routing directs EU partners to eu-west-1 and NA partners to us-east-1.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Rust 1.78 with Axum 0.7, tokio-stream for SSE, redis-rs async client for pub/sub, sqlx for async Postgres queries. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ per region with pg_partman for monthly partition management on audit tables. Cache/Fan-out: Redis 7 ElastiCache for live stream state and pub/sub channels. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB per region, Route53 latency routing, ECR, Secrets Manager for JWT keys and HMAC secrets. Observability: Datadog APM with DogStatsD metrics, PagerDuty for SLA alerts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Stream Session Management: partners register a live channel session and receive an SSE connection URL scoped to their channel_id. Real-Time SSE Delivery: connected player clients receive chapter_marker, ad_cue_start, ad_cue_end, and caption_available events as triggered by upstream systems. SCTE-35 Webhook Ingest: broadcast automation systems POST splice commands; the API validates HMAC signatures, parses splice_insert and splice_schedule messages, and converts to internal cue events. Chapter Marker Management: operator REST endpoints for creating, updating, and deleting chapter markers on a live channel timeline. Caption Track Availability: upstream caption services notify the API when tracks become available; fan-out caption_available events to subscribers. Partner API Key Management: admin endpoints for issuing and revoking partner JWTs with channel-scoped claims.
target_output.system_components: Axum HTTP server (SSE delivery, webhook ingest, management REST), Redis 7 ElastiCache (live stream state keys: current_ad_break:{channel_id}, active_chapter:{channel_id}; pub/sub channels: stream_events:{channel_id}), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (stream_manifests, chapter_markers, scte35_cue_points, caption_tracks, partner_channels, audit_events tables), AWS Secrets Manager (JWT signing keys, per-partner HMAC secrets), ALB with TLS termination per region, ECS Fargate cluster with auto-scaling, Route53 latency routing across us-east-1 and eu-west-1.
target_output.workflows: SCTE-35 Ad Cue Delivery: Broadcast automation system sends POST /v1/ingest/scte35 with splice_insert payload and X-Signature HMAC header. Axum handler verifies HMAC using partner stored secret, parses splice_event_id, cue_type, and pts_time. Redis HSET updates current_ad_break:{channel_id} and PUBLISH dispatches ad_cue_start event to stream_events:{channel_id} pub/sub topic. Subscribed SSE handlers receive the message and write it to each connected player SSE stream. Async sqlx task writes audit_events record. Total path from webhook receipt to player delivery targets p99 under 500 ms. Partner Stream Subscription: Partner player calls GET /v1/streams/{channel_id}/events with Bearer JWT. Axum middleware decodes JWT, validates channel_id claim matches path parameter, increments sse_subscriber_count DogStatsD gauge. Handler subscribes to Redis pub/sub channel and streams events as text/event-stream. On client disconnect, tokio select detects channel close, decrements gauge, and cleans up subscription.
target_output.data_model: stream_manifests (id UUID PK, channel_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, hls_url TEXT, status ENUM active/ended, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). chapter_markers (id UUID PK, channel_id UUID FK, timecode_ms BIGINT, title TEXT, inserted_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scte35_cue_points (id UUID PK, channel_id UUID FK, splice_event_id BIGINT, cue_type ENUM splice_in/splice_out, pts_time BIGINT, duration_ms INT, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ) PARTITION BY RANGE (received_at) monthly. caption_tracks (id UUID PK, channel_id UUID FK, language_code CHAR(3), availability ENUM available/unavailable, detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ). partner_channels (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, channel_name TEXT, ingest_hmac_secret_ref TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, channel_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, event_type TEXT, payload_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) PARTITION BY RANGE (created_at) monthly.
target_output.api_design: SSE: GET /v1/streams/{channel_id}/events - Bearer JWT required, channel_id claim validated, returns text/event-stream with event types chapter_marker/ad_cue_start/ad_cue_end/caption_available, each with JSON data payload. Webhook: POST /v1/ingest/scte35 - HMAC-SHA256 on X-Signature header, returns 202 Accepted. Management: POST /v1/channels/{channel_id}/chapters, PATCH /v1/channels/{channel_id}/chapters/{id}, DELETE /v1/channels/{channel_id}/chapters/{id}. Admin: POST /v1/partners/{id}/keys (issue JWT), DELETE /v1/partners/{id}/keys/{key_id} (revoke). Rate limits: 50 concurrent SSE connections per API key enforced via Redis counter.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT Authentication: RS256-signed JWTs with claims partner_id, allowed_channel_ids[], iat, exp (24h). Signing keys in Secrets Manager, rotated every 90 days. Axum tower middleware validates on every request. HMAC Webhook Validation: each partner channel has an HMAC-SHA256 secret; webhook handler computes HMAC over raw request body and compares to X-Signature header with constant-time comparison. FCC Compliance: caption_available events and all SCTE-35 cue publications written to partitioned audit_events table retained 1 year for FCC inquiry response. Partner Channel Isolation: JWT allowed_channel_ids claim prevents cross-partner data access; Postgres row-level queries always include partner_id filter. TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate service in us-east-1 and eu-west-1, each with auto-scaling on CPU (target 50%) and SSE connection count (custom CloudWatch metric). Minimum 2 tasks per region, maximum 20. ALB per region with HTTPS listener and TLS 1.3 policy. Route53 latency routing record for api.metadata.example.com pointing to both regional ALBs. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ per region; eu-west-1 instance is a cross-region read replica promotable on failover. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster per region. GitHub Actions CI: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --deny warnings, cargo test with testcontainers-rs (spins real Postgres and Redis), Docker build with cargo-chef caching, push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Secrets Manager rotation Lambda triggers 90-day JWT key rotation with 24h grace period for old keys.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces span: webhook receipt -> Redis HSET -> Redis PUBLISH -> SSE write, enabling per-stage latency breakdown. Custom DogStatsD metrics: sse_subscriber_count{channel_id} gauge, scte35_ingest_to_publish_latency_ms histogram, sse_event_delivery_latency_ms histogram, webhook_signature_failures_total counter. PagerDuty P1 alert when p99 scte35_ingest_to_publish_latency_ms exceeds 450 ms for more than 2 minutes. CloudWatch ALB metrics for 4xx and 5xx error rates per region.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Fargate: 2 tasks per region at 0.5 vCPU/1 GB baseline ~$60/month per region, scaling to 20 tasks under high subscriber load at ~$600/month. RDS Multi-AZ db.t4g.medium per region ~$140/month. ElastiCache cache.t4g.medium per region ~$60/month. Total baseline ~$320/month for two regions; peak during major live events ~$1,400/month. pg_partman partition drops eliminate old audit data without vacuum cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): SCTE-35 webhook ingest with HMAC validation, Redis pub/sub fan-out, SSE delivery for ad_cue_start/ad_cue_end events, partner JWT auth with channel scoping, PostgreSQL audit log, Datadog metrics, ECS Fargate single-region deployment. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Chapter marker REST API, caption_available signal delivery via separate ingest webhook, multi-region deployment with Route53 latency routing, PagerDuty SLA alerting, JWT key rotation automation. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Admin portal for partner channel management, advanced ad break window scheduling, capacity testing with synthetic 10,000-subscriber load, SLA report generation for partner billing.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: no unsafe blocks outside reviewed FFI crates; clippy::pedantic enabled in CI with deny(warnings). All Postgres queries via sqlx compile-time checked macros. Integration tests use testcontainers-rs to spin real Redis and Postgres. HMAC comparison must use constant_time_eq crate. JWT validation tests must cover expired token, wrong channel_id claim, and tampered signature cases. DogStatsD metric names follow sse.{metric} and scte35.{metric} prefixes for dashboard grouping.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis pub/sub message loss if a subscriber task crashes between PUBLISH and delivery. Mitigation: SSE reconnect with Last-Event-ID header triggers replay from scte35_cue_points table for the last 60 seconds of cue history. Risk: ECS task scaling lag during sudden major live event traffic spikes causing SSE connection refusals. Mitigation: pre-scale ECS tasks 15 minutes before scheduled high-profile events via EventBridge scheduled rule. Tradeoff: SSE over WebSocket sacrifices bidirectional control for HTTP/2 multiplexing compatibility with partner CDN and proxy infrastructure.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Caption signal source resolved: caption availability will come from a separate caption ingest webhook POST /v1/ingest/captions in Phase 2, not from manifest scraping, to avoid polling overhead. SSE chosen over WebSocket for HTTP/2 compatibility with partner CDN proxies. Redis pub/sub chosen over in-process Axum broadcast channel to enable horizontal scaling without sticky sessions.

metadata.notes: Real-time SCTE-35 metadata enrichment API in Rust/Axum with SSE fan-out, FCC compliance audit logging, and sub-500ms p99 delivery SLA.

project_goal.value: AI-driven video search and discovery engine for a large media archive, using multimodal embeddings of video frames, speech transcripts, and metadata to enable natural-language querying across millions of archival clips
project_goal.rationale: Core value proposition is semantic search over legacy archive content

target_users.rationale: Internal professional users with deep domain knowledge

project_class.rationale: Multimodal embedding pipeline and semantic retrieval are core to the system

capabilities.rationale: Embedding generation, vector search, batch ingestion, and REST API are all required

complexity_level.rationale: Millions of archival clips with petabyte-scale storage and real-time query latency requirements

risk_level.rationale: Internal data only but embedding models risk exposing sensitive broadcast content to unauthorized queries

data_sensitivity.rationale: Archival broadcast content is proprietary but not regulated under health or financial frameworks

external_exposure.rationale: Access restricted to authenticated internal staff via Google IAP

access_model.rationale: Granular access needed to restrict licensing-grade exports

feature_scope.rationale: Complete search lifecycle from ingestion through feedback-driven model improvement

mvp_scope.value: Ingestion pipeline generating multimodal embeddings, Weaviate-backed semantic search API, archivist search UI with clip previews and timecode navigation
mvp_scope.rationale: Core search capability delivers immediate value before feedback loops are built

security_baseline.value: Google IAP for perimeter auth, OIDC token validation on every API request, no embedding vectors accessible outside internal network, audit logging of all search queries
security_baseline.rationale: Prevent exfiltration of proprietary content embeddings

backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for ML integration; FastAPI for async throughput

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for metadata + Weaviate for vector store
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for structured catalog; Weaviate for ANN vector retrieval at scale

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot with Vertex AI for embedding batch jobs
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot reduces ops overhead; Vertex AI provides managed GPU for embedding generation

privacy_retention_policy.value: Search query logs retained 180 days for model fine-tuning; relevance feedback retained indefinitely as training data; no personal data collected beyond OIDC identity
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Query retention needed for model improvement; minimal personal data footprint

future_scope.value: External licensing portal with clip basket and rights clearance workflow; multimodal search expansion to audio-only archive content
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 monetization via licensing API

constraints.value: All compute and data must remain within GCP us-central1; no external model API calls for embedding generation due to content confidentiality; Weaviate cluster must handle 50M+ vectors
constraints.rationale: Content security and data residency requirements

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for infra metrics, custom search latency and recall rate dashboards, query throughput tracking per user role
observability_baseline.rationale: Retrieval quality metrics are as important as infra health

execution_preference.value: Iterative releases with embedding model versioning; blue-green switch for model promotions
execution_preference.rationale: Model quality regressions require fast rollback capability

compliance_context.value: Internal data governance policies for archival broadcast content; GDPR for any content containing identifiable individuals in EU productions
compliance_context.rationale: Broadcast archives may contain personal likenesses subject to GDPR

llm_integration.value: Self-hosted multimodal embedding model on Vertex AI (no external API); CLIP-family model for visual embeddings; Whisper for ASR transcription feeding text encoder
llm_integration.rationale: Content confidentiality prohibits sending frames or transcripts to external APIs

project.goal: Semantic search across millions of archival broadcast clips using multimodal embeddings
project.target_users: Archivists, researchers, licensing staff
project.timeline: MVP in 16 weeks

backend.framework: FastAPI microservices on GKE Autopilot
backend.api: REST with OpenAPI 3.0 + SSE for progressive search results

security.auth: Google IAP + OIDC token validation
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, GCP CMEK for storage at rest
security.audit: All search queries logged with user identity and result clip IDs

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for catalog metadata
data.vector_store: Weaviate 1.24 with HNSW index, 50M+ vector capacity

devops.ci: Cloud Build with container image scanning
devops.hosting: GKE Autopilot, Vertex AI batch prediction

reasoner_reviews.product: Natural-language archive search eliminates the current manual metadata-only workflow that requires archivists to know exact catalog codes. Timecode-level previews are essential for clip licensing decisions and must be accurate to the frame. Relevance feedback loop is critical for domain-specific retrieval quality improvement over time.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The two prior rounds exposed a critical flaw: the ingestion pipeline writes embeddings directly to Weaviate without versioning, making model upgrades require full re-indexing with no rollback path. The fix must introduce an embedding_model_versions table and version-tagged vector namespaces in Weaviate so old and new indices can coexist during migration. PostgreSQL is correctly handling catalog metadata but the schema lacks a catalog_taxonomies join table.
reasoner_reviews.security: Query logs containing clip IDs and user identities constitute personal data under GDPR if EU productions are in the archive. Retention must be enforced via automated TTL deletion jobs, not just policy. The Weaviate cluster has no Kubernetes NetworkPolicy restricting egress, which could allow embedding exfiltration if a pod is compromised.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GKE Autopilot auto-provisioning should handle embedding job GPU requirements, but Vertex AI custom model endpoints need warm instances pre-provisioned to avoid cold-start latency on search queries. Budget for warm endpoint hours must be factored into cost projections.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 fix_report claimed to address Weaviate versioning but plan sections still described a single unversioned collection. The data_model section omitted embedding_model_versions entirely. This is a plan consistency failure that the auditor flagged as a blocking issue across both previous rounds.

backend.service_design: Four FastAPI microservices: ingest-worker (embedding pipeline), search-api (query handling), catalog-api (metadata CRUD), admin-api (taxonomy and model version management). All communicate over internal GKE service mesh.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0; search endpoint returns Server-Sent Events stream for progressive ranking updates as ANN results arrive from Weaviate
backend.business_modules: EmbeddingOrchestrator coordinates frame extraction, ASR transcription, and Vertex AI embedding calls per clip. SearchRanker fuses ANN vector scores with PostgreSQL BM25 metadata scores. RelevanceFeedbackCollector batches archivist signals into monthly fine-tuning exports.
backend.backend_risks: Weaviate ANN recall degrades at 50M+ vectors if HNSW ef_construction parameter is too low during index build. Must benchmark recall@10 vs latency curve on staging before production cutover.

security.auth_design: Google IAP terminates at GKE ingress; downstream services validate OIDC tokens and extract user roles from Google Workspace group membership on each request
security.authorization_model: Role-based: archivist (search + annotate), researcher (search only), licensing (search + export clip manifest), admin (taxonomy + model version management)
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every search query logged to Cloud Logging with user_sub, query_text, result_clip_ids, and latency. Cloud Scheduler triggers daily deletion job removing entries older than 180 days from Cloud Logging and search_queries table.
security.security_risks: Weaviate cluster lacks Kubernetes NetworkPolicy to restrict inter-pod communication; must add egress deny-all with explicit allow rules for ingest-worker and search-api pods only

data.entities: archival_clips, multimodal_embeddings (with model_version_id FK), embedding_model_versions, transcript_segments, visual_keyframes, search_queries, relevance_feedback_logs, catalog_taxonomies
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for all structured metadata; Weaviate 1.24 with separate named collections per embedding_model_version enabling zero-downtime model migration; GCS for raw video files and keyframe images
data.migration_notes: Weaviate collection per model version: ingest new version collection in parallel, validate recall@10, flip search-api routing ConfigMap, deprecate old collection after 30-day coexistence window
data.data_risks: Weaviate HNSW index memory requirement for 50M 512-d vectors at float32 is approximately 100GB; must provision high-memory node pool accordingly

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot us-central1 with general node pool (search-api, catalog-api), GPU spot pool (ingest-worker), high-memory stateful pool (Weaviate). Vertex AI custom model endpoints with minimum 2 warm replicas for query embedding.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build pipeline: unit tests, container image build, Artifact Registry push, GKE rolling deploy. Model version promotion requires manual approval gate and shadow recall evaluation job comparing new vs old Weaviate collection recall@10.
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring with custom metrics: search p50/p95 latency, ANN recall@10, embedding throughput clips/hour, relevance feedback rate per day
devops.rollback_strategy: Weaviate collection versioning enables instant search rollback by reverting routing ConfigMap. GKE deployment rollback via rollout undo for application services.

ISS-001.title: Weaviate index has no embedding model versioning
ISS-001.detail: Plan describes a single Weaviate collection with no version namespace, making model upgrades require destructive full re-indexing with no rollback path. Two rounds of fix_report entries claimed to address this but plan sections omit embedding_model_versions from data_model and versioned collection routing from deployment.

ISS-003.detail: Privacy retention policy requires 180-day query log deletion but prior plans describe this as policy only with no automated TTL deletion job defined.

previous_plan.title: Media Archive Multimodal Search Engine - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: A GKE-hosted multimodal search system using Python FastAPI microservices, Weaviate vector store, and Vertex AI self-hosted embedding models to enable natural-language retrieval across millions of archival broadcast clips.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI microservices on GKE Autopilot with Weaviate for vector search and PostgreSQL for catalog metadata. Ingest pipeline extracts keyframes, runs Whisper ASR, and generates CLIP embeddings via Vertex AI.
previous_plan.data_model: archival_clips, multimodal_embeddings (missing model_version_id), transcript_segments, visual_keyframes, search_queries, relevance_feedback_logs. embedding_model_versions table absent from schema.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot with ingest-worker, search-api, catalog-api pods. Single Weaviate collection with no version namespace. NetworkPolicy not defined.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 must concretely fix ISS-001 by adding embedding_model_versions to data_model with version_tag and weaviate_collection_name, and describing per-version collection routing in deployment. ISS-002 requires a Kubernetes NetworkPolicy deny-all egress with explicit allow rules in security and deployment sections. The plan quality is intentionally flawed: the observability section remains thin to introduce a residual quality inconsistency for the auditor to flag, making this a partially-corrected flawed plan.

Updated deployment_and_operations to describe versioned Weaviate collection routing: ingest-worker writes to active collection, search-api routing ConfigMap switches namespace on promotion, old collection deprecated after 30-day coexistence.
