reasoner_reviews.product: The free-trial sign-up form that provisions sandbox credentials directly from the landing page is a high-conversion pattern for developer-focused B2B products. Redirecting to the developer portal on success gives engineers an immediate hands-on experience. The compliance use case section targeting DRM audit trail requirements is well-positioned for the compliance lead audience who are often the second decision-maker on watermarking purchases.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 static export is correct here -- the page has no dynamic data needs and static export on Vercel gives near-instant global delivery. Use Shiki for server-side syntax highlighting during the Next.js build to avoid shipping a highlighting library to the client, which keeps the bundle small and meets the 2-second mobile load constraint. The form should POST to the provisioning API with a loading state and redirect on 201 response.
reasoner_reviews.security: The GDPR consent banner must gate GA4 script initialization. The provisioning API call from the form goes to an external domain; CSP must allowlist that domain. The provisioning API should validate the email domain against a blocklist of disposable email providers before issuing sandbox credentials -- this is the provisioning service's responsibility, not the landing page.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 2-second mobile load time requires: Next.js static export (no server-side rendering latency), image optimization via next/image, Shiki server-side syntax highlighting (no client-side JS), font self-hosting to avoid Google Fonts round trip, and deferred GA4 script loading until after consent. Vercel Edge Network delivery from a nearby PoP handles the CDN latency component.

security.auth_design: No authentication on the landing page; fully public
security.abuse_prevention: Provisioning API (external service) is responsible for rate limiting trial sign-ups per email domain and blocking disposable email addresses; landing page form performs basic client-side email format validation only
security.privacy_controls: GDPR consent banner using a lightweight custom implementation blocks GA4 gtag.js initialization until explicit accept; consent state stored in localStorage; Vercel Analytics is cookie-free and does not require consent
security.security_risks: Risk: trial sign-up form submits email to external provisioning API from client -- if the provisioning API endpoint changes, the form breaks silently. Mitigation: versioned provisioning API URL in environment variable; Vercel preview deployments catch breakage before production.

data.entities: product_features (feature_name STRING, available BOOL, competitors [{name, available BOOL}] -- /src/data/features.ts), integration_snippets (language ENUM python/nodejs/java, title STRING, code TEXT -- /src/data/snippets.ts with Shiki pre-highlighted HTML), pricing_plans (plan_name STRING, price_usd_monthly INT, watermark_limit INT, support_tier STRING -- /src/data/pricing.ts), use_case_sections (id STRING, title STRING, description TEXT, compliance_standard STRING e.g. AACS/Marlin -- /src/data/usecases.ts), trial_signup_forms (email STRING, company STRING, intended_use STRING -- ephemeral; POSTed to provisioning API)
data.storage_design: All content in TypeScript data files in /src/data/; no database; syntax-highlighted code snippets pre-processed by Shiki at build time and stored as static HTML strings in data files

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions: next build (with NEXT_OUTPUT=export), vercel deploy CLI. Vercel Edge Network serves static output from /out directory.
devops.environments: Production: watermark-sdk.example.com on main; Preview: auto-generated Vercel URLs per PR branch
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions steps: bun install, bun run build (next build --export), vercel deploy for PR or vercel deploy --prod for main branch
devops.cost_controls: Vercel Pro ~$20/month; GA4 free; Vercel Analytics included in Vercel Pro

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export single-page marketing site; anchor navigation; syntax-highlighted integration snippets server-rendered at build time
frontend.pages_and_flows: Hero (watermarking use case positioning), Integration Code Samples, Pricing Table, Customer Testimonials, SDK Download / Trial CTA
frontend.performance_notes: Code blocks server-rendered with Shiki for zero client JS; pricing table static HTML; all images use WebP with responsive srcset
frontend.frontend_risks: SDK trial CTA must link to authenticated developer portal; avoid exposing trial license keys in client-side code or URL params

target_output.thinking_summary: Static Next.js 14 landing page for a B2B video watermarking SDK. Key decisions: Shiki server-side syntax highlighting during build to avoid client-side bundle cost while meeting 2-second mobile load constraint; GDPR consent banner gating GA4 without a third-party CMP to keep JS payload minimal; form submits to external provisioning API and redirects to developer portal on 201 response. Quality target is moderate -- most sections are solid but a few are somewhat thinner.
target_output.title: B2B Video Watermarking SDK Landing Page Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 static export landing page hosted on Vercel Edge Network for a B2B video watermarking SDK product. The page features server-side syntax-highlighted integration code snippets for Python and Node.js, compliance use case sections for DRM forensic watermarking and audit trail requirements, a feature comparison table, and a free-trial sign-up form that calls an internal provisioning API to create sandbox credentials and redirects to the developer portal. GDPR cookie consent gates GA4 initialization for EU visitors. The entire page delivers as optimized static files with no server-side runtime.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 configured with output: export generates a fully static site from TypeScript content files and MDX. Shiki processes all integration code snippets at build time, producing syntax-highlighted HTML stored as static strings in the data layer -- no client-side JavaScript highlighting library ships to the browser. The free-trial form POSTs to an external provisioning API at the watermarking SDK platform; on 201 response the form component redirects the browser to the developer portal. GA4 gtag.js is loaded only after the GDPR consent banner accept event fires, with consent state in localStorage. Vercel Edge Network delivers the static output globally with automatic cache invalidation on each deploy. GitHub Actions runs the build and deploys to Vercel on merge to main; PR branches receive preview URLs for content review.
target_output.technology_stack: Framework: Next.js 14 with output: export, TypeScript, React 18. Styling: Tailwind CSS. Syntax Highlighting: Shiki (server-side at build time, zero client JS). Forms: React controlled component with fetch to provisioning API. Consent: custom lightweight GDPR banner (~2KB, no CMP vendor). Analytics: Vercel Analytics (cookie-free) and GA4 (post-consent). CI: GitHub Actions with bun and Vercel CLI. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network with custom domain.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: headline positioning forensic watermarking for content protection, primary CTA button anchoring to the sign-up form. Integration Snippets Section: tabbed panels showing Shiki-highlighted Python (requests), Node.js (axios), and Java examples for embedding a watermark into a video stream via the SDK. Compliance Use Cases Section: DRM Forensic Watermarking for detecting leaked pre-release content, Audit Trail for studio and OTT regulatory reporting, Content Provenance for broadcast chain-of-custody. Feature Comparison Table: compares watermark invisibility threshold, extraction accuracy percentage, supported formats, and SDK language support against two incumbent solutions. Free-Trial Sign-Up Form: email and company fields, intended use dropdown, POST to provisioning API, redirect to developer portal on success. Cookie Consent Banner: gates GA4 gtag.js until explicit accept; respects navigator.doNotTrack.
target_output.system_components: Next.js Build: generates /out static directory with syntax-highlighted snippet HTML embedded. Vercel Edge Network: global CDN delivery of /out files with TLS termination. Provisioning API (external): https://api.watermark-sdk.example.com/v1/trials -- creates sandbox API credentials on POST, returns 201 with redirect URL. Developer Portal (external): destination after successful trial signup. GA4: loaded post-consent for CTA click and form submission event tracking. Vercel Analytics: cookie-free page view and Core Web Vitals collection.
target_output.workflows: Trial Sign-Up Workflow: visitor reads integration snippets and compliance use cases; fills the sign-up form with email, company, and intended_use; React component calls fetch('https://api.watermark-sdk.example.com/v1/trials', {method: POST, body: JSON.stringify({email, company, intended_use})}); on 201 response, component reads the redirect_url from the response body and calls window.location.href = redirect_url, landing the developer in the sandbox portal with credentials pre-populated; GA4 fires a trial_signup_completed event if consent was given. Content Update Workflow: developer updates a code snippet in /src/data/snippets.ts; Shiki re-processes the new snippet to syntax-highlighted HTML during the Next.js build; GitHub Actions deploys the updated static files to Vercel; changes are live within 3 minutes of merge.
target_output.data_model: product_features (/src/data/features.ts: feature_name STRING, our_value STRING, competitor_a_value STRING, competitor_b_value STRING e.g. watermark_invisibility_db: '-38 dB / -35 dB / -30 dB'). integration_snippets (/src/data/snippets.ts: language ENUM python/nodejs/java, title STRING, code_raw TEXT, code_html TEXT generated by Shiki at build). use_case_sections (/src/data/usecases.ts: id STRING, title STRING, description TEXT, compliance_standards STRING[] e.g. ['AACS', 'HDCP', 'Marlin'], icon_name STRING). pricing_plans (/src/data/pricing.ts: plan_name STRING, watermarks_per_month INT, extraction_accuracy_pct FLOAT, support_tier STRING, price_usd_monthly INT). trial_signup_forms (ephemeral React state: email, company, intended_use ENUM ott/studio/broadcast/other -- POSTed to provisioning API, not persisted on page).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API on the landing page. Single external API call from the form component: POST https://api.watermark-sdk.example.com/v1/trials with JSON body {email: string, company: string, intended_use: string}. Expected response on success: 201 {trial_id: string, redirect_url: string, sandbox_dashboard_url: string}. On 4xx: form shows inline error message. On 5xx or network error: form shows retry message. CSP connect-src must allowlist https://api.watermark-sdk.example.com.
target_output.security_and_compliance: CSP: default-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' https://api.watermark-sdk.example.com https://www.googletagmanager.com; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://www.googletagmanager.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'. HSTS: max-age=31536000. GDPR: GA4 gtag.js tag blocked until explicit consent accept event; consent stored in localStorage key watermark_analytics_consent; Vercel Analytics is consent-free and always active. Form submission: email submitted to provisioning API from client; no data stored on the page or in Vercel infrastructure.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions on push to main or PR: bun install, bun run build (NEXT_OUTPUT=export next build), for PR branches vercel deploy outputs preview URL posted to PR comment, for main branch vercel deploy --prod. Rollback: Vercel dashboard one-click rollback to prior deployment. Environment variables: NEXT_PUBLIC_PROVISIONING_API_URL stored in Vercel project settings. Domain: watermark-sdk.example.com with Vercel-managed TLS.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics: Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) per page, device type and geographic breakdown, cookie-free. GA4 (post-consent): events fired -- cta_button_click (hero CTA), snippet_tab_changed (language switch), trial_form_started, trial_signup_completed (with intended_use parameter), trial_signup_failed. Marketing team uses GA4 funnel report to track hero CTA click-to-trial conversion rate. Vercel build logs retained in dashboard for 30 days.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro: ~$20/month. GA4: free. No database or compute cost. Total: approximately $20/month regardless of traffic volume. Vercel Edge Network auto-scales to any traffic level; no capacity planning required for a static page.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Launch (3 weeks): Next.js 14 static export scaffold with Tailwind brand theme, hero section with primary CTA, Python and Node.js Shiki-highlighted integration snippet tabs, compliance use case sections for DRM forensic watermarking and audit trail, free-trial sign-up form with provisioning API call and developer portal redirect, GDPR consent banner, Vercel Analytics and GA4 integration, GitHub Actions CI with Vercel deploy. Phase 2 - Content Expansion (2 weeks): Java code snippet tab, feature comparison table, pricing plan section, CTA conversion tracking in GA4. Phase 3 - Interactive Demo (future): WebAssembly-based watermark strength demonstration allowing visitors to test invisible watermark embedding and extraction on a sample video clip in the browser.
target_output.development_guidelines: All code snippets authored as raw strings in /src/data/snippets.ts and pre-processed by Shiki in a build script before next build runs; never use a client-side highlighting library. NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefixed environment variables only for values safe to expose to the browser; provisioning API URL uses NEXT_PUBLIC_PROVISIONING_API_URL. Tailwind purge must include /src/data/ files if any class names are constructed there. GA4 initialization must be gated behind consent banner accept; never initialize unconditionally. Form component must show loading spinner and disable submit button during provisioning API call to prevent double submissions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: provisioning API downtime causes free-trial sign-up failures during peak traffic periods like conference launches. Mitigation: form shows a friendly error with an alternative email contact link; provisioning API SLA is the responsibility of the backend team. Risk: Shiki build step adds 10-30 seconds to the build time as snippet count grows. Mitigation: cache the Shiki processing output in the CI build cache keyed by snippets.ts hash. Tradeoff: custom lightweight GDPR banner over a CMP vendor saves 30-50KB of JavaScript but requires manual maintenance if consent requirements change -- acceptable for a single-page site with infrequent regulatory updates.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Shiki over Prism.js for server-side highlighting because Shiki uses VS Code's grammar engine and ships zero client JavaScript. Confirmed Next.js static export over Astro based on team familiarity. Chose custom consent banner implementation over OneTrust or Cookiebot to avoid third-party script overhead on a performance-sensitive landing page. Confirmed direct provisioning API call from form component over a Vercel Edge Function proxy since the provisioning API is not sensitive and the API URL is acceptable to expose in the client.

metadata.notes: Static Next.js landing page for B2B video watermarking SDK with Shiki syntax highlighting, GDPR consent, and provisioning API free-trial sign-up.

project_goal.value: Automation tool that analyzes a streaming platform's video storage and CDN costs, identifies cold or rarely-watched content, and automatically tiers assets to cheaper storage classes to reduce infrastructure spend
project_goal.rationale: Engineering team identified $200K+ annual savings opportunity by tiering assets not accessed in 60+ days

target_users.value: Platform engineering team and content operations team monitoring storage cost dashboards
target_users.rationale: Internal tool with no external users; human oversight of tiering decisions is required

project_class.rationale: Scheduled batch jobs with policy engine and cost reporting, no user-facing product features

capabilities.rationale: Python Celery workers, PostgreSQL, S3 API integration, CloudWatch analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Batch job orchestration with policy evaluation and S3 lifecycle API; no real-time or ML components

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect tiering decisions could render playback assets temporarily unavailable; automated S3 moves require careful validation

data_sensitivity.rationale: Content asset metadata is internal business data; no personal or financial data processed

external_exposure.rationale: Runs as internal automation with IAM role access; no external API exposure

access_model.value: IAM roles for S3, CloudWatch, and CDN API access; no human login to the tool itself; cost reports accessible via internal dashboard
access_model.rationale: Machine identity via IAM roles; human access limited to read-only cost dashboards

feature_scope.value: S3 access log and CDN analytics ingestion, per-asset view frequency computation over rolling windows, tiering policy engine with configurable rules, S3 lifecycle API tiering execution, CDN metadata update on tier change, monthly cost attribution report with savings calculation, rehydration request handling
feature_scope.rationale: Complete cost optimization lifecycle from access pattern analysis through tiering and reporting

mvp_scope.value: Nightly access frequency computation from S3 logs, tiering policy evaluation for S3 Standard to Glacier Instant Retrieval moves, automated S3 lifecycle transitions, weekly cost report email
mvp_scope.rationale: Core tiering automation delivers savings without full CDN metadata and rehydration handling in V1

security_baseline.value: IAM role with least-privilege S3 permissions (s3:GetObject, s3:PutLifecycleConfiguration per bucket), no S3 DeleteObject permission, CloudTrail logging of all S3 API calls, tiering decisions logged before execution
security_baseline.rationale: Least-privilege IAM prevents accidental or unauthorized deletions; audit trail required for cost accountability

frontend_stack.value: No frontend; internal automation tool with no browser UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Automation tool with CLI and email reports only; no web dashboard in scope

backend_stack.rationale: Python for boto3 AWS SDK integration; Celery for distributed batch job orchestration with Redis broker

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for access_frequency_records, tiering_decisions, and cost_attribution_reports with time-series query patterns

hosting_target.value: ECS Fargate
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers for Celery workers with CloudWatch Events triggers for scheduled jobs

privacy_retention_policy.value: Access frequency records retained 1 year; tiering decision logs retained indefinitely for audit; cost attribution reports retained 3 years for finance reconciliation
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Finance team requires multi-year cost attribution history for budget planning

future_scope.value: Machine learning model to predict content viewership decay curves for proactive tiering; CDN pre-positioning recommendations based on predicted demand spikes
future_scope.rationale: ML-driven tiering could capture additional 30-40% savings beyond rule-based thresholds

constraints.value: No S3 DeleteObject permissions; tiering decisions must be reversible; all changes logged before execution; rehydration from Glacier must complete within 12 hours for content ops requests
constraints.rationale: Content availability SLA requires that no content be permanently removed and cold storage retrieval is manageable

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for Celery worker metrics and job completion events, job failure alerts via PagerDuty, tiering execution logs in CloudWatch Logs, cost savings dashboard in AWS Cost Explorer
observability_baseline.rationale: Engineering team monitors via CloudWatch; cost team monitors via Cost Explorer integration

execution_preference.value: Nightly batch jobs triggered by CloudWatch Events; weekly and monthly report jobs scheduled separately
execution_preference.rationale: Batch schedule aligns with S3 log delivery cadence and reporting cycles

compliance_context.value: Internal data governance for content asset metadata; SOC 2 Type II change management controls require tiering decisions to be logged and reviewable
compliance_context.rationale: SOC 2 change management scope includes automated changes to production storage assets

project.goal: Automated content storage tiering to reduce S3 and CDN costs for rarely-accessed catalog assets
project.target_users: Platform engineering and content operations teams
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks

backend.framework: Celery 5 with Redis broker
backend.api: No external API; internal Celery task dispatch only

security.auth: IAM roles with least-privilege S3 and CloudWatch permissions
security.encryption: S3 SSE-S3 on all buckets; TLS 1.3 in transit
security.audit: All tiering decisions logged to PostgreSQL and CloudWatch before execution

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for access_frequency_records, tiering_decisions, cost_attribution_reports
data.cache: Redis 7 as Celery broker and result backend

constraints.reversible: All tiering moves reversible via lifecycle transition back
constraints.log_before_execute: Decision logged before S3 API call

reasoner_reviews.product: The core value proposition is clear: rule-based tiering of cold content generates immediate cost savings with low operational risk. The monthly cost attribution report is essential for finance team buy-in and must show actual savings versus projected, not just projected. Rehydration request handling ensures content operations can recover tiered assets within SLA without manual S3 console work.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Round 1 introduced the tiering policy engine but the data model lacked the rehydration_requests entity, which is necessary to track content operations requests to restore tiered assets within the 12-hour SLA. The access_frequency_records table also needs a storage_class_current column so the policy engine can skip assets already on Glacier without redundant S3 API calls. These gaps must be addressed in round 2.
reasoner_reviews.security: The IAM role must not have s3:DeleteObject under any circumstances. The round 1 security section described this correctly but the development_guidelines did not include a CI check to validate that the Terraform IAM policy never introduces delete permissions. Adding a rego policy check in CI would provide a strong control.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 12-hour rehydration SLA requires that rehydration_requests trigger an S3 RestoreObject API call for Glacier Instant Retrieval immediately upon request, not batch-processed. Glacier Instant Retrieval has millisecond retrieval latency for the first byte but the restore request must be explicit; the automation must handle this path.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 also omitted cdn_usage_logs from the data model despite it being in the manifest key_entities. The access frequency computation relies on both S3 access logs and CDN analytics to get an accurate picture of content demand; omitting CDN logs means cold content frequently accessed via CDN would be incorrectly tiered.

backend.service_design: Four Celery task groups: access_analyzer (parse S3 and CDN logs, update access_frequency_records), policy_evaluator (evaluate tiering rules against frequency records), tiering_executor (execute S3 lifecycle transitions after logging tiering_decisions), report_generator (compute cost attribution and savings). Tasks are idempotent and can be re-run safely.
backend.api_patterns: No external API. Internal: Celery task dispatch via Redis broker. EventBridge triggers ECS Fargate run-task for each scheduled job. Cost report delivered via SES email with attached PDF.
backend.business_modules: AccessFrequencyComputer aggregates S3 server access logs and CloudFront access logs to compute 30/60/90-day rolling window view counts per asset. TieringPolicyEngine evaluates each content_asset against tiering_policies using configurable threshold rules. StorageTieringExecutor calls S3 PutLifecycleConfiguration and logs tiering_decisions before execution. RehydrationHandler processes rehydration_requests and calls S3 RestoreObject for Glacier assets.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly at 02:00 UTC: access_frequency_computation Celery task parses prior day S3 and CDN logs. Nightly at 03:00 UTC: policy_evaluation task evaluates all assets and enqueues qualifying tiering_execution tasks. Weekly Sunday 06:00 UTC: weekly_cost_report task. Monthly first day 07:00 UTC: monthly_attribution_report task.

security.auth_design: ECS task IAM role with inline policy: s3:GetObject, s3:ListBucket, s3:PutLifecycleConfiguration, s3:RestoreObject per content bucket; cloudwatch:PutMetricData; ses:SendEmail for reports. Explicitly deny s3:DeleteObject.
security.authorization_model: IAM role boundary enforces least privilege. No human login to the automation. Cost report email recipients managed via SSM Parameter Store list.
security.secrets_management: Database credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation; injected into ECS task as environment variables via ECS secrets integration.
security.security_risks: S3 lifecycle transitions are irreversible in the short term (Glacier restore takes milliseconds but requires explicit RestoreObject call); policy engine bugs could tier active content. Mitigation: dry-run mode for policy evaluation that logs decisions without executing; mandatory dry-run review before enabling live tiering.

data.entities: content_assets, storage_tier_policies, access_frequency_records, cost_attribution_reports, tiering_decisions, cdn_usage_logs, rehydration_requests, savings_projections
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for all structured data. S3 server access logs and CloudFront access logs delivered to a dedicated analysis S3 bucket; log parsing Celery tasks read from this bucket and write aggregated counts to PostgreSQL.
data.schema_notes: access_frequency_records.view_count_30d, view_count_60d, view_count_90d pre-computed nightly. content_assets.storage_class_current column updated after each tiering execution to avoid redundant S3 API calls in policy evaluation.
data.data_risks: CloudFront access log delivery can be delayed up to 24 hours; access frequency computation must handle late-arriving logs by using a 2-day lookback window to avoid undercounting demand for recently popular content

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate in us-east-1: Celery worker tasks launched on-demand by EventBridge run-task triggers. Redis 7 on ElastiCache for Celery broker. PostgreSQL on RDS in Multi-AZ. No always-on worker fleet; tasks run to completion and terminate.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with coverage gate 80%, Terraform plan validation including OPA rego check that IAM policy never contains s3:DeleteObject, container build, ECR push, ECS task definition update.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Logs for all Celery task stdout/stderr. CloudWatch custom metrics: assets_tiered_count, tiering_cost_savings_usd, rehydration_sla_met_rate. PagerDuty alert for Celery task failure and rehydration SLA breach.
devops.rollback_strategy: Tiering decisions logged before execution; rollback via S3 RestoreObject for Glacier assets or manual S3 CopyObject to reinstate Standard class for recently tiered assets. No automated rollback; content ops team executes manual restoration with decision log as reference.

ISS-001.title: rehydration_requests entity missing from data model
ISS-001.detail: The 12-hour rehydration SLA requires tracking content operations requests to restore tiered assets, but rehydration_requests table was absent from the round 1 data_model. Without it, the RehydrationHandler has no persistent state for SLA tracking.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner in round 1 review

ISS-002.title: cdn_usage_logs omitted from data model and access frequency computation
ISS-002.detail: Access frequency computation in round 1 relied only on S3 access logs, omitting CloudFront CDN analytics. Content accessed frequently via CDN but rarely directly from S3 would be incorrectly identified as cold and tiered.

history.note: Identified by critic in round 1 review

target_output.title: Streaming Platform Storage Cost Tiering Automation
target_output.executive_summary: A Python Celery automation tool on ECS Fargate that analyzes S3 server access logs and CloudFront access logs to compute per-asset view frequency over rolling 30/60/90-day windows, evaluates configurable tiering policies to identify cold content, automatically executes S3 lifecycle transitions to cheaper storage classes, and generates monthly cost attribution reports quantifying realized savings. Rehydration requests from content operations are handled with 12-hour SLA tracking.
target_output.architecture_overview: Stateless Python Celery workers launched on-demand by EventBridge scheduled rules running on ECS Fargate in us-east-1. Four task chains: access_analyzer parses S3 server access logs and CloudFront standard logs delivered to a dedicated analysis S3 bucket, aggregating per-asset view counts into PostgreSQL access_frequency_records. policy_evaluator queries access_frequency_records against storage_tier_policies to identify qualifying assets. tiering_executor logs tiering_decisions to PostgreSQL then calls S3 PutLifecycleConfiguration to move assets to Glacier Instant Retrieval or S3 Intelligent-Tiering. report_generator computes monthly cost attribution from tiering_decisions and CDN billing exports. Redis on ElastiCache serves as Celery broker and result backend. No always-on worker processes; ECS tasks spin up for scheduled jobs and terminate on completion.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, Celery 5, boto3 AWS SDK, psycopg3, pandas for log aggregation. Broker: Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate us-east-1, EventBridge scheduled rules, S3 analysis bucket for log delivery, SES for report emails. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with OPA rego IAM policy checks. Observability: CloudWatch Logs, CloudWatch custom metrics, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Access Frequency Analysis: nightly parsing of S3 server access logs and CloudFront standard logs, merging per-asset view counts for 30, 60, and 90-day rolling windows stored in access_frequency_records. Tiering Policy Engine: configurable rules such as 'tier to Glacier Instant Retrieval if view_count_90d < 5 and storage_class_current = standard'; rules stored in storage_tier_policies table and editable by engineering team. Automated Tiering Execution: tiering_decisions logged before S3 PutLifecycleConfiguration execution; CDN metadata flag updated to reflect retrieval latency change. Rehydration Handling: content operations team submits rehydration_requests via internal CLI; RehydrationHandler calls S3 RestoreObject immediately and polls until restore completes or SLA deadline passes. Cost Attribution Report: monthly computation of actual savings comparing current storage costs against pre-tiering baseline with per-content-type breakdown.
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate Task Fleet: on-demand Celery worker containers launched by EventBridge run-task triggers for each scheduled job. Redis 7 ElastiCache: Celery broker and result backend. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ: persistent store for all frequency records, tiering decisions, and reports. S3 Analysis Bucket: receives S3 server access log deliveries from content buckets and CloudFront standard log deliveries; analysis tasks read from this bucket. S3 Content Buckets: target of PutLifecycleConfiguration tiering calls. EventBridge Scheduler: triggers nightly access_analysis at 02:00 UTC, nightly policy_evaluation at 03:00 UTC, weekly report at 06:00 UTC Sunday, monthly attribution report first of month. SES: delivers cost attribution report emails to engineering and finance distribution lists.
target_output.workflows: Access Frequency Computation Workflow: EventBridge triggers access_analyzer Celery task at 02:00 UTC. Task lists S3 server access log objects for prior day from analysis bucket, downloads and parses each log file extracting content_asset_id and request count. Downloads CloudFront standard log files for the same day from the CDN log prefix, parses edge pop request counts per content_asset_id. Merges S3 and CDN view counts per asset, updates access_frequency_records with rolling 30/60/90-day window counts using an UPSERT keyed on content_asset_id and log_date. Updates content_assets.storage_class_current for any assets detected as having changed class. Tiering Execution Workflow: policy_evaluator task queries content_assets joined to access_frequency_records where view_count_90d < policy.threshold and storage_class_current = policy.source_class. For each qualifying asset, inserts a tiering_decisions record with status=pending. tiering_executor task picks up pending decisions, calls S3 PutLifecycleConfiguration to transition the asset, updates tiering_decisions.status=executed and content_assets.storage_class_current, writes CloudWatch metric for assets_tiered_count. Rehydration Workflow: content operations engineer runs internal CLI command submit-rehydration --asset-id and reason. CLI creates rehydration_requests record with sla_deadline_at = requested_at + 12h. RehydrationHandler Celery task calls S3 RestoreObject for the asset immediately. Polls restore status every 30 minutes by calling S3 HeadObject and checking x-amz-restore header. On restore completion updates restore_completed_at and status=complete. If sla_deadline_at passes before restore completes, updates status=sla_breached and sends PagerDuty P2 alert.
target_output.data_model: content_assets (id UUID PK, s3_key VARCHAR UNIQUE, bucket_name VARCHAR, title VARCHAR, duration_seconds INT, storage_class_current ENUM standard/intelligent_tiering/glacier_instant/glacier_flexible, last_accessed_at, created_at). access_frequency_records (id UUID PK, content_asset_id FK, log_date DATE, view_count_s3 INT, view_count_cdn INT, view_count_total INT, bandwidth_bytes_total BIGINT). cdn_usage_logs (id UUID PK, content_asset_id FK, cdn_edge_pop VARCHAR, view_count INT, bandwidth_bytes BIGINT, log_date DATE). storage_tier_policies (id UUID PK, policy_name VARCHAR, source_class ENUM, target_class ENUM, threshold_view_count_90d INT, enabled BOOLEAN, updated_by VARCHAR, updated_at). tiering_decisions (id UUID PK, content_asset_id FK, policy_id FK, decided_at, executed_at, source_class ENUM, target_class ENUM, status ENUM pending/executed/failed, savings_usd_monthly DECIMAL). rehydration_requests (id UUID PK, content_asset_id FK, requested_by VARCHAR, reason TEXT, requested_at TIMESTAMP, sla_deadline_at TIMESTAMP, restore_completed_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM pending/restoring/complete/sla_breached). cost_attribution_reports (id UUID PK, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, total_assets_tiered INT, projected_monthly_savings_usd DECIMAL, actual_savings_usd DECIMAL, generated_at, s3_report_key VARCHAR). savings_projections (id UUID PK, content_asset_id FK, current_monthly_cost_usd DECIMAL, projected_tiered_cost_usd DECIMAL, computed_at).
target_output.api_design: No external REST or gRPC API. Internal interfaces: Celery task dispatch via Redis broker using task names (access_analyzer.compute_frequency, policy_evaluator.evaluate_all, tiering_executor.execute_pending, report_generator.generate_monthly). CLI tool for content operations: storage-tier-cli with subcommands: submit-rehydration --asset-id --reason, list-tiering-decisions --status --date-range, get-cost-report --month, dry-run-policy --policy-id. CLI reads PostgreSQL directly via read-only IAM database user. EventBridge rules invoke ECS run-task with task override environment variables specifying job scope.
target_output.security_and_compliance: ECS task IAM role with explicitly scoped inline policy: s3:GetObject, s3:ListBucket, s3:PutLifecycleConfiguration, s3:RestoreObject, s3:HeadObject on content buckets; s3:GetObject, s3:ListBucket on analysis log bucket; cloudwatch:PutMetricData; ses:SendEmail; secretsmanager:GetSecretValue for database credentials. s3:DeleteObject explicitly denied via IAM Deny statement. Terraform OPA rego check in GitHub Actions CI validates that IAM policy document never contains s3:DeleteObject. Tiering decisions logged to PostgreSQL before any S3 API call is made; if S3 call fails, decision record updated to status=failed for audit trail. Database credentials in Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation via RDS Lambda rotation function. CloudTrail logging enabled on all S3 content buckets to capture lifecycle configuration changes. SOC 2 Type II change management: tiering decisions constitute automated changes to production assets and are logged in tiering_decisions table with policy reference and timestamp.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate us-east-1: task definitions for access_analyzer, policy_evaluator, tiering_executor, and report_generator Celery tasks. Each task definition specifies CPU 0.5 vCPU and 1GB memory. EventBridge Scheduler rules: access_analysis cron(0 2 * * ? *), policy_evaluation cron(0 3 * * ? *), weekly_report cron(0 6 ? * SUN *), monthly_report cron(0 7 1 * ? *). GitHub Actions CI: pytest with 80% coverage gate, Terraform validate and plan, OPA rego IAM policy check (deny s3:DeleteObject guard), Docker build, ECR push, ECS task definition registration. Staging environment runs weekly dry-run jobs against a subset of content assets to validate policy logic before production execution. Manual dry-run mode via CLI flag available to engineering team for ad-hoc policy testing.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Logs group per ECS task with structured JSON logging including job_id, task_name, content_asset_id, action, result, and duration_ms fields. CloudWatch custom metrics namespace storage-tier-optimizer: assets_analyzed_count (daily), assets_tiered_count (daily), tiering_failures_count (daily), rehydration_sla_met_rate (weekly), monthly_savings_usd (monthly). PagerDuty alerts: Celery task failure (P2), rehydration SLA breach (P2), S3 API error rate above 5% in tiering execution (P1). AWS Cost Explorer integration: tagged resources with project=storage-tier-optimizer to isolate tool operational cost from storage savings measurement.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate spot pricing for batch tasks reduces compute cost approximately 70% versus on-demand; nightly jobs complete in under 30 minutes for a catalog of 500K assets, approximately $12 per month in compute. ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.micro at $18 per month. RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium at $85 per month. Total tool operational cost approximately $120 per month against projected $200K+ annual savings from tiering. At catalog scale of 2M assets, processing time scales linearly with S3 log volume; Celery task fan-out supports parallel log parsing to maintain sub-1-hour nightly completion.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): S3 server access log and CloudFront log parsing, access_frequency_records with 30/60/90-day rolling windows, tiering policy engine with configurable thresholds, automated S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval tiering execution with pre-execution logging, cdn_usage_logs entity and merged frequency computation, rehydration_requests with S3 RestoreObject and 12-hour SLA tracking, weekly cost summary email via SES. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Monthly cost attribution report with actual vs projected savings breakdown, savings_projections precomputation, Terraform OPA IAM guard in CI, CloudWatch metrics and PagerDuty alerting. Phase 3 (4 weeks): S3 Intelligent-Tiering policy support for partially-cold assets, dry-run CLI mode for policy validation, CDN metadata flag update on tier change, staging dry-run weekly validation job.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints enforced via mypy in CI. Celery tasks must be idempotent: duplicate EventBridge triggers must not result in duplicate tiering decisions for the same asset. All S3 API calls wrapped in retry logic with exponential backoff using boto3 retry configuration max_attempts=3. PostgreSQL schema migrations via Alembic with version-numbered migration scripts. No pandas DataFrames persisted to PostgreSQL directly; use psycopg3 COPY for bulk access_frequency_records upserts. OPA rego policy in infra/policies/iam_no_delete.rego enforced in GitHub Actions Terraform plan job.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CloudFront access log delivery delay of up to 24 hours means nightly frequency computation may undercount demand for very recently popular content. Mitigation: use 2-day lookback window for CDN log parsing and add 10% demand buffer to tiering thresholds for Standard-to-Glacier decisions. Risk: tiering_executor fails mid-batch leaving some assets tiered and some not from the same policy evaluation run. Mitigation: idempotent task retry via Celery task_id and tiering_decisions status tracking; re-run picks up remaining pending decisions. Tradeoff: ECS Fargate spot instances may be interrupted during nightly jobs; mitigated by Celery task checkpointing via Redis result backend. Risk: Glacier Instant Retrieval restore for a 10GB asset takes milliseconds for first byte but full object access requires restore to complete; content operations must account for this in SLA expectations beyond the 12-hour tracking window.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval over S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval to maintain content operations SLA of 12 hours; Flexible Retrieval restore can take 3-5 hours vs milliseconds for Instant. CloudFront standard logs chosen over real-time logs because standard logs are sufficient for 24-hour frequency computation and cost significantly less. Celery chosen over AWS Step Functions to keep operational complexity low and leverage existing Python AWS SDK expertise on the team.

metadata.notes: Strong round 2 revision resolving both data model gaps. CDN log merging and rehydration SLA tracking fully addressed with domain-specific content throughout.

project_goal.value: Multi-tenant AI content moderation platform for video streaming services, using fine-tuned computer vision and audio models to detect policy violations in near-real-time across uploaded user-generated content
project_goal.rationale: Core product: provide streaming partners with automated UGC moderation to reduce human review volume while meeting DSA transparency obligations

target_users.value: Streaming platform partners submitting UGC for moderation via API; trust-and-safety teams using the human review console; platform operations teams managing model versions and policy rule sets
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user personas with different access levels confirmed in discovery

project_class.rationale: Core product is an ML inference pipeline with multi-tenant orchestration, model lifecycle management, and a human-in-the-loop review workflow

capabilities.value: backend, data, ai_llm, batch_jobs, integrations, public_api, admin_panel, analytics
capabilities.rationale: FastAPI microservices, PostgreSQL/Redis, vision/audio LLM inference, nightly retraining batch, partner API, moderator console, ops admin, DSA analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-tenant inference at scale, model A/B shadow evaluation, concurrent human review queues across multiple partners, DSA transparency reporting

risk_level.rationale: Processes partner UGC under DSA obligations; incorrect moderation decisions carry legal liability; confidential partner policy configurations must not leak across tenants

data_sensitivity.rationale: Partner-specific policy rule sets and moderation verdicts are commercially confidential; cross-tenant isolation is a contractual requirement

external_exposure.rationale: Public API exposed to streaming platform partners; moderator console and admin panel are internal to the platform operator

access_model.value: Multi-tenant RBAC: partner API key with tenant_id scope, moderator role per tenant (human review queue access), platform admin role (model management, DSA reporting, tenant config), read-only analyst role for reporting
access_model.rationale: Strict tenant isolation required; platform admin must not access partner-specific content without explicit audit trail

feature_scope.value: Partner API for video asset submission, parallel visual/audio/metadata inference pipeline, confidence-threshold routing to auto-action or human review queue, moderator review console, appeal management, model version management, A/B shadow evaluation, DSA transparency report generation, per-tenant analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set confirmed in partner contract requirements

mvp_scope.value: Partner API for asset submission, visual and audio classifier inference pipeline, auto-action above confidence threshold, human review queue with basic moderator console, per-tenant policy rule set configuration
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP sufficient for first partner pilot with one active partner tenant

security_baseline.value: mTLS for partner API connections, Auth0 SAML federation for moderator console SSO, tenant_id row-level security on all data tables, audit log for every moderation verdict and human review action, secrets in AWS Secrets Manager, WAF on partner API ALB
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API with confidential multi-tenant data requires transport-layer and data-layer isolation controls

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript (moderator review console and admin panel)
frontend_stack.rationale: React 18 with Auth0 SAML SSO integration; moderator console requires real-time queue updates via WebSocket

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async performance for high-throughput inference orchestration; Python ecosystem for ML model serving and data processing

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7
data_platform.rationale: Postgres with RLS for multi-tenant data isolation; Redis for job queue, inference result caching, and human review queue state

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for API services plus EC2 G5 instances for GPU inference
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate for stateless API microservices; G5 instances (NVIDIA A10G) for vision and audio model inference with cost-efficient spot capacity for batch retraining

privacy_retention_policy.value: Content submissions retained 90 days for appeal window; moderation verdicts retained 3 years for DSA transparency reporting; human review actions retained 5 years for audit; raw video frames used for inference deleted immediately after scoring
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: DSA Article 17 requires appeal preservation; GDPR data minimization requires deleting raw inference inputs

future_scope.value: Real-time stream moderation for live content, multimodal content hashing for CSAM detection, federated model training across partner datasets without centralizing raw content
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items contingent on successful platform launch and regulatory guidance

constraints.value: P95 inference latency under 10 seconds per video asset; tenant data must be fully isolated with no cross-tenant query possible; GPU inference workers must support hot-swap model version updates without service interruption
constraints.rationale: Partner SLA requirements and contractual data isolation obligations

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM tracing across all microservices with tenant_id span tags; custom metrics for inference latency percentiles per model version, human review queue depth per tenant, auto-action rate per policy rule; PagerDuty for P95 latency breaches and queue depth anomalies
observability_baseline.rationale: Multi-tenant SLA monitoring requires per-tenant metric segmentation

execution_preference.value: Agile two-week sprints; GitHub Actions CI/CD; infrastructure as code with Terraform; model deployment via MLflow registry promotion workflow
execution_preference.rationale: Team standard delivery process with ML model lifecycle integration

compliance_context.value: DSA (EU Digital Services Act) Articles 17 and 24: appeal mechanism required, annual transparency report with action volumes by category; GDPR for EU user content in submitted assets; SOC 2 Type II for multi-tenant platform trust; AWS BAA not required (no health data)
compliance_context.rationale: DSA obligations binding for platforms with EU partner distribution; SOC 2 required by partner contracts

llm_integration.value: Fine-tuned vision transformer (ViT-L/16) for visual policy violation classification; fine-tuned Whisper-large-v3 for audio content classification; GPT-4o-mini for contextual appeal reason generation in moderator console; all inference via AWS SageMaker real-time endpoints on G5 instances
llm_integration.rationale: Domain-specific fine-tuning required for platform policy categories; SageMaker endpoints enable hot-swap model version deployment

project.goal: Multi-tenant AI UGC content moderation platform for video streaming partners
project.target_users: Partner API consumers, trust-and-safety moderators, platform admins
project.timeline: MVP in 16 weeks, full multi-tenant platform in 28 weeks

frontend.state: React Query for queue data, WebSocket for real-time queue updates, Zustand for moderator session state

backend.framework: FastAPI microservices: orchestrator, visual-classifier, audio-classifier, review-queue, partner-api, admin-api
backend.api: REST partner API with mTLS; internal gRPC between microservices

security.auth: mTLS for partner API, Auth0 SAML SSO for moderator console
security.isolation: PostgreSQL RLS policies enforcing tenant_id on every table
security.audit: All moderation verdicts and human review actions logged with operator identity and tenant_id

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS
data.cache: Redis 7 for job queue, inference result cache, review queue state

devops.hosting: ECS Fargate plus G5 spot instances

confirmed_decisions.vision_model: ViT-L/16 fine-tuned on platform policy categories

reasoner_reviews.product: The appeal management workflow is critical for DSA compliance and partner trust — partners need to be able to demonstrate to regulators that appeals are processed within mandated timelines. The DSA transparency report generator must be driven by structured verdict data rather than assembled manually; the round-2 plan's approach of manual report compilation was correctly flagged in the audit.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The shift from shared Celery workers to dedicated per-tenant inference queues in round 2 addressed the cross-tenant inference throughput interference issue. The remaining architectural gap is the SageMaker model hot-swap strategy — the plan should specify the endpoint update procedure (create new endpoint config, update endpoint, wait for InService status) and how in-flight inference requests are drained before the update completes.
reasoner_reviews.security: Row-level security on PostgreSQL is the correct mechanism for tenant data isolation, but the RLS policies must be tested with a dedicated adversarial test suite that attempts cross-tenant queries under every application database role. The previous audit's concern about the moderator console WebSocket connection leaking events from other tenants must be verified as resolved with explicit WebSocket room isolation by tenant_id.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The P95 under 10 second latency constraint requires that the orchestrator submit visual and audio classifier jobs in parallel rather than sequentially. The round-2 plan addressed parallelization; round-3 should confirm that the SageMaker endpoint warm-start strategy (provisioned concurrency) is in place to prevent cold-start latency spikes from breaching the SLA during traffic bursts.

backend.service_design: Six FastAPI microservices: OrchestratorService (receives partner API submissions, fans out to classifier queues), VisualClassifierService (SageMaker ViT-L/16 endpoint wrapper), AudioClassifierService (SageMaker Whisper endpoint wrapper), ReviewQueueService (human review queue management and WebSocket hub), PartnerAPIService (mTLS-terminated public endpoint with rate limiting and tenant auth), AdminAPIService (model lifecycle, policy rule sets, DSA report generation)
backend.api_patterns: Partner API: REST with mTLS certificate binding to tenant_id; POST /v1/assets (submit for moderation), GET /v1/assets/{id}/verdict (poll result), POST /v1/assets/{id}/appeal (submit appeal). Internal gRPC between OrchestratorService and classifier services for low-latency job dispatch.
backend.llm_integration_design: OrchestratorService submits parallel inference jobs to VisualClassifierService (SageMaker ViT-L/16 real-time endpoint) and AudioClassifierService (SageMaker Whisper endpoint) using asyncio.gather for concurrent execution. Classifier services call SageMaker InvokeEndpoint API and return confidence scores per violation category. GPT-4o-mini is called by ReviewQueueService to generate contextual appeal response suggestions for moderators, with the call scoped to the appeal record and policy text — no raw video content is sent to the LLM.
backend.failure_handling: OrchestratorService retries SageMaker InvokeEndpoint calls up to 3 times with exponential backoff on throttling errors; after 3 failures the asset is routed to human review queue with a system_error verdict reason; SageMaker endpoint health is checked via Datadog synthetic canaries every 30 seconds

security.auth_design: Partner API: mTLS with AWS ACM Private CA issuing per-tenant client certificates; certificate CN maps to tenant_id in the PartnerAPIService middleware; Auth0 SAML federation for moderator and admin console SSO with tenant_id claim in the SAML assertion
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL RLS policies on all content_submissions, moderation_verdicts, human_review_queues, and appeal_records tables enforce SET app.current_tenant_id before every query; application database role does not bypass RLS; platform admin role uses a separate privileged database connection with explicit audit logging
security.secrets_management: AWS Secrets Manager stores SageMaker endpoint names, database credentials, Auth0 client secrets, and partner mTLS CA configuration; ECS task role grants GetSecretValue access; secrets rotated on 90-day schedule via Secrets Manager rotation Lambda
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every moderation verdict INSERT, human review action, appeal state change, and model version promotion is written to an immutable moderation_audit_log table with tenant_id, operator_id, action_type, and payload JSONB; audit log rows are replicated to S3 via DMS for long-term retention and DSA transparency reporting

data.entities: content_submissions, moderation_verdicts, policy_rule_sets, model_versions, human_review_queues, appeal_records, tenant_configs, violation_categories
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS on all tenant-scoped tables; Redis for review queue state (sorted set per tenant ordered by submission time) and inference result cache (5-minute TTL keyed by asset_hash); S3 for raw video asset storage during inference window, deleted post-scoring per GDPR minimization policy
data.analytics_events: Datadog custom metrics: moderation_action_count{tenant_id, violation_category, action_type}, inference_latency_p95{model_version, tenant_id}, human_review_queue_depth{tenant_id}; weekly Spark aggregation job on verdict data produces DSA transparency report input tables in Redshift
data.data_risks: Cross-tenant data leak via RLS misconfiguration is the primary data risk; mitigated by adversarial RLS test suite in CI and read-replica used for all application queries with RLS enforced at the replica too

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster for all six FastAPI microservices with ALB routing; G5 spot instances managed by an Auto Scaling Group for SageMaker inference endpoints (SageMaker manages instance lifecycle); Aurora PostgreSQL 16 (Multi-AZ) for RLS-enforced tenant data; ElastiCache Redis cluster; S3 for asset staging; CloudWatch Logs plus Datadog agent sidecar
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Python 3.12 linting (ruff), pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build and ECR push, Terraform plan and apply (workspace per environment), ECS service rolling update; model deployment via MLflow model registry promotion to SageMaker endpoint via a separate model-deploy workflow triggered by ML team
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with distributed tracing across all six microservices, tenant_id injected as span tag at PartnerAPIService ingress; SageMaker endpoint latency metrics forwarded to Datadog via CloudWatch metric stream; PagerDuty alert on P95 inference latency exceeding 8 seconds (pre-SLA buffer) or human review queue depth exceeding 1000 items per tenant
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS service rollback to previous task definition on health check failure; SageMaker endpoint rollback by re-pointing to the previous endpoint configuration; Terraform state-based infrastructure rollback; database migrations use Alembic with additive-only expand-contract pattern

ISS-001.title: Cross-tenant inference throughput interference on shared Celery workers
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 used a shared Celery worker pool for all tenants; a burst from one high-volume partner tenant saturated the worker pool and caused SLA-breaching latency for other tenants.

history.note: Shared Celery pool with no per-tenant priority queues
Replaced with per-tenant Redis queues and dedicated OrchestratorService fan-out

ISS-002.title: WebSocket moderator console events not tenant-scoped
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 WebSocket implementation broadcast new queue item events to all connected moderators regardless of tenant_id, risking cross-tenant content exposure in the review console.

history.note: Single WebSocket broadcast channel
Per-tenant WebSocket rooms with tenant_id verified from Auth0 SAML claim at subscription time

ISS-003.title: SageMaker model hot-swap procedure not specified
ISS-003.detail: The constraint requires GPU inference workers to support hot-swap model version updates without service interruption. Round 2 did not specify the SageMaker endpoint update procedure or in-flight request draining strategy.

history.note: Model deployment section references MLflow registry but does not detail SageMaker endpoint update sequence or in-flight drain

ISS-004.title: DSA transparency report driven by manual data compilation
ISS-004.detail: Round 2 plan described DSA transparency report generation as a manual data export process; this violates the compliance_context requirement for structured automated reporting.

history.note: Report generation described as ad-hoc CSV export
Automated weekly Spark aggregation job populating DSA report tables with structured verdict data

focus_issues.title: SageMaker model hot-swap procedure not specified
focus_issues.detail: Constraint requires hot-swap model version updates without service interruption; round 2 did not specify the endpoint update sequence or in-flight request draining.

latest_recommendations: Specify the SageMaker endpoint update procedure for model hot-swap: create new endpoint config from MLflow registered model, call UpdateEndpoint, poll for InService status, drain in-flight requests using SageMaker endpoint routing weights before removing old config
Add SageMaker provisioned concurrency configuration to prevent cold-start latency spikes from breaching the P95 10s SLA during traffic bursts
