previous_plan.title: AI Content Moderation Platform v2 — Tenant Queue Isolation
previous_plan.executive_summary: Multi-tenant AI UGC content moderation platform on AWS ECS Fargate and G5 GPU instances. Round 2 resolves cross-tenant inference throughput interference via per-tenant Redis queues and fixes WebSocket tenant isolation via Auth0-scoped room subscriptions.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Six FastAPI microservices on ECS Fargate with per-tenant Redis queues isolating inference load. OrchestratorService fans out parallel visual and audio classifier jobs. SageMaker ViT-L/16 and Whisper endpoints serve inference. PostgreSQL RLS enforces tenant data isolation.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: ECS rolling updates for API microservices. MLflow model registry used for model version management. SageMaker endpoint updated via UpdateEndpoint API call. No detail on traffic draining or provisioned concurrency.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS partner API, Auth0 SAML SSO, PostgreSQL RLS, AWS Secrets Manager. Per-tenant WebSocket rooms verified against SAML tenant_id claim. DSA transparency report via weekly Spark aggregation job.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round-3 revision with one remaining medium issue: the SageMaker model hot-swap procedure needs to be fully specified. The constraint requires hot-swap without service interruption — this means using SageMaker's UpdateEndpoint with production variant routing weights to shift traffic gradually from the old model config to the new one, with in-flight request draining via a minimum 0% weight on the old variant before deletion. Also need to add provisioned concurrency to prevent cold-start latency from breaching the P95 10s SLA. All prior high-severity issues are resolved; this is the convergence round for a strong-quality plan.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified the full SageMaker model hot-swap procedure in deployment_and_operations and workflows sections: (1) ML team promotes a new model version in MLflow registry with a production tag; (2) the model-deploy GitHub Actions workflow creates a new SageMaker endpoint configuration referencing the new model artifact; (3) UpdateEndpoint is called with two production variants — old config at 90% weight, new config at 10% — allowing gradual traffic shift; (4) Datadog monitors new variant latency and error rate for 15 minutes; (5) if metrics are healthy, UpdateEndpoint shifts to 0% old / 100% new and the old variant config is deleted; (6) if metrics degrade, UpdateEndpoint reverts to 100% old. Added provisioned concurrency of 2 minimum instances per SageMaker endpoint to eliminate cold-start latency spikes during traffic bursts.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Model version updates are deployed with gradual traffic shifting and automatic rollback capability, with no service interruption to partner API callers and no cold-start latency events breaching the P95 10s SLA.

target_output.title: AI Content Moderation Platform v3 — Model Hot-Swap and Convergence
target_output.executive_summary: A multi-tenant AI content moderation platform for video streaming service partners, running on AWS ECS Fargate and EC2 G5 GPU instances. Six FastAPI microservices orchestrate parallel visual (ViT-L/16) and audio (Whisper-large-v3) inference for UGC policy violation detection, with confidence-threshold routing to automated action or human review queues. Round-3 completes the architecture by specifying the SageMaker model hot-swap procedure with gradual traffic shifting and automatic rollback, and adds provisioned concurrency to prevent cold-start SLA breaches. PostgreSQL RLS, mTLS partner API, and per-tenant WebSocket rooms provide multi-tenant data and session isolation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Six-service microservices architecture on ECS Fargate: PartnerAPIService (mTLS-terminated public endpoint), OrchestratorService (parallel classifier fan-out via per-tenant Redis queues), VisualClassifierService and AudioClassifierService (SageMaker real-time endpoint wrappers), ReviewQueueService (human review queue and tenant-scoped WebSocket hub), and AdminAPIService (model lifecycle, DSA reporting, tenant config). SageMaker G5 endpoints serve ViT-L/16 and Whisper-large-v3 inference with 2-instance provisioned concurrency minimum. PostgreSQL 16 with RLS on all tenant-scoped tables. Redis 7 Cluster with per-tenant sorted sets for review queue ordering. S3 stores video assets during the inference window and deletes them immediately post-scoring per GDPR minimization. mTLS via ACM Private CA binds partner API connections to tenant_id. Auth0 SAML SSO with tenant_id claim gates moderator and admin console access. Datadog distributed tracing with tenant_id span tags provides per-tenant SLA visibility.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.115, Pydantic v2, asyncio for parallel SageMaker invocations. ML Models: ViT-L/16 fine-tuned on platform violation categories (SageMaker real-time endpoint), Whisper-large-v3 fine-tuned (SageMaker real-time endpoint), GPT-4o-mini for appeal response suggestion (OpenAI API, no raw video content). Databases: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with RLS, ElastiCache Redis 7 Cluster. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, EC2 G5 Auto Scaling Group for SageMaker, S3, ACM Private CA, Secrets Manager. Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Auth0 SAML. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Terraform, MLflow, Alembic.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Partner API for video asset submission (POST /v1/assets) with mTLS certificate-to-tenant binding, verdict polling (GET /v1/assets/{id}/verdict), and appeal submission (POST /v1/assets/{id}/appeal). Parallel inference pipeline: OrchestratorService fans out VisualClassifier and AudioClassifier jobs simultaneously via per-tenant Redis queues; OrchestratorService aggregates scores against the tenant's active policy_rule_set and determines auto-action or human review routing. Human Review Console: moderator claims queue items, views asset with model scores and policy text overlay, issues approve/reject/escalate verdict. Appeal Management: submitter appeal reason displayed alongside GPT-4o-mini suggested response; moderator issues final appeal verdict within DSA-mandated timeline. Model Version Management: admin promotes MLflow model versions to SageMaker endpoints with gradual traffic shifting. DSA Transparency Report: automated weekly aggregation of action volumes by violation_category with appeal outcome statistics.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (partner API ingress and moderator console ingress), PartnerAPIService (ECS Fargate, mTLS termination, rate limiting per tenant_id), OrchestratorService (ECS Fargate, Redis per-tenant queue consumer and classifier fan-out), VisualClassifierService (ECS Fargate, SageMaker ViT-L/16 endpoint wrapper), AudioClassifierService (ECS Fargate, SageMaker Whisper endpoint wrapper), ReviewQueueService (ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL queue operations and tenant-scoped WebSocket hub), AdminAPIService (ECS Fargate, model lifecycle and DSA report API), SageMaker real-time endpoints (G5 instances with 2-instance provisioned concurrency per endpoint), Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with RLS, ElastiCache Redis 7 Cluster, S3 for asset staging, ACM Private CA for mTLS, AWS Secrets Manager, Datadog agent sidecars on all ECS tasks.
target_output.workflows: Asset Submission and Inference: partner POSTs to /v1/assets with mTLS cert; PartnerAPIService validates tenant_id from cert CN, writes content_submission row with SET app.current_tenant_id RLS context, enqueues job ID to Redis per-tenant sorted set; OrchestratorService dequeues and calls asyncio.gather(visual_classify(asset_id), audio_classify(asset_id)); each classifier calls SageMaker InvokeEndpoint and returns confidence scores per violation_category; OrchestratorService evaluates aggregated scores against policy_rule_set thresholds: if max score exceeds auto_action_threshold the asset is auto-actioned and moderation_verdict is written; if score is between human_review_threshold and auto_action_threshold the asset is added to the human review queue. Human Review: ReviewQueueService receives a queue claim request from a moderator; RLS ensures only the moderator's tenant_id queue is visible; moderator reviews the asset in the React console, sees model scores and matched policy text, and issues a verdict; WebSocket event notifies the moderation team's session that the queue depth has decreased. SageMaker Model Hot-Swap: ML team tags a new model version as production in MLflow registry; GitHub Actions model-deploy workflow triggers; workflow creates a new SageMaker endpoint configuration referencing the new model artifact in S3; calls UpdateEndpoint with two production variants (old at 90% weight, new at 10%); Datadog monitors new variant P95 latency and error rate for 15 minutes; if healthy, workflow calls UpdateEndpoint again with 0% old and 100% new and deletes the old endpoint configuration; if latency or errors degrade beyond threshold, workflow reverts to 100% old variant automatically via rollback step.
target_output.data_model: content_submissions (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID NOT NULL, asset_s3_key TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/processing/actioned/in_review/appealed) with RLS: tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id'). moderation_verdicts (id UUID PK, submission_id FK, tenant_id UUID NOT NULL, violation_category TEXT, confidence_score NUMERIC, action_type ENUM auto_remove/auto_approve/human_review, verdict_source ENUM model/human, model_version_id FK, issued_at TIMESTAMPTZ) with RLS. policy_rule_sets (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID NOT NULL, name TEXT, rules_json JSONB, active BOOL, created_at) with RLS. model_versions (id UUID PK, model_type ENUM visual/audio, mlflow_run_id TEXT, sagemaker_endpoint_config TEXT, deployed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM staging/shadow/active/retired). human_review_queues (id UUID PK, submission_id FK, tenant_id UUID NOT NULL, claimed_by UUID, claimed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, priority INT) with RLS. appeal_records (id UUID PK, submission_id FK, tenant_id UUID NOT NULL, appeal_reason TEXT, suggested_response TEXT, final_verdict ENUM upheld/overturned, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ) with RLS. tenant_configs (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID UNIQUE, display_name TEXT, policy_rule_set_id FK, webhook_url TEXT, created_at). violation_categories (id UUID PK, code TEXT UNIQUE, display_name TEXT, dsa_category_code TEXT).
target_output.api_design: Partner REST API (mTLS required): POST /v1/assets (submit video for moderation, returns submission_id), GET /v1/assets/{id}/verdict (poll verdict with status and action_type), POST /v1/assets/{id}/appeal (submit appeal with appeal_reason). Moderator console REST: GET /v1/review/queue (paginated queue for current tenant), POST /v1/review/queue/{id}/claim, POST /v1/review/queue/{id}/verdict. Admin REST: GET/POST /v1/models (model version list and promotion trigger), GET/POST /v1/policy-rules (rule set management per tenant), GET /v1/dsa/report?period=2025-Q1 (DSA transparency report JSON). WebSocket /ws/review-queue: subscribes moderator session to tenant-scoped room, emits new_queue_item and queue_depth_update events scoped to tenant_id.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS Partner API: ACM Private CA issues X.509 client certificates per tenant; certificate CN parsed by PartnerAPIService middleware and set as the authenticated tenant_id; ALB listener configured to require mTLS and forward certificate CN as a header. Multi-tenant Data Isolation: PostgreSQL RLS policies on all eight tenant-scoped tables enforce SET app.current_tenant_id before every query; application role does not have BYPASSRLS privilege; adversarial RLS test suite in CI attempts cross-tenant SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE under all application database roles. WebSocket Isolation: ReviewQueueService verifies tenant_id from the Auth0 SAML token at WebSocket connection upgrade; socket joins only the tenant_id-keyed room; server-side room fanout filters events by room membership. Secrets Management: AWS Secrets Manager stores database credentials, SageMaker endpoint names, Auth0 client secrets, and GPT-4o-mini API key; ECS task role GetSecretValue permission; 90-day automatic rotation. DSA Compliance: all moderation_verdicts and appeal_records written to immutable moderation_audit_log; weekly Spark aggregation job computes DSA Article 24 transparency metrics (action volumes by violation_category, appeal overturn rate) into structured report tables; SOC 2 Type II controls documented in Vanta.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster with six services behind ALB; ALB listener rules route /v1 (partner), /v1/review (moderator), /v1/admin traffic to respective services. G5 Auto Scaling Group managed by SageMaker for inference instances with 2-instance provisioned concurrency per endpoint to eliminate cold starts. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, pytest (80% coverage), Docker build and ECR push, Terraform plan and apply (workspace per environment: staging, production). Model Deploy Workflow: triggered by MLflow model registry production tag webhook; creates new SageMaker endpoint config; calls UpdateEndpoint with 90%/10% variant split; polls endpoint status to InService; runs 15-minute Datadog canary evaluation on new variant; shifts to 100% new and deletes old config on success, or reverts on latency or error threshold breach. Database migrations via Alembic with additive-only expand-contract pattern; migration applied via ECS one-off task before service rolling update.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM distributed tracing across all six FastAPI services with tenant_id injected as span tag at PartnerAPIService ingress; trace propagated via standard HTTP headers to downstream classifier and queue services. Custom Datadog metrics: moderation.inference_latency_p95 per model_version and tenant_id, moderation.queue_depth per tenant_id, moderation.auto_action_rate per violation_category. SageMaker endpoint CloudWatch metrics (Invocations, ModelLatency, InvocationsPerInstance) forwarded to Datadog via metric stream. PagerDuty alerts: P95 inference latency exceeding 8 seconds (pre-SLA buffer) for any tenant, human review queue depth exceeding 1000 items for any tenant, SageMaker endpoint InvocationsPerInstance exceeding provisioned concurrency limit (cold-start risk indicator). Datadog dashboard 'Moderation SLA' shows per-tenant P95 latency trends, auto-action rates, and queue depth heatmap.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate for six microservices at approximately 800 USD per month at baseline. SageMaker G5 spot instances for inference at approximately 1.50 USD per hour per endpoint; provisioned concurrency (2 instances per endpoint) fixed at 3 USD per hour total; at 10 million monthly submissions the inference compute cost is approximately 2200 USD per month. ElastiCache Redis Cluster at approximately 300 USD per month. Aurora PostgreSQL Multi-AZ at approximately 400 USD per month. Scale path: add SageMaker provisioned concurrency instances and increase OrchestratorService ECS task count as partner submission volume grows; per-tenant Redis queue design scales horizontally without re-architecture.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): PartnerAPIService with mTLS, OrchestratorService with per-tenant Redis queues, VisualClassifierService and AudioClassifierService with SageMaker ViT-L/16 and Whisper endpoints, ReviewQueueService with moderator console, PostgreSQL RLS schema, Secrets Manager integration, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Datadog APM, DSA audit log. Phase 2 (8 weeks): appeal management workflow with GPT-4o-mini response suggestions, AdminAPIService with model version management and MLflow hot-swap workflow, DSA transparency report generator via weekly Spark aggregation, per-tenant analytics dashboard. Phase 3 (6 weeks): model A/B shadow evaluation harness, multi-region active-passive deployment for EU data residency, federated model evaluation framework for partner-hosted fine-tuning.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with strict type annotations and Pydantic v2 models for all API contracts. ruff linter and formatter enforced in GitHub Actions CI. pytest with 80% coverage gate; adversarial RLS test suite required for all new tenant-scoped tables. FastAPI dependency injection for database connections with tenant_id context management. All SageMaker InvokeEndpoint calls use async boto3 with asyncio.gather for parallel classifier invocations. MLflow experiment tracking required for all model fine-tuning runs before promotion. Terraform modules for all AWS resources; no manual console changes in production.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ViT-L/16 fine-tuning drift degrades precision on emerging violation categories not in the training set. Mitigation: continuous F1 score evaluation against human-reviewed ground truth in weekly batch job; automatic retraining trigger when F1 drops below 0.85 for any violation category. Risk: SageMaker endpoint update routing weights not supported for real-time endpoints in all AWS regions. Mitigation: verified supported in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 target deployment regions; fallback is blue-green endpoint swap with a brief dual-endpoint traffic split period. Risk: PostgreSQL RLS bypass via application role misconfiguration. Mitigation: adversarial RLS test suite in CI, regular penetration testing of multi-tenant data isolation, read-replica used for all application queries with RLS enforced at replica. Tradeoff: GPT-4o-mini for appeal response suggestions introduces an external API dependency and per-call cost; scoped to moderator console only with appeal volume caps per tenant to bound cost.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SageMaker UpdateEndpoint with variant routing weights as the hot-swap mechanism — supported in both us-east-1 and eu-west-1. Chose provisioned concurrency of 2 instances minimum per endpoint over Lambda-style pre-warming because SageMaker real-time endpoints do not support Lambda warming patterns. Confirmed GPT-4o-mini for appeal suggestions scoped to text-only appeal reason and policy text — no raw video content or user PII sent to OpenAI API, meeting GDPR data minimization for this use case.

metadata.notes: Multi-tenant AI UGC content moderation platform; round-3 specifies SageMaker hot-swap with gradual traffic shifting and adds provisioned concurrency for SLA compliance.

project_goal.value: Children's educational video streaming app for ages 4-12 with parental controls, PIN-locked profile switching, and content filtering to enforce age-appropriate viewing targeting COPPA compliance
project_goal.rationale: Growing regulatory scrutiny on children's apps requires COPPA-native design from the first release rather than retrofitting

target_users.value: Parents (account holders) and child users ages 4-12; content administrators managing educational video catalog
target_users.rationale: Two distinct user classes with asymmetric permissions: parents hold accounts and set controls, children interact within parent-defined constraints

project_class.rationale: Primary experience is React Native mobile on iOS and Android; web admin panel for content management is secondary

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, auth, data, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Mobile frontend, FastAPI backend for content and parental control APIs, Cognito auth with parental verification flow, PostgreSQL content and profile data, watch time analytics dashboard, content admin panel

complexity_level.rationale: COPPA compliance, PIN enforcement, content rating filtering, and parental verification add regulatory complexity beyond a simple streaming app

risk_level.rationale: COPPA violations carry FTC civil penalties up to $51,744 per violation; collecting any data from children under 13 without verifiable parental consent is a strict liability risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores child profile names, ages, viewing histories, and watch time data which are personal data under COPPA and GDPR-K

external_exposure.rationale: App available on iOS App Store and Google Play; subscriber-facing app is publicly downloadable

access_model.value: Cognito parent accounts with mandatory email verification; child profiles do not hold independent credentials - access gated entirely by parent PIN entry in app
access_model.rationale: COPPA requires parental consent flow; child profiles must not be independently authenticatable without parental knowledge

feature_scope.value: Parent account registration with verifiable consent flow, child profile creation with age and content rating ceiling, PIN-locked profile switching, content rating filter enforcement, daily watch time limits with PIN override, parental activity dashboard, content admin catalog management panel
feature_scope.rationale: Full parental control suite required for COPPA compliance and App Store child-category approval

mvp_scope.value: Parent account with Cognito, one child profile per parent, content rating filter, daily watch time limit with PIN lock, basic catalog browsing and playback
mvp_scope.rationale: Core COPPA-compliant experience: parental account, child profile, and content filtering in one release

security_baseline.value: No advertising SDKs in child-session context, no third-party analytics trackers in child session, TLS 1.3, Cognito parent email verification, PIN stored as bcrypt hash, content rating enforcement at API not client-side
security_baseline.rationale: COPPA prohibits behavioral advertising to children; App Store requires no tracking in children's category apps

frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform iOS and Android from a single TypeScript codebase; team expertise in React Native

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI dependency injection cleanly separates parent-context and child-context request handling for data isolation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for parent-child profile hierarchy, content ratings, and playlist assignments

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container scaling; ECS integrates with Cognito and CloudFront for stream token delivery

privacy_retention_policy.value: Child viewing history retained 12 months; deleted within 30 days of parental account closure; no data sold or shared with third parties; COPPA data inventory maintained
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: COPPA requires data minimization and timely deletion upon parental request; 12-month retention supports recommendation quality

future_scope.value: Tablet-optimized iPad layout, educational progress tracking per learning category, teacher account type for classroom playlists, voice-navigation accessibility for early readers
future_scope.rationale: Education market expansion requires teacher accounts and progress tracking in next phase

constraints.value: No advertising SDKs permitted in any dependency; no third-party analytics SDKs in child session context; App Store Kids category compliance required
constraints.rationale: App Store Kids category requirements and COPPA prohibit any third-party data collection during child sessions

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for API metrics and ECS health; internal-only watch time analytics dashboard for content team; no external analytics SDK in child session
observability_baseline.rationale: CloudWatch provides operational visibility without introducing third-party data collection that would violate COPPA

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; COPPA consent flow and content filtering delivered in sprint 1-2 before any catalog features
execution_preference.rationale: COPPA compliance gates App Store submission; must be validated before catalog features are built

compliance_context.value: COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act): verifiable parental consent required before collecting data from children under 13; FTC Safe Harbor program membership planned; GDPR-K for EU child users under 16 with parental consent; ESRB and PEGI content rating standards for content labeling; App Store Kids category requirements prohibiting advertising and third-party tracking SDKs
compliance_context.rationale: COPPA is strict liability; FTC Safe Harbor membership provides affirmative defense; App Store Kids category rejection would block distribution

project.goal: COPPA-compliant children's educational video app with parental controls
project.target_users: Parents and child users ages 4-12
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks targeting App Store submission in week 12

frontend.state: React Query for server state, React Context for child session state

backend.framework: FastAPI with dependency injection for parent vs child context separation

security.auth: Cognito parent accounts with email verification
security.pin: Child profile PIN stored as bcrypt hash, never transmitted
security.content_filter: Content rating enforcement at API layer, not client-side

data.db: PostgreSQL 16

constraints.no_tracking_sdks: No third-party analytics in child sessions

confirmed_decisions.content_enforcement: Server-side rating filter on all content API responses in child context

reasoner_reviews.product: The PIN-locked profile switching is the UX cornerstone of the parental control experience - it must be fast enough that children do not find ways to circumvent it by repeatedly tapping. The daily watch time limit enforcement needs to survive app restarts; time tracking must persist to the backend, not rely on in-memory state only.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI dependency injection is the right mechanism to distinguish parent context from child context: a get_child_session dependency validates the child profile PIN and injects child_profile_id, while a get_parent_session dependency validates the parent Cognito JWT. All content catalog endpoints called from child context go through get_child_session which applies the content rating filter automatically.
reasoner_reviews.security: The content rating ceiling must be enforced at the API layer with the child profile's max_rating_ceiling parameter, never relying on the mobile client to filter. The bcrypt PIN must be compared server-side; the client sends the PIN to a dedicated /profiles/{id}/unlock endpoint, receives a short-lived child session token (15-minute JWT), and uses that token for subsequent requests. This prevents the PIN value from being stored or cached on the device.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: App Store Kids category review is strict about SDK dependencies. A dependency audit must be run before submission to verify no third-party analytics or advertising SDKs are transitively included through any React Native library.

frontend.app_structure: React Native app with two navigation stacks: ParentStack (Cognito sign-in, account settings, child profile management, parental dashboard) and ChildStack (profile selection with PIN entry, content browsing, video player). ChildStack is only accessible after successful PIN unlock.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Parent: sign-up with email verification, parental consent form, child profile creator (name, age, avatar, rating ceiling, daily time limit), parental dashboard (watch history, time used today, controls). Child: profile selector carousel, PIN entry keypad, content catalog with age-filtered tiles, video player with time limit overlay.
frontend.state_management: React Query for content catalog and viewing history. React Context for child session state (active child profile, time remaining today). No Redux or Zustand - child session state is intentionally ephemeral and reset on PIN lock.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: No advertising SDK dependencies permitted. All third-party analytics calls disabled when child_session context is active. react-native-permissions used only for microphone permission in future voice navigation - not requested during child sessions.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with route groups: /parent (account and profile management, requires Cognito JWT), /child (content catalog and playback, requires child session JWT), /admin (catalog management, requires admin Cognito JWT), /webhooks (Cognito post-confirmation trigger for parental consent recording).
backend.api_patterns: Child session flow: POST /profiles/{id}/unlock accepts {pin} and returns {child_session_token, expires_at} (15-minute JWT). Child catalog: GET /catalog scoped by child profile max_rating_ceiling automatically applied via get_child_session dependency. Playback: GET /playback/{content_id}/token returns CloudFront signed URL, validates child entitlement and watch time budget before issuing.
backend.failure_handling: Watch time budget enforcement: playback token issuance checks today_watched_seconds against daily_limit_seconds; if over budget, returns 403 with {reason: 'daily_limit_reached', limit_seconds, watched_seconds, reset_at}. PIN brute-force protection: 5 failed PIN attempts in 60 seconds locks the child profile for 15 minutes and sends parental email notification.

security.auth_design: Cognito User Pool for parent accounts with email verification mandatory. Post-confirmation Lambda trigger records parental consent timestamp and COPPA consent version to parent_accounts table. Child profiles are local database records under the parent account - they do not have Cognito identities.
security.authorization_model: FastAPI dependency injection: Depends(get_parent_session) validates Cognito JWT and injects parent_id. Depends(get_child_session) validates 15-minute child session JWT and injects child_profile with max_rating_ceiling and time_remaining_today. All /child/* routes use get_child_session.
security.privacy_controls: COPPA deletion: DELETE /parent/account triggers cascade deletion of all child profiles, viewing_histories, and watch_time_records within 30 days via Lambda async job. No behavioral data collected during child sessions - watch_time_records stores only total seconds per content_id, not granular seek positions. No third-party SDKs receive any child data.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: parental_consent_records table stores consent_version, consent_timestamp, ip_address, and parent_id for COPPA audit. data_access_log captures all API requests with child_profile_id for FTC Safe Harbor audit trail. Retained 3 years per Safe Harbor requirements.

data.entities: parent_accounts (id, cognito_user_id, email, coppa_consent_version, coppa_consent_at, created_at), child_profiles (id, parent_id FK, display_name, age, avatar_id, max_rating_ceiling ENUM g/pg/pg13, daily_limit_seconds, pin_hash, created_at), content_videos (id, title, description, duration_seconds, rating ENUM g/pg/pg13, learning_category, hls_manifest_url, thumbnail_url, published_at), playlist_assignments (id, child_profile_id FK, content_id FK, assigned_by, assigned_at), watch_time_records (id, child_profile_id FK, content_id FK, watched_date DATE, watched_seconds INT, completed BOOLEAN), viewing_histories (id, child_profile_id FK, content_id FK, last_position_seconds INT, last_watched_at TIMESTAMPTZ), parental_consent_records (id, parent_id FK, consent_version TEXT, consented_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ip_address INET), blocked_categories (id, child_profile_id FK, category TEXT, blocked_by_parent_id FK).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ. S3 and CloudFront for HLS video delivery. No caching layer - data sensitivity and simplicity preference given moderate complexity.
data.retention_and_deletion: viewing_histories and watch_time_records deleted 12 months after last watch activity. Full child profile cascade delete within 30 days of parent account closure. parental_consent_records retained 3 years for FTC Safe Harbor audit.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service with auto-scaling (CPU target 60%, min 2 tasks max 10). ALB with HTTPS. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. CloudFront distribution for HLS video delivery with signed URLs. S3 for video assets. Cognito User Pool in us-east-1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: mypy, ruff linter, pytest with coverage gate 80%, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. React Native: ESLint, TypeScript check, Detox E2E for profile switch and PIN flows on iOS simulator. npm audit --audit-level=high blocks CI on high-severity dependencies.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch alarms: API 5xx error rate, ECS CPU, RDS connections. Custom CloudWatch metric for failed PIN attempts per child profile (alert at 5/minute per profile for brute-force detection). Internal watch time analytics dashboard via CloudWatch Contributor Insights on watch_time_records API calls.

ISS-001.title: Watch time tracking relies on client-side state and is reset on app restart, allowing children to circumvent daily limits
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan tracked today_watched_seconds in React Context only; app kill-and-restart resets the counter, allowing a child to bypass the daily time limit by force-quitting the app

history.note: Client-side watch time counter with no server persistence; app restart bypasses daily limit enforcement

ISS-002.title: Content rating filter applied client-side only, allowing bypass via API request manipulation
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan filtered content tiles in the React Native component based on child profile's max_rating_ceiling, but the backend /catalog endpoint returned all content regardless of caller context, so a direct API call bypassed the filter

history.note: Content rating filter in UI component only; backend catalog endpoint unrestricted

focus_issues.title: Watch time tracking relies on client-side state and is reset on app restart, allowing children to circumvent daily limits
focus_issues.detail: Client-side React Context counter for watch time resets on app restart; children can bypass daily limits by force-quitting the app

focus_issues.title: Content rating filter applied client-side only, allowing bypass via API request manipulation
focus_issues.detail: Backend catalog endpoint returns all content regardless of child profile context; filter only in React Native UI component

latest_recommendations: Persist watch time to watch_time_records table on each playback heartbeat (every 60 seconds) so server-side time budget is authoritative
Enforce content rating filter in FastAPI get_child_session dependency applied to all /child/* catalog and playback endpoints, not in the mobile client
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: KidStream Educational Video App v1

convergence_state.reason: Two high-severity issues unresolved in round 1; score 5.9 below pass threshold

previous_audits.summary: Two high-severity security issues prevent passage: client-side watch time enforcement and client-side content rating filter are both bypassable, which would fail App Store Kids category review and COPPA compliance
strengths: Cognito parent account design with post-confirmation consent recording is correct
PIN brute-force protection with 5-attempt lockout is well-designed
COPPA deletion cascade logic is well-specified
concerns: Watch time enforcement relies entirely on React Context state, reset on app restart
Content rating filter is a UI-layer concern only; backend returns all content to child context callers
Both issues together mean a child can bypass both daily limits and age restrictions
blocking_issues: ISS-001: App restart bypasses daily watch time limit
ISS-002: Direct API call bypasses content rating filter
recommendations: Add watch_time_records server-side persistence with 60-second heartbeat from player; child session token issuance checks today_watched_seconds from database
Move content rating filter to FastAPI get_child_session dependency that injects max_rating_ceiling as a query filter on all catalog and content requests

issue_updates.title: Watch time bypass via app restart
issue_updates.detail: React Context resets on restart; no server-side time tracking

issue_updates.title: Content rating filter client-side only
issue_updates.detail: Backend catalog endpoint ignores child context

previous_plan.title: KidStream Educational Video App v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Round 1 plan established parent Cognito auth, child PIN unlock, and COPPA consent recording but left watch time tracking in React Context and content rating filtering in the UI component.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: React Native 0.74 frontend, FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL 16, Cognito parent auth, CloudFront HLS delivery. Child profiles created under parent accounts with PIN-locked switching.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito parent accounts with email verification and consent recording. PIN bcrypt hashing. Content rating filter in React Native catalog screen component. Watch time counter in React Context - not persisted to backend. No ad SDKs.
previous_plan.workflows: Child selects profile, enters PIN, React Context loads child profile data including max_rating. Catalog screen filters tiles client-side by rating. Player increments local watch time counter. At daily limit, player shows overlay. App restart resets counter.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 targets two high security issues: ISS-001 requires server-side watch time persistence via 60-second heartbeat from the player, with today_watched_seconds computed from watch_time_records at child session token issuance; ISS-002 requires moving the content rating filter into the FastAPI get_child_session dependency so every /child/* endpoint automatically applies max_rating_ceiling as a WHERE clause. Both fixes are primarily in the backend and workflows sections. The plan quality is weak: while the critical COPPA compliance fixes are present, several sections have thin detail and the observability section lacks domain-specific metrics for child safety monitoring.

fix_report.action_taken: Added server-side watch time persistence: React Native video player fires POST /child/watch-time/heartbeat every 60 seconds with {child_profile_id, content_id, session_seconds_delta}. FastAPI handler upserts watch_time_records for today. Child session token issuance (POST /profiles/{id}/unlock) now queries SUM(watched_seconds) WHERE watched_date = today from watch_time_records and embeds today_watched_seconds in the JWT claim. Playback token endpoint re-validates remaining_seconds > 0 before issuing CloudFront signed URL.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Daily watch time limit is enforced by server-side accumulated seconds that survive app restarts, preventing circumvention via force-quit.

fix_report.action_taken: Content rating filter moved from React Native catalog screen to FastAPI get_child_session dependency. The dependency injects child_profile.max_rating_ceiling into the request context. All catalog, search, and content detail endpoints in /child/* apply WHERE rating <= max_rating_ceiling as a SQLAlchemy filter expression automatically. No content above the configured ceiling is returned in any API response to a child session caller.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Content rating filter cannot be bypassed by API manipulation; enforcement is at the backend query layer independent of client behavior.

target_output.title: KidStream Educational Video App v2
target_output.executive_summary: A COPPA-compliant children's educational video streaming app built with React Native 0.74 and Python FastAPI on ECS Fargate, serving educational video content for ages 4-12 under parent-configured controls. Parent accounts use Cognito with mandatory email verification and recorded consent. Child profiles are PIN-protected records under parent accounts with configurable content rating ceilings and daily watch time limits. Round 2 addresses both high-severity issues from round 1: watch time limits are now enforced server-side via 60-second heartbeat persistence to watch_time_records, and content rating filtering is applied in the FastAPI get_child_session dependency for all child-context catalog and playback endpoints.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-context request architecture: parent-context requests authenticate via Cognito JWT and access account management, child profile configuration, and parental dashboard endpoints; child-context requests authenticate via a 15-minute child session JWT (obtained by PIN unlock) and access catalog and playback endpoints with automatic content rating ceiling enforcement injected by the FastAPI get_child_session dependency. FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate connects to PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ. CloudFront delivers HLS video via signed URLs issued only after server-side watch time budget validation. No third-party analytics or advertising SDKs in any dependency. Cognito post-confirmation Lambda records COPPA consent version and timestamp.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript, React Query for server state, React Navigation for stack routing. Backend: Python 3.12 with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, passlib bcrypt for PIN hashing, python-jose for child session JWT. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ. Auth: AWS Cognito User Pool with email verification trigger. Video delivery: S3 and CloudFront with signed URLs. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, Secrets Manager. CI: GitHub Actions with Detox E2E on iOS simulator.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Parent Account Registration: Cognito signup with email verification; post-confirmation Lambda records COPPA consent version and timestamp to parental_consent_records. Child Profile Management: parent creates child profile with display name, age, avatar, max content rating ceiling (G/PG/PG-13), and daily watch time limit in minutes; PIN set at creation. PIN-Locked Profile Switch: child selects profile, enters PIN on keypad, POST /profiles/{id}/unlock validates bcrypt hash, returns 15-minute child session JWT with today_watched_seconds claim. Content Catalog: GET /child/catalog returns only content at or below child max_rating_ceiling enforced by get_child_session dependency. Video Playback with Time Enforcement: GET /child/content/{id}/token validates remaining watch budget from server-side watch_time_records before issuing CloudFront signed URL; player heartbeats every 60 seconds to persist watch time. Parental Dashboard: parent views daily watch time per child, recent viewing history, and remaining time budget for today.
target_output.system_components: React Native app (iOS and Android), FastAPI ECS Fargate service (parent_router, child_router, admin_router), get_child_session FastAPI dependency (PIN JWT validation, max_rating_ceiling injection, time budget check), get_parent_session FastAPI dependency (Cognito JWT validation), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (parent_accounts, child_profiles, content_videos, watch_time_records, viewing_histories, parental_consent_records), Cognito User Pool (parent authentication), Cognito post-confirmation Lambda (consent recording), CloudFront distribution (HLS delivery with signed URLs), S3 (HLS video storage), ECS Fargate auto-scaling group, ALB.
target_output.workflows: Child PIN Unlock and Time Budget Load: Child taps profile on selector screen, enters PIN digits on custom keypad (never system keyboard). POST /profiles/{id}/unlock sends {pin}. FastAPI verifies bcrypt hash. Queries SUM(watched_seconds) WHERE child_profile_id = ? AND watched_date = today from watch_time_records. If today_watched >= daily_limit_seconds, returns 403 {reason: daily_limit_reached}. Otherwise issues 15-minute child session JWT with {child_profile_id, max_rating_ceiling, today_watched_seconds, daily_limit_seconds}. JWT stored in React Native SecureStore. Server-Side Watch Time Heartbeat: Player component fires POST /child/watch-time/heartbeat every 60 seconds with {content_id, delta_seconds: 60}. FastAPI uses get_child_session to extract child_profile_id, then upserts watch_time_records using INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (child_profile_id, content_id, watched_date) DO UPDATE SET watched_seconds = watched_seconds + delta_seconds. If running total exceeds daily_limit_seconds, returns 403 {reason: limit_reached_mid_playback}. React Native player receives 403 and immediately shows time limit overlay requiring parent PIN to extend. Rating-Filtered Catalog Browse: Child opens catalog. GET /child/catalog goes through get_child_session dependency which extracts max_rating_ceiling from JWT and passes it to catalog query. SQLAlchemy query: SELECT * FROM content_videos WHERE rating_ordinal <= :max_rating_ordinal AND published = true. No content above ceiling is included in response regardless of client request parameters.
target_output.data_model: parent_accounts (id UUID PK, cognito_user_id TEXT UNIQUE, email TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). child_profiles (id UUID PK, parent_id UUID FK, display_name TEXT, age SMALLINT, avatar_id SMALLINT, max_rating_ceiling ENUM g/pg/pg13, daily_limit_seconds INT, pin_hash TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). content_videos (id UUID PK, title TEXT, description TEXT, duration_seconds INT, rating ENUM g/pg/pg13, rating_ordinal SMALLINT, learning_category TEXT, hls_manifest_url TEXT, thumbnail_url TEXT, published BOOLEAN, published_at TIMESTAMPTZ). watch_time_records (id UUID PK, child_profile_id UUID FK, content_id UUID FK, watched_date DATE, watched_seconds INT, completed BOOLEAN, UNIQUE(child_profile_id, content_id, watched_date)). viewing_histories (id UUID PK, child_profile_id UUID FK, content_id UUID FK, last_position_seconds INT, last_watched_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(child_profile_id, content_id)). parental_consent_records (id UUID PK, parent_id UUID FK, consent_version TEXT, consented_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ip_address INET). blocked_categories (id UUID PK, child_profile_id UUID FK, category TEXT).
target_output.api_design: Parent context (requires Cognito JWT): POST /auth/register, POST /profiles (create child profile), GET /profiles (list child profiles), PATCH /profiles/{id} (update settings), GET /parent/dashboard (watch time per child today). Child context (requires child session JWT from PIN unlock): GET /child/catalog (rating-filtered), GET /child/catalog/search?q= (rating-filtered), GET /child/content/{id} (detail, rating-filtered), GET /child/content/{id}/token (returns CloudFront signed URL if budget allows), POST /child/watch-time/heartbeat (upsert watch_time_records). PIN unlock: POST /profiles/{id}/unlock {pin} - returns child session JWT or 403 if limit reached or PIN wrong. Admin context: POST /admin/content, PATCH /admin/content/{id}, DELETE /admin/content/{id}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: COPPA Consent: Cognito post-confirmation Lambda records consent_version and IP to parental_consent_records. Verifiable parental consent via email verification is the chosen method. FTC Safe Harbor membership application in progress. Child Session Isolation: all /child/* routes use get_child_session which validates the short-lived PIN-issued JWT; Cognito parent tokens cannot access /child/* routes. Content Rating Enforcement at Backend: get_child_session injects max_rating_ceiling into all catalog and content queries as a SQLAlchemy filter - no client-side trust. Watch Time Enforcement at Backend: heartbeat endpoint and token issuance both validate against server-side watch_time_records sum - app restart cannot bypass limit. PIN Security: PIN value is never stored on device; POST /profiles/{id}/unlock is the only transmission point; bcrypt hash comparison server-side; 5-attempt lockout per 60 seconds sends parent email alert. No Third-Party SDKs: npm audit --audit-level=high in CI; all React Native library dependencies manually reviewed for advertising or tracking SDK transitive dependencies before App Store Kids category submission. App Store Kids category: no advertising, no third-party analytics, no in-app purchase of content (subscription only).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate: FastAPI service with min 2 max 10 tasks, CPU target 60%. ALB with HTTPS. RDS Multi-AZ db.t4g.medium. GitHub Actions CI: mypy strict, ruff, pytest with 80% coverage, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Detox E2E on iOS simulator: PIN unlock flow, watch time limit enforcement, catalog rating filter verification. React Native production builds via Fastlane: iOS via App Store Connect, Android via Google Play internal track. Secrets Manager for Cognito client secret and PostgreSQL credentials. CloudFront distribution for HLS with signed URL policy requiring valid expiry and child_profile_id path restriction.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch alarms: ECS API 5xx error rate >1% (P2 alert), failed PIN attempts per child_profile_id >5/minute (potential brute force, P1 alert), watch_time_records heartbeat gap >180 seconds during active playback session (heartbeat failure detection). Internal dashboard: daily active child profiles, watch time distribution by content category, daily limit hits per day (useful for UX calibration). No external analytics SDK; all metrics from CloudWatch custom metric API calls from FastAPI. App Store crash reporting via Apple/Google built-in tooling only.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate: 2 tasks at 0.5 vCPU/1 GB ~$60/month baseline. RDS Multi-AZ db.t4g.medium ~$80/month. CloudFront data transfer varies by video consumption. S3 HLS storage at $0.023/GB. Estimated $200-400/month for up to 5,000 daily active child profiles. Scaling headroom sufficient for 50,000 DAU without architecture change.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Cognito parent registration with email verification and COPPA consent recording, child profile creation with PIN and rating ceiling, PIN unlock flow with server-side watch time budget check, server-side heartbeat watch time persistence (ISS-001 fix), rating-filtered catalog and playback endpoints via get_child_session dependency (ISS-002 fix), CloudFront HLS delivery with signed URLs, GitHub Actions CI with npm audit and Detox E2E. Phase 2 (weeks 11-18): Parental dashboard with per-child watch history and daily time charts, playlist assignment by parent, blocked learning categories per child profile, COPPA deletion flow (30-day cascade on account closure), App Store Kids category submission. Phase 3 (weeks 19-26): Multiple child profiles per parent (up to 5), educational progress tracking per learning category, teacher account type with classroom playlist management, tablet-optimized iPad layout.
target_output.development_guidelines: FastAPI: all /child/* endpoints must use Depends(get_child_session); CI linting rule to catch any /child route that imports get_parent_session instead. SQLAlchemy: all catalog queries against content_videos must include rating_ordinal <= child_profile.max_rating_ordinal filter; PR template checklist item for content endpoints. PIN handling: never log PIN values; never include PIN in error response bodies; no PIN in CloudWatch logs. React Native: SecureStore for child session JWT storage, not AsyncStorage. Detox E2E test suite must cover: PIN brute-force 5-attempt lockout, daily limit reached mid-playback response, catalog returns only age-appropriate content for G-rated child profile. npm audit required in CI before App Store build.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: 60-second heartbeat granularity means a child who closes the app at second 59 of a minute loses up to 59 seconds of tracked watch time, slightly under-counting consumption. Mitigation: send a final heartbeat on app backgrounding via React Native AppState listener. Risk: App Store Kids category review may flag specific React Native libraries as containing disallowed tracking code even if not called. Mitigation: manual SDK audit before submission using a tool like Exodus Privacy scanner. Tradeoff: 15-minute child session JWT means a parent must re-enter PIN after 15 minutes of inactivity; intended security behavior but may generate parental UX complaints for long viewing sessions - configurable to 30 minutes in parent settings.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Server-side watch time enforcement confirmed via 60-second heartbeat to watch_time_records (ISS-001 resolved). Content rating filter moved to FastAPI get_child_session dependency (ISS-002 resolved). COPPA verifiable parental consent method confirmed as email verification under FTC guidance for low-risk data collection contexts.

metadata.notes: Round 2 fixes ISS-001 and ISS-002 (client-side bypass vulnerabilities). Weak quality: observability and cost sections at minimum depth. COPPA-compliant children's mobile app with server-side watch time enforcement and backend content rating filtering.

project_goal.value: Mobile app for fitness streaming subscribers to download workout videos for offline playback, with cost-optimized download scheduling that defers large downloads to off-peak hours and WiFi-only connections
project_goal.rationale: Subscribers in areas with limited mobile data benefit from offline access; off-peak scheduling reduces CDN egress costs

target_users.value: Fitness streaming subscribers on iOS and Android who travel or work out in areas with unreliable connectivity
target_users.rationale: Offline access is a core retention feature for mobile fitness subscribers

project_class.rationale: Native iOS and Android apps with offline-first download management

capabilities.rationale: Native mobile UI, Fastify backend for license management, Cognito auth, PostgreSQL for subscriber and download records, analytics for CDN optimization

complexity_level.rationale: Download scheduling, DRM offline license management, and expiry enforcement are non-trivial but within moderate complexity range

risk_level.rationale: Content partner license terms govern offline window; expiry enforcement failure could violate DRM agreements

data_sensitivity.rationale: Subscriber profiles, viewing history, and device identifiers are personal data under GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: Mobile apps reach subscribers globally via App Store and Google Play

access_model.value: Cognito user pools with JWT; device registration required before offline license issuance; max 3 registered devices per subscriber account
access_model.rationale: Per-device DRM offline licenses require device identity tracking

feature_scope.value: Video download queue management with WiFi and off-peak scheduling, per-device DRM offline license issuance and expiry enforcement, device storage budget management, download analytics for CDN optimization, offline playback with license validation, expired download cleanup
feature_scope.rationale: Complete offline lifecycle from queue to expired content removal

mvp_scope.value: Workout video download to device with WiFi-only enforcement, offline license issuance via backend, 30-day offline license window, expired download cleanup, max 5GB device storage budget
mvp_scope.rationale: Core offline download covers the majority of use cases without off-peak scheduling complexity in V1

security_baseline.value: Cognito JWT on all API calls, per-device offline licenses encrypted on-device with platform keystore, HTTPS for all downloads, device registration capped at 3 per account, certificate pinning on mobile API client
security_baseline.rationale: DRM content partner requirements mandate encrypted offline storage and device-bound licenses

frontend_stack.value: Swift 5.10 with SwiftUI (iOS) + Kotlin with Jetpack Compose (Android)
frontend_stack.rationale: Native UI frameworks for best download background task integration with iOS URLSession and Android WorkManager

backend_stack.rationale: Fastify for high-throughput license issuance and download token generation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for subscriber profiles, device registrations, download licenses, and analytics events

hosting_target.value: ECS Fargate
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers on AWS with auto-scaling for download license request spikes

privacy_retention_policy.value: Download analytics retained 90 days for CDN optimization; subscriber profile data retained per GDPR subject to deletion requests; offline license records retained for duration of license plus 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimization; DRM audit requires brief post-expiry retention

future_scope.value: Off-peak download scheduling using subscriber time zone and CDN peak hour data; collaborative playlist downloads for group fitness classes
future_scope.rationale: Off-peak scheduling deferred to V2 after core download infrastructure is stable

constraints.value: iOS URLSession background downloads must not drain battery; Android WorkManager constraints: require-network WiFi-only option; offline license maximum 48-hour renewal cycle; maximum 3 registered devices per account
constraints.rationale: Platform guidelines for background tasks and DRM partner license terms

observability_baseline.value: Datadog RUM for mobile crash rates and download success rates, custom metrics for offline queue depth and license expiry events, PagerDuty alerts for license issuance failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Download failures are high-impact subscriber experience events requiring rapid detection

execution_preference.value: Bi-weekly mobile releases on TestFlight and Play Store internal testing tracks; backend continuous deployment via GitHub Actions
execution_preference.rationale: Mobile release cadence aligned with App Store review and subscriber expectation

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU subscriber data including download history and device identifiers; content license expiry terms governed by fitness content partner agreements
compliance_context.rationale: GDPR data subject rights apply to download and viewing history; content partners audit offline license compliance

project.goal: Offline workout video download with DRM license management for fitness streaming mobile app
project.target_users: Fitness streaming subscribers on iOS and Android
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks

frontend.approach: Native apps; background download via URLSession (iOS) and WorkManager (Android)

backend.framework: Fastify with TypeScript

security.auth: Cognito user pools with JWT, device registration endpoint
security.encryption: HTTPS for downloads; offline license encrypted in iOS Keychain and Android Keystore
security.drm: Per-device offline license with 30-day expiry window

data.db: PostgreSQL 16

constraints.wifi_only: iOS URLSession and Android WorkManager WiFi constraint

reasoner_reviews.product: The offline download feature directly addresses the top-rated subscriber feature request in the Q1 survey. The 5GB device storage budget and 30-day license window are well-calibrated for fitness content; a typical 45-minute HD workout is approximately 800MB so the budget allows for 6 full workouts. The V2 off-peak scheduling is a meaningful CDN cost optimization that requires accurate time zone detection per device.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rounds 1 and 2 lacked a concrete description of the offline license renewal mechanism. The 48-hour renewal cycle means the app must check license freshness and re-request from the backend when the device is online and the license is within 48 hours of expiry. This background refresh must work while the app is in the background using iOS BGAppRefreshTask and Android WorkManager periodic tasks. The workflows section must describe this renewal flow explicitly.
reasoner_reviews.security: Certificate pinning on the Fastify license API client is correctly specified and must be implemented using iOS TrustKit and Android OkHttp CertificatePinner. The device_registrations table must store device_fingerprint rather than just device_id to detect and block fraudulent device multiplication attempts. A subscriber attempting to register a 4th device must receive a clear error with a device management deeplink.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: iOS URLSession background download transfers have a 30-second timeout for each individual HTTP request within the download session; large 4K workout files must be segmented into HLS chunks to ensure each chunk completes within the timeout. The backend download token endpoint must return HLS manifest URLs, not raw file URLs.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 added the renewal workflow to the workflows section but the data model still lacks a license_renewals log table, making it impossible to audit how many times a license was renewed within its 30-day window for DRM compliance reporting.

frontend.app_structure: iOS: SwiftUI app with DownloadManager (URLSession), OfflineLicenseManager (Keychain), and WorkoutLibraryView. Android: Jetpack Compose app with DownloadWorker (WorkManager), LicenseRepository (Keystore), and WorkoutLibraryScreen.
frontend.pages_and_flows: My Downloads screen: list of downloaded workouts with storage usage bar, expiry date per workout, and remove button. Workout Detail screen: download button with WiFi-only indicator and storage budget remaining. Download Queue screen: queued and in-progress downloads with pause/resume controls. Device Management screen: list of registered devices with remove option for freeing registration slots.
frontend.state_management: iOS: Swift Combine for download progress observables and license expiry timers. Android: StateFlow in ViewModel backed by Room local database for offline state persistence.
frontend.performance_notes: HLS chunked downloads prevent URLSession 30-second timeout on large files. Download progress streamed via URLSessionDownloadDelegate callbacks to avoid polling. Android WorkManager WiFi constraint set at WorkRequest level.

backend.service_design: Four Fastify route groups: /auth (Cognito token verification), /devices (registration and management), /licenses (offline license issuance and renewal), /analytics (download event ingestion). Stateless pods; all state in PostgreSQL.
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0. License issuance returns signed JWT containing content_id, device_id, expires_at, and license_key_reference. Client validates license JWT signature locally on playback without backend roundtrip.
backend.failure_handling: License issuance idempotent keyed on device_id + content_id + request_nonce; duplicate requests within 60 seconds return cached response from Redis. Cognito token refresh handled client-side; expired tokens return 401 with WWW-Authenticate header.
backend.backend_risks: License issuance spike during new workout release could exhaust PostgreSQL connection pool; must configure pgBouncer connection pooler in front of RDS

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with JWT; all Fastify routes protected by Cognito JWT verifier middleware validating iss, aud, and exp claims. Device registration requires valid Cognito token plus device_fingerprint in request body.
security.authorization_model: Subscriber role: access own download_licenses, device_registrations, download_schedules. Admin role: read-only access to aggregate analytics. No subscriber can access another subscriber's licenses or devices.
security.secrets_management: Database credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Fastify reads secrets at startup via AWS SDK. Certificate pinning public keys stored in mobile app bundle and rotatable via feature flag.
security.security_risks: Device fingerprint spoofing could allow a subscriber to circumvent 3-device limit. Mitigation: fingerprint combines platform, OS version, hardware model hash, and a server-issued device_nonce bound to the account on first registration.

data.entities: subscriber_profiles, workout_videos, download_licenses, offline_queues, device_storage_budgets, network_preference_settings, download_schedules, expiry_policies
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for all entities. Redis 7 for license token caching (60-second TTL for idempotency) and download_schedule queue state. S3 pre-signed HLS manifest URLs generated by backend at license issuance time.
data.retention_and_deletion: download_licenses retained 30 days post-expiry for DRM audit. subscriber download_schedules purged 90 days after completion. GDPR deletion request removes subscriber_profile, device_registrations, and download_schedules; download_licenses anonymized (subscriber_id nulled) rather than deleted to preserve DRM audit trail.
data.data_risks: License expiry enforcement relies on client-side timer; a subscriber with device clock set backwards could extend offline access beyond 30 days. Mitigation: backend validates license not-before and expiry at every renewal request; license JWT uses server-issued iat claim.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate us-east-1 with Fastify API tasks (2 minimum, HPA on license request RPS) and pgBouncer sidecar. PostgreSQL on RDS Multi-AZ. Redis on ElastiCache. CloudFront for HLS manifest and segment delivery with signed URLs.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: TypeScript type-check, Jest unit tests with 80% coverage gate, container build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Mobile: Fastlane for iOS TestFlight and Android internal track deployments on main branch merge.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog RUM on iOS and Android SDKs: download success rate, download failure by error type, license issuance latency p95. Backend: Datadog APM on Fastify for license endpoint latency. PagerDuty P1 alert for license issuance error rate above 2%.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deploy with minimum healthy percent 100%; rollback via ECS service update to previous task definition. Mobile hotfixes via emergency TestFlight and Play Store expedited review.

previous_plan.title: Fitness Streaming Offline Download App - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Native iOS and Android fitness streaming app enabling offline workout video downloads with DRM license management, WiFi-only scheduling, 30-day license windows, and background license renewal.
previous_plan.workflows: License Issuance: subscriber taps download; app calls POST /licenses with device_id and content_id; backend validates Cognito JWT, checks device registration, generates offline license JWT, returns HLS manifest URLs signed for download. License Renewal: iOS BGAppRefreshTask fires every 24 hours; app checks licenses expiring within 48 hours; calls POST /licenses/renew for each; backend validates and issues new expiry. Android WorkManager periodic task runs every 24 hours with WiFi constraint for renewal.
previous_plan.data_model: subscriber_profiles, workout_videos, download_licenses, device_registrations, download_schedules, device_storage_budgets. license_renewals table absent.
